ref: “A virgin will have a baby boy, and he will be called Immanuel,” which means “God is with us.”
text: Nɩan, talua muan'n 'ba nwʋnnzɛ, ɔ 'ba wʋ baa belenzua kʋn. Baa sɔ'n, bɛ 'ba fɛlɛ yɩ́ Emannuɛ.

ref: From David to the time of the exile in Babylonia, the ancestors of Jesus were: David, Solomon , Rehoboam, Abijah, Asa, Jehoshaphat, Jehoram; Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, Hezekiah, Manasseh, Amon, Josiah, and Jehoiachin and his brothers.
text: Zoziasɩ a wʋ Zekonia nʋn yɩ́ nianman'n-mɔ. Mɛlɛ sɔ nɩn anun yɛ̂ bɛ hyɩlɩ Izalayɛ amma'n-mɔ hɔlɩ Babilɔnʋn ɔ.

ref: From the exile to the birth of Jesus, his ancestors were: Jehoiachin, Shealtiel, Zerubbabel, Abiud, Eliakim, Azor, Zadok, Achim; Eliud, Eleazar, Matthan, Jacob, and Joseph, the husband of Mary, the mother of Jesus, who is called the Messiah.
text: Zʋakɔbʋ a wʋ Zozɛfʋ. Yɩ́ yɩ y'ɔ le Mali. Mali sɔ'n, yɩ́ yɛ̂ ɔ wʋlɩ Zozi mɔ bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ *Kilisi nɩn ɔ.

ref: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
text: Azɔɔ a wʋ Sadɔkɩ, Sadɔkɩ a wʋ Akimʋn, Akimʋn a wʋ Eliwudɩ,

ref: This is how Jesus Christ was born. A young woman named Mary was engaged to Joseph from King David's family. But before they were married, she learned that she was going to have a baby by God's Holy Spirit.
text: Ɛ 'nwun kɛ ɔ kpɩnlɩn yɔlɩ mɔ bɛ wʋlɩ Zozi Kilisi'n. Anɩn Mali m'ɔ tɩ yɩ́ nin'n, ɔ nʋn Zozɛfʋ b'a ja bɛ́ nwʋn. Nan asannan nán Zozɛfʋ a fa Mali atɔnvɔlɛ'n, Wawɛ Nwannzan-nwannzan'n tunmin'n kɔ man Mali kɔ nwʋnnzɛ.

ref: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
text: Zozɛfʋ m'ɔ tɩ yɩ́ hun'n tɩ sʋanlan sɛsɛ. Yɛ̂ kɛmɔ ɔ ngulo man kɛ ɔ bɔ Mali anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ ɔ kele menian'n-mɔ, ɔ fualɩ kɛ ɔ nʋn yɩ́ bɛ 'tɩtɩ nvɩalɩɛ nun.

ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: Kɛmɔ anɩn ajʋnlɩn sɔ'n wɔ yɩ́ ti anun'n, yɛ́ Mɩn bɔfʋɛ'n kʋn kɔ a kɔ hɩn yɩ́ ɛlalɩɛ. Yɩ́ nwan: “Belemgbin *Davidi anʋnman Zozɛfʋ, n'ɛ hyʋhyʋɩn wɔ́ bʋ asɩ, fa Mali sie wɔ́ nwʋn kɛ wɔ́ yɩ. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ baa'n m'ɔ lɛ nwʋnnzɛ yɩ́'n fi Wawɛ Nwannzan-nwannzan'n.

ref: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
text: Ɔ 'ba wʋ baa belenzua kʋn. Baa sɔ'n, ɛ 'ba tʋn yɩ́ Zozi, yɩ́ dein yɛ̂ ɔ 'ba le yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ fi bɛ́ ɛtɛ nɩn anun ɔ.”

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Ɛhɩ kʋalaatin walɩ maan ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ yɛ́ Mɩn manlɩn yɩ́ kpɔmanfʋɛ'n hanlɩn'n kpɩnlɩn sʋ. Yɩ́ nwan:

ref: After Joseph woke up, he and Mary were soon married, just as the Lord's angel had told him to do.
text: Mɔ Zozɛfʋ fi nafɛlɛ nɩn anun tinngelɩ'n, ɔ yɔlɩ like'n mɔ yɛ́ Mɩn bɔfʋɛ'n helelɩ yɩ́'n. Ɔ sɔlɩ Mali nun ɔ falɩ yɩ́ kacili yɩ́ yɩ.

ref: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
text: Nan kʋsʋ ɔ nʋn yɩ́ b'a nna man dede Mali wʋlɩ yɩ́ wa belenzua'n nán ɔ'a tʋn yɩ́ Zozi.

ref: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
text: Bɛ wʋlɩ Zozi Zude mân nɩn anun kulo'n mɔ bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ Betelehɛmʋn nɩn asʋ. Mɛlɛ sɔ'n, anɩn belemgbin *Elɔdɩ y'ɔ sie Zude mân nɩn ɔ. Mɔ bɛ wʋlɩ Zozi'n, ngɛlɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ nɩan nzalama'n, bɛ fi lɩka mɔ sɛnzɛ'n fite'n, bɛ walɩ Zoluzalɛmʋn ɛbɛlɛ.

ref: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
text: Mɔ bɛ juli ɛbɛlɛ'n, nán b'a bisa menian'n-mɔ kɛ: “Zufʋ'n-mɔ belemgbin'n mɔ b'a wʋ yɩ́'n, ɔ wɔ nin? Y'a nwun yɩ́ nzalama'n sɛnzɛ afitelɩɛ ɛlɔ, yɛ walɩ kɛ yɛ 'ba a sʋ yɩ́.”

ref: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
text: Mɔ belemgbin Elɔdɩ ɔ nʋn Zoluzalɛmʋn kulo nɩn asʋ menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa tɩlɩ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n, bɛ́ nwʋn wʋsʋlɩ bɛ́.

ref: Herod brought together the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses and asked them, “Where will the Messiah be born?”
text: Belemgbin Elɔdɩ kɔ yia Nyanmɩan *tɛɛyɩfʋɛ mgbain'n-mɔ kʋalaatin, ɔ nʋn *mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ nán ɔ'a bisa bɛ́ lɩka m'ɔ sɛ kɛ bɛ wʋ *Kilisi'n.

ref: They told him, “He will be born in Bethlehem, just as the prophet wrote,
text: Bɛ́ nwan: “Ɔ sɛ kɛ bɛ wʋ yɩ́ Betelehɛmʋn, Zude mân nɩn anun. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ɛjɔlɛ mɔ Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ'n hɛlɛlɩ'n y'ɔ le kɛ:

ref: ‘Bethlehem in the land of Judea, you are very important among the towns of Judea. From your town will come a leader, who will be like a shepherd for my people Israel.’ ”
text: Ɛmɔ Betelehɛmʋn amma mɔ ɛmɔ wɔ Zude mân nɩn anun'n, ɛmɔ kulo'n tɩ man kulo kaan wɔ Zude kulo mgbili'n-mɔ afian. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ bɛ 'ba wʋ sʋanlan kʋn bɛ tʋ ɛmɔ anun. Sʋanlan sɔ'n yɛ̂ ɔ 'ba nɩan Izalayɛ amma'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ tɩ mɩ́n dɩɛ nɩn asʋ kɛ bʋa ɛnɩanfʋɛ nɩan yɩ́ mmʋa'n-mɔ asʋ'n.”

ref: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
text: Mɔ belemgbin Elɔdɩ tɩlɩ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n, ɔ kɔ fɛlɛ ngɛlɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ nvɩalɩɛ nun ɔ kɔ bisa bɛ́ mɛlɛ kpa bɔkɔɔ mɔ nzalama'n fitelɩ'n.

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, ɔ kɔ sʋan bɛ́ Betelehɛmʋn ɛlɔ, yɩ́ nwan: “Ɛmɔ hɔ́ Betelehɛmʋn ɛlɔ, nán bɛ hɔ́ kpʋ́nndɛ batʋnman sɔ'n kpa. Sɛ ɛmɔ nwun yɩ́ a, bɛ mán mɩ́n nzʋ tɩ́, maan mɩ́n kʋsʋ n gɔ́ n zʋ́ yɩ́ bie.”

ref: The wise men listened to what the king said and then left. And the star they had seen in the east went on ahead of them until it stopped over the place where the child was.
text: Mɔ belemgbin Elɔdɩ hanlɩn sɔ helelɩ bɛ́ yuelɩ'n yɛ̂ bɛ hɔlɩ ɔ. Mɔ bɛ 'kɔ'n, bɛ 'nɩan an, anɩn nzalama'n mɔ bɛ nwunlin yɩ́ sɛnzɛ afitelɩɛ ɛlɔ'n li bɛ́ nyunnun. Ɔ li bɛ́ nyunnun dede m'ɔ juli sua'n mɔ batʋnman kaan'n wɔ nun nɩn ati sʋ'n, ɔ kɔ jinlan.

ref: They were thrilled and excited to see the star.
text: Nzalama sɔ'n mɔ bɛ nwunlin yɩ́'n man bɛ kɔ li fɛ kpili kpa.

ref: Later they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, and they went back home by another road. The Escape to Egypt
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Nyanmɩan kɔ hɩn bɛ́ ɛlalɩɛ kɛ nán bɛ sa bɛ́ nzin bɛ sɩn belemgbin Elɔdɩ anwʋn ɛlɔ kʋ́n. Ɔ man bɛ sɩnlɩn lɩka fʋfɔlɛ bɛ hɔlɩ bɛ́ kulo. Bɛ nʋn Zozi 'ba hɔ Ezipiti mân nun

ref: After the wise men had gone, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Hurry and take the child and his mother to Egypt! Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is looking for the child and wants to kill him.”
text: Mɔ ngɛlɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ hɔlɩ'n, yɛ́ Mɩn bɔfʋɛ'n kʋn kɔ a kɔ hɩn Zozɛfʋ ɛlalɩɛ, yɩ́ nwan: “Zozɛfʋ, jasʋ fa batʋnman'n nʋn yɩ́ nin nwanndi kɔ tanlan Ezipiti mân nɩn anun ɛlɔ. Sɛ n ganlɩn n gelelɩ man wɔ́ kɛ ɛ sá bála a, n'ɛ ba. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ, belemgbin Elɔdɩ 'kpʋnndɛ kɛ ɔ kun batʋnman'n.”

ref: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
text: Kɔngɔɛ sɔ nɩn ala, Zozɛfʋ kɔ fa batʋnman'n nʋn yɩ́ nin, bɛ kɔ nwanndi bɛ kɔ hɔ Ezipiti.

ref: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
text: Bɛ wɔ ɛlɔ dede belemgbin Elɔdɩ wuli nán b'a sa b'a a. Yɩ́ sɔ'n, ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ Nyanmɩan manlɩn kpɔmanfʋɛ'n hanlɩn yɩ́ daba nɩn a kpɩn sʋ. Yɩ́ nwan: M'an han m'an hele mɩ́n wa'n kɛ ɔ fi Ezipiti ɔ jásʋ. Mmatʋnman nganngan'n-mɔ ehuan'n

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Mɔ belemgbin Elɔdɩ nwunlin kɛ ngɛlɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ a laka yɩ́'n, ɔ fɛlɩ ɛya kpa. Ɔ tɩtɩlɩ cɩan'n mɔ ngɛlɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ nwunlin nzalama'n dede a, anɩn ɔ'a nwun kɛ batʋnman'n ka kaan di afʋɛ nnyuan. Yɩ́ ti, ɔ kɔ sʋan yɩ́ nzalafʋ'n-mɔ Betelehɛmʋn nʋn kulo'n-mɔ kʋalaa mɔ bɛ mantan mantan yɩ́'n-mɔ asʋ kɛ bɛ hɔ́ hún mmatʋnman mmelenzua mɔ b'a li afʋɛ nnyuan'n, ɔ nʋn bɛ́ mɔ bɛ lili man afʋɛ nnyuan'n kʋalaatin.

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
text: Kɛ ɔ yɔlɩ mɔ Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ Zolomin ɛjɔlɛ'n m'ɔ hanlɩn'n kpɩnlɩn sʋ nɩn anɩn. Yɩ́ nwan:

ref: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
text: Bɛ tɩlɩ ɛtɩan nʋn esuan nʋn awɔ́lɛ mgbili Lama kulo nɩn asʋ. Lasɛɛ yɛ̂ anɩn ɔ lɛ sun yɩ́ mma'n-mɔ ɔ. Ɔ ngulo man kɛ bɛ kpata yɩ́, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ yɩ́ mma'n-mɔ a wu. Zozɛfʋ-mɔ fi Ezipiti bɛ 'ba sa bɛ ba

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Mɔ belemgbin Elɔdɩ wuli'n, yɛ́ Mɩn bɔfʋɛ'n kʋn walɩ hɩnlɩn Zozɛfʋ ɛlalɩɛ Ezipiti mân nun.

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: Yɩ́ nwan: “Zozɛfʋ, jasʋ fa batʋnman'n nʋn yɩ́ nin sa kɔ Izalayɛ mân nɩn anun. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ, bɛ́ mɔ anɩn bɛ lɛ kpʋnndɛ batʋnman'n bɛ kun yɩ́'n, b'a yue wu.”

ref: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
text: Mɔ Zozɛfʋ juli ɛbɛlɛ m'ɔ tɩlɩ kɛ Aakelawusi a li yɩ́ sɩ Elɔdɩ aja ɔ'a kaci Zude belemgbin'n, ɔ sulolɩ ɛbɛlɛ lɩka sɔ nɩn anun ɛtanlan. Ɛlalɩɛ nun, mɔ Nyanmɩan helelɩ yɩ́ atɩn m'ɔ di kɛ ɔ sɩn sʋ'n, Zozɛfʋ bɔlɩ yɩ́ ti Galile mân nɩn anun.

ref: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
text: M'ɔ hɔlɩ juli ɛlɔ'n, ɔ tanlanlɩn kulo'n mɔ bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ Nazalɛtɩ nɩn asʋ. Ɔ man ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ Nyanmɩan mgbɔmanfʋɛ'n-mɔ hanlɩn nɩn a kpɩn sʋ. Bɛ́ nwan: Bɛ 'ba fɛlɛ yɩ́ Nazalɛtɩ sʋanlan.

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
text: Yɩ́ ti, ɛmɔ yɔ́ nyɔlɩɛ m'ɔ kele kɛ ɛmɔ a kaci bɛ́ abalabɔ ɛtɛ'n b'a fa bɛ́ nwʋn b'a man Nyanmɩan.

ref: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
text: Ɔ kan kɛ: “Ɛmɔ nún bɛ́ nwʋn bɛ yáci bɛ́ abalabɔ ɛtɛ'n. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ Anwunno Belemgbin elie mɛlɛ nɩn a kpunnge.”

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Zʋan taladɩɛ'n m'ɔ wula'n, bɛ le sʋamo enyuan yɛ̂ b'a nwʋn ɔ, yɛ̂ naan kpolo bɛlɛtɩ hyɩ yɩ́ afian. Tɛtɛbʋabʋa nʋn wɛ yɛ̂ ɔ di ɔ.

ref: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
text: Zoluzalɛmʋn menian nʋn Zude mân nɩn anun menian, ɔ nʋn bɛ́ mɔ bɛ́ kulo'n-mɔ mantan asue Zuludɩn'n, bɛ ba Zʋan anwʋn ɛbɛlɛ.

ref: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
text: Bɛ kan bɛ́ ɛtɛ'n kʋalaa menian'n-mɔ anyunnun bɛ man Zʋan sɔnɩn bɛ́ asue Zuludɩn nɩn anun.

ref: Many Pharisees and Sadducees also came to be baptized. But John said to them: You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
text: Mɔ Zʋan nwunlin kɛ *Falisifʋɛ nʋn *Sadusifʋɛ'n-mɔ dɔʋn lɛ ba maan ɔ sɔnɩn bɛ́'n, yɩ́ nwan: “Ati anun ɛtɛ mma kɛ owo! Nwan yɛ̂ ɔ selɩ ɛmɔ kɛ ɛsɔnɩan nɩn ala kʋala kɛ ɔ de bɛ́ fi Nyanmɩan sikpɛ nɩn anwʋn ɔ?

ref: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
text: B'a si akunman nɩn a yue. Baka biala m'ɔ nzu man mma kpa'n, bɛ 'kpɛ bɛ tʋ sɩ̂n nɩn anun.

ref: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
text: Ɔ le yɩ́ huhuamʋa'n yɩ́ sa nun, ɔ 'ba huhu *bele mma kpa'n ɔ fa sie yɩ́ esie nɩn anun, nan ɔ ju ewula'n gua sɩ̂n'n m'ɔ nnun man lé nɩn anun.” Zʋan 'ba sɔnɩn Zozi (Maakɩ

ref: Jesus left Galilee and went to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.
text: Mɛlɛ sɔ nɩn anun, Zozi fi Galile mân nɩn anun ɔ walɩ asue Zuludɩn nɩn anʋan Zʋan anwʋn ɛbɛlɛ kɛ ɔ sɔ́nɩn yɩ́.

ref: But John kept objecting and said, “I ought to be baptized by you. Why have you come to me?”
text: Zʋan a nne a ndʋ man nun, yɩ́ nwan: “Mɩ́n yɛ̂ ɔ fata kɛ m ba wɔ́ nwʋn m man ɛ sɔnɩn mɩ́n ɔ. Nan wɔ́ dɩɛ, wɔ́ lɩlɩ yɛ̂ ɛ lɛ ba maan mɩn sɔnɩn wɔ́ ɔ!”

ref: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
text: Mɔ Zʋan anʋan tɔlɩ'n, Zozi nwan: “Dɔɛ mɔ yɛ wɔ nun'n, tie nán sɔnɩn mɩ́n. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ, ɛbɛlɛ nɩn anun yɛ̂ yɛ 'ba yɔ like m'ɔ tɩ sɛsɛ wɔ Nyanmɩan anyunnun ɔ.” Ɛhɩ a, Zʋan kɔ tie ɔ kɔ sɔnɩn Zozi.

ref: So Jesus was baptized. And as soon as he came out of the water, the sky opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down on him like a dove.
text: Mɔ Zozi fi asue nɩn anun ɔ 'fite'n, anwunno kɔ buke. Zozi 'nɩan an, anɩn Nyanmɩan Wawɛ'n lɛ ju ɔ ba yɩ́ nwʋn kɛ abubule.

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Ɛbɛlɛ ala, anɩɛ kʋn kɔ te anwunno ɛlɔ kɛ: “Sʋanlan ɛhɩ tɩ mɩ́n ehulo Baa, m'an fa mɩ́n lʋa'n kʋalaa m'an wula yɩ́ nun.”

ref: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi 'tie a, b'a tʋ Zʋan fiadɩ. Zozi kɔ hɔ Galile mân nɩn anun ɛlɔ.

ref: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: Ɔ'a ndanlan man Nazalɛtɩ kulo nɩn asʋ. Nan ɔ hɔlɩ tanlanlɩn kulo'n mɔ bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ Kapɛɛnawɔmʋn nɩn asʋ. Kulo sɔ'n wɔ Galile asue nɩn anʋan, Zabulʋn nʋn Nɛfʋtali etin dɩɛ nɩn asʋ.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Abɔnsanmʋn kɔ kpʋ yɩ́. Yɩ́ nwan: “Sɛ Nyanmɩan Awa y'ɔ le wɔ́ sakpa a, anɩn kan kele nyɔbʋɛ ɛhɩ-mɔ maan bɛ káci kpaʋn.”

ref: Jesus answered, “The Scriptures also say, ‘Don't try to test the Lord your God!’ ”
text: Mɔ Abɔnsanmʋn hanlɩn sɔ'n, Zozi nwan: “B'a hɛlɛ wɔ Ɛhɛlɛ Ɛjɔlɛ nɩn anun kɛ: N'ɛ sɔ yɛ́ Mɩn m'ɔ tɩ wɔ́ Nyanmɩan'n nɩan.”

ref: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
text: Ɛsɛ Abɔnsanmʋn kɔ fa Zozi kɔ hɔ bʋka kannganlaun kpa kʋn asʋ. Ɔ kɔ yɩ mân nɩn anun mmelemgbin mân'n-mɔ kʋalaa nʋn bɛ́ anunminnyanmʋn'n ɔ kɔ hele yɩ́.

ref: The devil said to him, “I will give all this to you, if you will bow down and worship me.”
text: Yɩ́ nwan: “Sɛ ɛ tu tɔ mɩ́n ja nun ɛ sʋ mɩ́n an, mɩn 'fa ɛhɩ kʋalaa mɩn man wɔ́.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Go away Satan! The Scriptures say: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve only him.’ ”
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi nwan: “*Satan, kɔ! Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ b'a hɛlɛ wɔ Ɛhɛlɛ Ɛjɔlɛ nɩn anun kɛ: Yɛ́ Mɩn m'ɔ tɩ wɔ́ Nyanmɩan'n, yɩ́ ngʋnmɩn cein y'ɔ di kɛ ɛ sʋ yɩ́ ɔ.”

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said,
text: Ɔ yɔlɩ sɔ maan ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ *Ezayi hanlɩn yɩ́ daba'n kpɩnlɩn sʋ. Yɩ́ nwan:

ref: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
text: Zabulʋn nʋn Nɛfʋtali amma nʋn bɛ́ mɔ bɛ wɔ Asue Kpili nɩn anʋan ɛlɔ dede ɔ kɔ ju asue Zuludɩn nɩn anzin ɛlɔ ebue kʋn, ɔ nʋn bɛ́ mɔ bɛ wɔ Galile mân nɩn anun mɔ bɛ́ dɔʋn bɛ tɩ man Zufʋ'n,

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: Kɛ Zozi anʋan 'tɔ ala, bɛ kɔ yaci bɛ́ dada'n-mɔ ɛbɛlɛ bɛ kɔ si yɩ́ sʋ.

ref: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
text: Mɔ Zozi 'sɩn Galile asue nɩn anʋan'n, ɔ kɔ nwun anianman nnyuan. Kʋn li Simʋn, bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ biekun Pɩɛlɩ, ɔ nʋn yɩ́ nianman Andele. Anɩn bɛ lɛ gua dada, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ bɛ tɩ asufuɛ.

ref: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
text: Zozi fi ɛbɛlɛ ɔ kɔ hɔ yɩ́ nyunnun kaan, ɔ 'tʋ yɩ́ nyɩn an, ɔ kɔ nwun anianman nnyuan mmiekun-mɔ. Bɛ tɩ Zebede amma. Kʋn li Zʋakɩ yɛ̂ kʋn kʋsʋ li Zʋan. Anɩn bɛ nʋn bɛ́ sɩ Zebede bɛ gua ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun, bɛ lɛ kpʋnmgba bɛ́ dada'n-mɔ anun. Zozi kɔ fɛlɛ bɛ́.

ref: At once they left the boat and their father and went with Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Luke
text: Ɛbɛlɛ ala, bɛ kɔ yaci bɛ́ sɩ Zebede nʋn ɛlɛɛ nɩn ɛbɛlɛ bɛ kɔ si Zozi sʋ. Zozi 'ba nanndɩ Galile mân'n lɩka kʋalaatin (Luku

ref: Jesus went all over Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the good news about God's kingdom. He also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi nanndɩ Galile mân'n lɩka kʋalaatin. Ɔ kekele menian'n-mɔ Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ'n Zufʋ'n-mɔ asɔnɩn sua'n-mɔ anun. Ɔ bɔ Nyanmɩan Belemgbin Mân nɩn anwʋn Ɛjɔlɛkpa'n ɔ kele bɛ́, ɛsɛ menian mɔ ewue ngacile ngacile kʋalaa lɛ kun bɛ́, ɔ nʋn bɛ́ kʋalaa mɔ b'a bubu'n, ɔ man bɛ́ nwʋn sa.

ref: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
text: Ɛhɩka ati, Zozi dunman'n kɔ fa Sili mân bɔndɩn'n. Bɛ fa bɛ́ mɔ ewue ngacile ngacile kʋalaa lɛ kun bɛ́'n bɛ bɛlɛ Zozi: bɛ́ mɔ bɛ́ nwʋn fʋfʋ bɛ́ o, bɛ́ mɔ wawɛ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ wɔ bɛ́ nun o, bɛ́ mɔ bɛ tɔ mgbutie o, ɔ nʋn bɛ́ mɔ b'a bubu o, ɔ man bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ́ nwʋn sa.

ref: Large crowds followed Jesus from Galilee and the region around the ten cities known as Decapolis. They also came from Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.
text: Menian dɔʋn kpa si yɩ́ sʋ. Mmie-mɔ fi Galile, mmie-mɔ fi mân'n mɔ bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ Kulo Bulu nɩn anun, mmie-mɔ fi Zoluzalɛmʋn kulo nɩn asʋ ɔ nʋn Zude mân nun ɔ nʋn mân'n m'ɔ wɔ asue Zuludɩn nɩn anzin ɛlɔ nɩn anun.

ref: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on the side of a mountain and sat down. Blessings (Luke
text: Mɔ Zozi nwunlin meninsʋnman'n, ɔ kɔ fʋ bʋka kʋn asʋ, ɔ kɔ tanlan asɩ. Yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ kpʋ yɩ́,

ref: You must obey God's commands better than the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law obey them. If you don't, I promise you will never get into the kingdom of heaven. Anger
text: Mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ, sɛ ɛmɔ a mmɔ man bala kpa m'ɔ sɔ Nyanmɩan anyɩn b'a ndala man *mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn *Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ a, Nyanmɩan ngɔ man man ɛmɔ atɩn maan bɛ ngɔ wʋlʋ man yɩ́ mân nɩn anun ɛlɔ. Ɛya ɛfɛ nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n

ref: So if you are about to place your gift on the altar and remember that someone is angry with you,
text: “Yɩ́ sɔ ati, sɛ ɛ 'fa like kɔ man Nyanmɩan, sɛ ɛ ju Nyanmɩan awulo nɩn anun lɩka mɔ bɛ yɩ tɛɛ'n nán ɛ kacɩ kɛ a se wɔ́ manngʋn bie sa a,

ref: God blesses those people who depend only on him. They belong to the kingdom of heaven!
text: “Nyila hán bɛ́ mɔ bɛ sɩ yɩ́ fɔ́ʋ́n kɛ Nyanmɩan anyunnun bɛ tɩ wawɛ nun ehianfʋɛ'n. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n tɩ bɛ́ dɩɛ.

ref: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
text: “Nyila hán bɛ́ mɔ bɛ bɛbɛlɛ bɛ́ nwʋn asɩ'n. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ Nyanmɩan 'ba fa asɩɛ'n sie bɛ́ ajâ.

ref: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
text: “Nyila hán bɛ́ mɔ bɛ nwun bɛ́ manngʋn'n-mɔ anwʋn anwunnvoe'n. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ Nyanmɩan kʋsʋ 'ba nwun bɛ́ nwʋn anwunnvoe.

ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: “Nyila hán bɛ́ mɔ bɛ fa bɛ́ ahʋnlɩn'n kʋalaa bɛ kulo Nyanmɩan bɛ yɔ like m'ɔ kulo'n. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ bɛ 'ba nwun Nyanmɩan.

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: “Eyuadɩ nɩn anun, ɛmɔ tɩ kɛ kanlannɩɛ. Kulo mɔ bɛ kɔ si yɩ́ bʋka sʋ'n, kulo sɔ'n, ɔ nvɩa man.

ref: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
text: Mɩ́n dɩɛ, mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ nán bɛ kan ndâ bɔbɔ fɩ́ɩ́. Nán bɛ kan ndâ bɛ fa anwunno nɩn adanzɩɛ, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ɛlɔ yɛ̂ Nyanmɩan belemgbin bia'n wɔ ɔ.

ref: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
text: Nán bɛ kan ndâ bɛ fa asɩɛ nɩn adanzɩɛ, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ asɩɛ'n tɩ Nyanmɩan bia m'ɔ sesie yɩ́ ja sʋ ɔ. Nán bɛ kan ndâ bɛ fa Zoluzalɛmʋn kulo nɩn adanzɩɛ, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ɛbɛlɛ tɩ Nannan Nyanmɩan Kpili kulo.

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: “Asɩɛ nɩn asʋ ɛwa, ɛmɔ tɩ kɛ njɩn m'ɔ man like nzɛcɩ man ɔ. Nan sɛ njɩn'n ta a, bɛ 'yɔ sɛ nán b'a man ɔ'a yɔ kalʋ-kalʋ biekun? Bɛ ngʋala man yɩ́ like fɩ́ɩ́ kʋ́n yɔ. Asɩ ala yɛ̂ bɛ ju bɛ gua bɛ man menian'n-mɔ tetia sʋ ɔ.

ref: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
text: Sɛ bɛ sɔ kanlannɩɛ a, bɛ nva man like bɛ mmutu man sʋ. Nan bɛ sɔ bɛ sie like sʋ anwunno sua nɩn anun maan ɔ ta bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ nwun asɩ.

ref: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
text: “Nán bɛ jʋnlɩn kɛ m'an a *Moyizɩ mala'n nʋn mgbɔmanfʋɛ'n-mɔ ngehele nɩn abʋsʋ enunnuan. Nán yɩ́ enunnuan ati yɛ̂ m malɩ ɔ. Nan m malɩ kɛ mɩn 'ba maan ɔ kpɩn sʋ.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Do not murder” and “A murderer must be brought to trial.”
text: “Ɛmɔ tɩlɩ kɛ bɛ hanlɩn bɛ helelɩ ɛmɔ anan-mɔ kɛ: Ɛ ngɔ li man awue, yɩ́ ti, sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ li awue'n, bɛ sánman yɩ́ bɛ mán kpain'n m'ɔ di ɛjɔlɛ'n.

ref: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
text: Nan mɩ́n dɩɛ, mɩ́n nwan: Sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ fɛ yɩ́ manngʋn anwʋn ɛya'n, ɔ fata nzanman. M'ɔ kɔ se yɩ́ manngʋn kɛ: ‘Laka!’ , sʋanlan sɔ'n, ɔ fata ahɩnlɩnnvie. Yɛ̂ sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ se yɩ́ manngʋn kɛ: ‘Sɛnvʋɛ!’, yɩ́ kʋsʋ ɔ fata kɛ bɛ tʋ yɩ́ sɩ̂n'n m'ɔ le man ayuelɩɛ nɩn anun.

ref: leave your gift there in front of the altar. Make peace with that person, then come back and offer your gift to God.
text: anɩn wɔ́ like'n mɔ ɛ 'fa man Nyanmɩan'n, yaci yɩ́ ɛbɛlɛ, kɔ tʋa wɔ́ manngʋn'n bɛ sésie bɛ́ afian, nán afɩ a sa a fa wɔ́ like nɩn a man Nyanmɩan.

ref: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
text: “Sɛ sʋanlan bie kɔ sanman wɔ́, mɔ ɛmɔ kɔ fa atɩn'n mɔ bɛ lɛ kɔ'n, ɛbɛlɛ ala, tʋa yɩ́, ɛmɔ sésie ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n bɛtɛɛ sʋ, nán ɔ'a nva man wɔ́ ɔ'a mman man kpain'n m'ɔ di ɛjɔlɛ'n, nán yɩ́ kʋsʋ ɔ'a nva man wɔ́ ɔ'a mman man nzalafʋ'n, nán b'a ndʋ man wɔ́ fiadɩ.

ref: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
text: “Ɛsɛ bɛ hanlɩn bɛ helelɩ yɛ́ nan-mɔ kɛ: Sɛ sʋanlan kʋn kulo kɛ ɔ yila yɩ́ yɩ a, anɩn ɔ hɛ́lɛ kalata m'ɔ kele kɛ b'a yila'n ɔ fá mán yɩ́.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Ɛmɔ tɩlɩ kɛ bɛ hanlɩn bɛ helelɩ yɛ́ nan-mɔ kɛ: Like mɔ ɛ kɔ han ndâ wɔ Nyanmɩan anyunnun kɛ ɛ 'yɔ'n, n'ɛ kpɔ ndâ'n, nan yɔ like sɔ'n sakpa.

ref: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
text: Ɛmɔ hán yɩ́ tika kɛ: ‘Yuo’ anaan ‘Cɛcɛ.’ Yɩ́ nzin dɩɛ'n kʋalaa mɔ bɛ kan bɛ gua sʋ'n, ɔ fi Abɔnsanmʋn ɛlɔ. Bɛ́ mɔ bɛ kele yɛ́ yalɛ nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n (Luku

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: Nan mɩ́n dɩɛ, mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ sɛ sʋanlan yɔ ɛmɔ ɛtɛ a, nán bɛ yɔ bie bɛ sie yɩ́ bʋsʋ. Sɛ sʋanlan kpacɩ wɔ́ nyɩn fama nɩn asʋ a, kaci bɛ̂'n maan ɔ kpácɩ sʋ.

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Sɛ mân mgbain'n-mɔ bie fa yɩ́ ninnge tinndin wɔ́ kɛ ɛ sʋ́a ɛ hɔ́ sóman yɩ́ atɩn cilo kʋn an, kɔ dede ju cilo nnyuan.

ref: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
text: Sʋanlan sɛlɛ wɔ́ like a, fa man yɩ́. Sɛ sʋanlan kulo kɛ ɔ fɛ wɔ́ like a, n'ɛ yɔ yɩ́ abɔlɛ. Ehulo m'ɔ di kɛ yɛ kulo bɛ́ mɔ bɛ kpɔ yɛ́ nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n (Luku

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: “Ɛmɔ tɩlɩ kɛ bɛ́ nwan: Kulo wɔ́ manngʋn, nán kpɔ sʋanlan m'ɔ kpɔ wɔ́'n.

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
text: Nan mɩ́n dɩɛ mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ ɛmɔ húlo bɛ́ mɔ bɛ kpɔ ɛmɔ'n. Ɛmɔ sɛ́lɛ Nyanmɩan bɛ mán bɛ́ mɔ bɛ kele ɛmɔ ahʋlʋwa'n,

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: nán ɛmɔ a kaci ɛmɔ Asɩ'n m'ɔ wɔ anwunno nɩn amma. Afɩ Nyanmɩan man eyua'n fi ɔ man meninkpa'n-mɔ nʋn ɛtɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ kʋalaatin. Ɔ man esue'n tɔ ɔ man bɛ́ mɔ bɛ bɔ bala kpa ɔ nʋn bɛ́ mɔ bɛ yɔ ɛtɛ'n.

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Sɛ bɛ́ mɔ bɛ kulo ɛmɔ nɩn ala yɛ̂ ɛmɔ kulo bɛ́ a, anɩn nzu nvasʋɛ dɩɛ yɛ̂ ɛmɔ nyan yɩ́ wɔ Nyanmɩan ɛlɔ ɔ? Ɛtɛfʋɛ kɛ bɛ́ mɔ bɛ dide ajule'n, kɛ bɛ́ kʋsʋ bɛ yɔ yɩ́ nɩn ala anɩn.

ref: But you must always act like your Father in heaven.
text: Yɩ́ sɔ ati, kɛmɔ ɛmɔ Asɩ m'ɔ wɔ anwunno'n tɩ sʋanlan kpa'n, ɛmɔ kʋsʋ káci meninkpa.

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: “Ɛmɔ nɩ́an bɛ́ nwʋn kpa! Nán bɛ yɔ ninnge mɔ Nyanmɩan kulo'n bagua nɩn anun maan menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa nwun ɛmɔ. Sɛ ɛmɔ yɔ yɩ́ sɔ a, ɛmɔ nnyan man ɛmɔ Asɩ m'ɔ wɔ anwunno nɩn asa nun like fɩ́ɩ́.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: Nan wɔ́ dɩɛ, sɛ ɛ 'fa esika ɛ man ehianfʋɛ'n-mɔ a, n'ɛ man wɔ́ manngʋn'n muonun mɔ ɛ nʋn yɩ́ nanndɩ'n nwun nun.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Sɛ ɛmɔ 'yɔ asɔnɩn an, nán bɛ yɔ kɛ apoo mma'n-mɔ yɔ yɩ́'n. Bɛ kulo nzisi ejinlan bɛ yɔ asɔnɩn Zufʋ'n-mɔ asɔnɩn sua'n-mɔ anun nʋn gua'n-mɔ ayialɩɛ nɩn asʋ maan menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa nwun bɛ́. Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ b'a nyan bɛ́ ahatua'n dada angʋnmɩn.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Nan wɔ́ dɩɛ, sɛ ɛ 'yɔ asɔnɩn an, wʋlʋ wɔ́ sua nɩn anun, nyin wɔ́ nzin anʋan, nán sɛlɛ wɔ́ Sɩ m'ɔ wɔ wɔ́ lɩka mɔ ɛ fɩa'n. Yɛ̂ wɔ́ Sɩ m'ɔ nwun like mɔ ɛ lɛ yɔ yɩ́ nvɩalɩɛ nun'n, ɔ 'ba man ɛ nyan yɩ́ nwʋn ahatua.

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: Sɛ ɛmɔ 'yɔ asɔnɩn an, nán bɛ kan ndɩnndɩɩn kɛ bɛ́ mɔ bɛ nzʋ man Nyanmɩan'n yɔ yɩ́'n. Bɛ jʋnlɩn kɛ bɛ 'kan yɩ́ nwala nwala nán Nyanmɩan a tɩ.

ref: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
text: “Nan ɛ 'nwun kɛ ɛmɔ kpɩ́n bɛ yɔ́ asɔnɩn'n: Yɛ́ Sɩ mɔ ɛ wɔ anwunno, man yɛ fá anyɩnnzɔ yɛ bɔ́ wɔ́ dunman nwannzan-nwannzan'n.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Man wɔ́ belemgbin elie mɛlɛ'n jú. Man yɛ yɔ́ wɔ́ kunnun like asɩɛ nɩn asʋ ɛwa kɛ bɛ yɔ yɩ́ wɔ nyanmɩansʋ ɛlɔ'n.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: “Afɩ sɛ ɛmɔ yaci bɛ́ manngʋn'n-mɔ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ ce bɛ́ sakpa a, ɛmɔ Asɩ m'ɔ wɔ nyanmɩansʋ'n, ɔ 'fa ɛmɔ kʋsʋ dɩɛ'n-mɔ ɔ ce ɛmɔ.

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Nan sɛ ɛmɔ nva man bɛ́ manngʋn'n-mɔ ɛtɛ dɩɛ'n-mɔ nje man bɛ́ a, ɛmɔ Asɩ'n kʋsʋ ngɔ fa man ɛmɔ ɛtɛ dɩɛ'n-mɔ ɔ ngɔ hye man ɛmɔ. Abuala nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: nán menian'n-mɔ a nnwun man kɛ ɛ lɛ di abuala. Wɔ́ Sɩ'n m'ɔ wɔ nvɩalɩɛ nun'n, yɩ́ ngʋnmɩn cein ɔ kɔ nwun yɩ́. Yɛ̂ wɔ́ Sɩ'n m'ɔ nwun mɔ ɛ lɛ yɔ yɩ́ nvɩalɩɛ nun'n, ɔ 'ba man ɛ nyan yɩ́ nwʋn ahatua. Anwʋndɩɛ kpa nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n (Luku

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Yɩ́ ti, ɛmɔ lí mʋa bɛ kpʋ́nndɛ Nyanmɩan Belemgbin Mân'n, ɔ nʋn asɩtanlan'n m'ɔ sɔ Nyanmɩan anyɩn'n, nán Nyanmɩan 'ba fa ninnge'n kʋalaa m'ɔ ka'n ce ɛmɔ.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Yɩ́ sɔ ati, nán bɛ jʋnlɩn ɛhɩnman alɩ́ɛ dɩɛ nɩn anwʋn. Alɩ́ɛ'n muonun yɛ̂ ɔ 'di ɛjɔlɛ'n m'ɔ kɔ a nun nɩn ɔ. Nunhan ɛjɔlɛ'n kʋalaa tɩ yɩ́ ɛjɔlɛ.

ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: “Nán bɛ kpʋ́nndɛ mân ɛhɩ anun anwʋndɩɛ'n. Mân ɛhɩ anun ɛwa, ngakʋba di, ngannɔlɛ kan yɩ́, awofʋɛ'n-mɔ bubu anʋan bɛ wua.

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
text: Nan bɛ kpʋnndɛ nyanmɩansʋ anwʋndɩɛ dɩɛ'n. Nyanmɩansʋ, ngakʋba nnʋn man ɛlɔ bɛ nni man, ngannɔlɛ nnʋn man ɛlɔ, awofʋɛ nnʋn man ɛlɔ nán b'a bubu anʋan b'a wua.

ref: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ lɩka mɔ wɔ́ anwʋndɩɛ'n wɔ'n, ɛbɛlɛ kʋsʋ yɛ̂ wɔ́ ahʋnlɩn'n wɔ ɔ. Anyɩan nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n (Luku

ref: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
text: Nan sɛ wɔ́ nyɩn'n sin an, anɩn wɔ́ nwʋnnaan'n lɩka kʋalaatin wɔ awosin nun. Sɛ kanlannɩɛ'n m'ɔ wɔ wɔ́ nun'n tɩ awosin an, nwun yɩ́ kɛ awosin'n mɔ ɛ wɔ nun'n tɩ awosin kpili kpa. Mɩnlɩan nnyuan nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n (Luku

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: “Sʋanlan kʋn ngʋala man kɛ ɔ sʋ mɩnlɩan nnyuan. Sɛ ɔ sʋ mɩnlɩan nnyuan an, ɔ 'kpɔ kʋn nán ɔ kulo kʋn. Yɩ́ nyɩn 'sɔ kʋn yɛ́ ɔ 'tu yɩ́ nyɩn sa kʋn. Ɛmɔ ngʋala man kɛ bɛ ka sʋ bɛ sʋ Nyanmɩan nán b'a sʋ esika. Nzusue nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n (Luku

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: Yɩ́ sɔ ati, nán bɛ di yalɛ bɛ kan kɛ: Nzukɛ yɛ̂ yɛ 'di ɔ? Nzukɛ yɛ̂ yɛ 'nʋn ɔ? Taladɩɛ benin yɛ̂ yɛ 'wula ɔ?

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Ɛmɔ nɩ́an nnʋnman'n-mɔ. Bɛ nnua man like fɩ́ɩ́, bɛ ngɔ man ebo sʋ bɛ nva man alɩɛ fɩ́ɩ́, bɛ nva man alɩɛ bɛ nzie man, nan kʋsʋ ɛmɔ Asɩ m'ɔ wɔ anwunno'n, ɔ gua bɛ́ nʋan alɩɛ! Ɛmɔ anwʋn cian kpa tala nnʋnman'n-mɔ.

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: Nán nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ ɛmɔ di taladɩɛ mɔ ɛmɔ 'wula nɩn anwʋn yalɛ ɔ? Ɛmɔ nɩ́an kɛ ebo nɩn anun ndile'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ le ndetele'n-mɔ kpɩn fifi'n. Bɛ nni man junman, bɛ nnwʋn man taladɩɛ.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Nan kʋsʋ mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ belemgbin Salomʋn muonun nʋn yɩ́ anyanbɛnwʋn'n m'ɔ nyanlɩn yɩ́'n, taladɩɛ m'ɔ tɩ kanlanman kɛ ndetele'n-mɔ'n, ɔ'a nwula man bie lé.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Ndile'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ wɔ ebo nɩn anun ɛnɛ, mɔ ɛhɩnman bɛ 'ju bɛ́ bɛ gua sɩ̂n nun'n, sɛ Nyanmɩan sesie bɛ́ sʋa, ɔ yɔ sɛ mɔ ɔ ngɔ nɩan man ɛmɔ ɔ ngɔ tala man bɛ́ ɔ? Ɛmɔ dedi'n tɩ kaan!

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Bɛ́ mɔ bɛ nzʋ man Nyanmɩan'n, bɛ́ yɛ̂ cɩan daa bɛ susu ninnge ɛhɩ-mɔ anwʋn ɔ. Nan ɛmɔ Asɩ'n m'ɔ wɔ anwunno'n, ɔ sɩ kɛ ɛmɔ a hian ninnge sɔ'n-mɔ anwʋn.

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ, kɛ ɛmɔ kɔ kpɩn kɔ bua menian'n-mɔ ndɛɛ'n yɛ̂ Nyanmɩan kʋsʋ 'nɩan sʋ ɔ bua ɛmɔ ndɛɛ ɔ. Ɛsɛ kɛ ɛmɔ kɔ kpɩn kɔ susu kɔ man bɛ́ manngʋn'n-mɔ'n yɛ̂ Nyanmɩan 'nɩan sʋ ɔ susu man ɛmɔ ɔ.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: “Ɔ yɔ sɛ mɔ tabua munmuin kaan'n m'ɔ la wɔ́ manngʋn nɩn anyɩn nɩn asʋ'n yɛ̂ ɛ nwun yɩ́, nan kʋsʋ ɛ nnwun man tabua ebue'n m'ɔ la wɔ́ nyɩn dɩɛ nɩn asʋ'n?

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Ɛ bɔ wɔ́ nwʋn apoo, di mʋa yɩ tabua ebue'n m'ɔ la wɔ́ nyɩn nɩn asʋ'n, nán a nwun asɩ a yɩ tabua munmuin kaan'n m'ɔ la wɔ́ manngʋn nɩn anyɩn nɩn asʋ'n.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Ɛmɔ mɔ bɛ tɩ ɛtɛfʋɛ'n, ɛmɔ sɩ ninnge mgbakpa fa man bɛ́ mma'n-mɔ. Ɔ yɔ sɛ mɔ ɛmɔ Asɩ'n m'ɔ wɔ anwunno'n, sɛ sʋanlan sɛlɛ yɩ́ like a, ɔ nva man like kpa ɔ mman man yɩ́ ɔ? Mala kpa nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n (Luku

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: Sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ kpʋnndɛ'n, ɔ 'nwun yɩ́. Sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ bɔ anʋan'n, bɛ 'tike. Ɛmɔ anun benin yɛ̂ sɛ yɩ́ wa sɛlɛ yɩ́ kpaʋn an, ɔ fa ɛbʋɛ man yɩ́ ɔ?

ref: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text: “Ɛmɔ sɛ́lɛ nán b'a fa b'a man ɛmɔ. Ɛmɔ kpʋ́nndɛ nán b'a nwun yɩ́. Ɛmɔ bɔ́ anʋan'n nán b'a tike.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ sʋanlan biala m'ɔ kɔ sɛlɛ like'n, bɛ 'fa bɛ man yɩ́.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Anaan sɛ ɔ sɛlɛ yɩ́ ejue kʋn an, ɔ fa owo man yɩ́ ɔ?

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: “Like mɔ *Moyizɩ mala'n nʋn Nyanmɩan mgbɔmanfʋɛ'n-mɔ kalata'n kele'n y'ɔ le kɛ: Like kʋalaa mɔ ɛmɔ kulo kɛ ɛmɔ amanngʋn'n-mɔ yɔ́ mán ɛmɔ'n, ɛmɔ kʋsʋ yɔ́ like sɔ'n bie mán bɛ́. Anʋan nnyuan nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n (Luku

ref: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
text: Ɛsɛ Zozi nwan: “Ɛhɩka ati, sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ tɩ ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ m'an han m'an hele ɛmɔ'n, sɛ ɔ nanndɩ sʋ a, anɩn ɔ tɩ kɛ bɛngɛlɛfʋɛ mɔ ɔ'a si yɩ́ sua nɩn ɛbʋɛ sʋ.

ref: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
text: Nan anʋan fɩtɩɩ'n nʋn akpɔ bɔhyʋɩ'n, bɛ kele ngʋan atɩn. Bɛ́ mɔ bɛ nwun yɩ́ bɛ sɩn sʋ'n, b'a nzʋn man. Ato mgbɔmanfʋɛ'n-mɔ anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n (Luku

ref: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
text: “Ɛmɔ nɩ́an bɛ́ nwʋn kpa wɔ ato mgbɔmanfʋɛ'n-mɔ anwʋn. Bɛ fa bʋa kpolo bɛ wula bɛ ba ɛmɔ anwʋn. Nan bɛ́ kunnun an, bɛ tɩ kɛ bedi aminlefʋɛ kpa.

ref: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
text: Bɛ́ nyɔlɩɛ'n-mɔ anun yɛ̂ ɛmɔ 'ba fa nwun bɛ́ ɔ. Bɛ ndɩ man divɩn mma ndile mmowe sʋ, yɛ̂ bɛ ndɩ man *figi mma eminlan mmowe sʋ.

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
text: Baka kpa biala su mma kpa. Yɛ̂ baka ɛtɛ kʋsʋ biala su mma ɛtɛ.

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Baka kpa kʋn ngʋala man kɛ ɔ su mma ɛtɛ. Yɛ̂ baka ɛtɛ kʋsʋ kʋn ngʋala man kɛ ɔ su mma kpa.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Baka biala m'ɔ nzu man mma kpa'n, bɛ 'kpɛ bɛ tʋ sɩ̂n nɩn anun.

ref: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
text: Ɛhɩka ati, ɔ fi bɛ́ nyɔlɩɛ nɩn anwʋn, ɛmɔ 'ba nwun ato mgbɔmanfʋɛ'n-mɔ angʋnmɩn. Mân'n ndɛɛ ebua cɩan nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n (Luku

ref: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
text: Nan sʋanlan biala m'ɔ kɔ tɩ ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ m'an han mɔ ɔ'a nnanndɩ man sʋ'n, anɩn ɔ tɩ kɛ sʋanlan m'ɔ nzɩ man ngɛlɛ, mɔ ɔ'a si yɩ́ sua nɩn anyʋan nɩn asʋ.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
text: Esue nɩn a tɔ dede ɔ'a bu asɩ, anwʋnman nɩn a tu sɛlɛ kpa a si sua sɔ'n. Ɔ'a bubu ɔ'a gua bɔkɔɔ.” Zozi tunmin'n (Maakɩ

ref: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
text: “Nán bɛ́ kʋalaa mɔ bɛ fɛlɛ mɩ́n: ‘Yɛ́ Mɩn, yɛ́ Mɩn’ yɛ̂ bɛ 'kɔ Anwunno Belemgbin Mân nɩn anun ɔ. Nan bɛ́ mɔ bɛ yɔ mɩ́n Sɩ m'ɔ wɔ anwunno nɩn akunnun like'n, bɛ́ ala yɛ̂ bɛ 'kɔ ɛlɔ ɔ.

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Ɛbɛlɛ nɩn anun yɛ̂ mɩn 'ba han mɩn kele bɛ́ kɛ: Cɩan bie lé mɩn nzɩ man ɛmɔ. Ɛmɔ hɔ́ mʋa, ɛmɔ mɔ ɛtɛ yɛ̂ bɛ yɔ'n.” Sua nnyuan nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n (Luku

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Esue nɩn a tɔ dede ɔ'a bu asɩ, anwʋnman nɩn a tu sɛlɛ kpa a si sua'n, nan kʋsʋ kɛmɔ ɔ jin ɛbʋɛ nɩn asʋ'n, ɔ man ɔ'a mmu man.

ref: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ yɩ́ ngehele'n nʋn *mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ dɩɛ'n tɩ man kʋn. Nan ɔ kekele bɛ́ tunmin sʋ.

ref: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
text: Nan bɛ́ mɔ yɛ̂ ɔ sɛ kɛ ahan bɛ kɔ Anwunno Belemgbin Mân nɩn anun'n, bɛ 'ju bɛ́ bɛ gua gua nɩn asʋ awosin nɩn anun. Ɛbɛlɛ, bɛ 'sun, ɛsɛ bɛ 'kʋan bɛ́ je'n.”

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Mɔ alɩ́ɛ'n tɔlɩ nun'n, bɛ falɩ menian dɔʋn kpa mɔ wawɛ ɛtɛ wɔ bɛ́ nun bɛ bɛlɛlɩ Zozi. Zozi le yɩ́ Nʋan Ɛjɔlɛ'n ɔ kɔ fʋan wawɛ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ maan bɛ fi menian'n-mɔ anun bɛ kɔ tu. Ɛsɛ ɔ kɔ man nwuluwafʋɛ'n-mɔ kʋalaa anwʋn kɔ sa.

ref: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Mɛlɛ sɔ nɩn anun, kokobefʋɛ kʋn kɔ kpunnge Zozi, ɔ kɔ tu kɔ tɔ yɩ́ ja nun, yɩ́ nwan: “Yɛ́ Mɩn, sɛ ɛ kulo a, ɛ kʋala kɛ ɛ man mɩ́n nwʋn sa.”

ref: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
text: Ɔ hanlɩn sɔ a, Zozi kɔ tɩnngɛ yɩ́ sa'n ɔ kɔ han yɩ́. Yɩ́ nwan: “Yuo o, mɩn kulo, tɩ akpɔlɛ.” Ɛbɛlɛ ala, kokobefʋɛ nɩn anwʋn kɔ sa.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Mɔ nzalafʋ'n hanlɩn sɔ'n, Zozi nwan: “Mɩn 'ba hɔ mɩn man yɩ́ nwʋn sa.”

ref: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
text: Mɔ Zozi tɩlɩ nzalafʋ kpain sɔ nɩn anʋan nun ɛjɔlɛ'n, ɔ sinlin yɩ́ nwʋn. Ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele bɛ́ mɔ bɛ si yɩ́ sʋ'n kɛ: “Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ Izalayɛ mân nɩn anun ɛwa, sʋanlan m'ɔ le dedi kpili kpa kɛ bian ɛhɩ'n, n nwunlin man yɩ́ nwʋn sʋ.

ref: Many people will come from everywhere to enjoy the feast in the kingdom of heaven with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
text: Mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ menian dɔʋn kpa fi sɛnzɛ afitelɩɛ, ɔ nʋn sɛnzɛ atɔlɩɛ, bɛ 'ba a Anwunno Belemgbin Mân nɩn anun, bɛ nʋn Abalahamʋn, Izakɩ, nʋn Zʋakɔbʋ bɛ 'ba tʋ asaa.

ref: Then Jesus said to the officer, “You may go home now. Your faith has made it happen.” Right then his servant was healed. Jesus Heals Many People (Mark
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi kɔ han kɔ hele nzalafʋ kpain'n kɛ: “Kɔ wɔ́ awulo, wɔ́ dedi'n mán wɔ́ sa hán mɔ ɛ lɛ kpʋnndɛ'n.” Mɛlɛ sɔ nɩn ala, nzalafʋ kpain nɩn akʋa nɩn anwʋn kɔ sa. Zozi 'ba man nwuluwafʋɛ dɔʋn anwʋn sa (Maakɩ

ref: Suddenly they shouted, “Jesus, Son of God, what do you want with us? Have you come to punish us before our time?”
text: Mɔ bɛ walɩ juli Zozi anyunnun ɛbɛlɛ'n, bɛ kɔ tɛtɩan nun. Bɛ́ nwan: “Nyanmɩan Awa, nzu ɔ? Ɛ se yɛ́ sɛ? Asʋ a a ɛwa kɛ ɛ 'ba tua yɛ́ ɛtɛ nɩn anwʋn kalɛ kʋalaaka nán ndɛɛ ebua cɩan nɩn a ju ɔ?”

ref: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.
text: Mɔ kulo nɩn asʋ menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa tɩlɩ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n, bɛ kɔ kpʋ Zozi. Mɔ bɛ nwunlin yɩ́'n, bɛ kɔ sɛlɛ yɩ́ kɛ ɔ fi bɛ́ mân nɩn anun ɔ jásʋ.

ref: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Zozi kɔ hɔ Pɩɛlɩ awulo ɛlɔ. Ɔ 'kɔ a ju a, anɩn ebunnun a hyɩ Pɩɛlɩ asebala'n, ɔ la ɛbɛlɛ.

ref: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
text: Zozi kɔ han Pɩɛlɩ asebala nɩn asa'n, ebunnun'n fi yɩ́ nwʋn kɔ tu. Ɔ kɔ jasʋ, ɔ kɔ kpʋnndɛ like kɔ man Zozi kɔ li.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “He healed our diseases and made us well.” Some Who Wanted To Go with Jesus (Luke
text: Ɔ yɔlɩ sɔ maan ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ *Ezayi hanlɩn'n kpɩnlɩn sʋ. Yɩ́ nwan: Ɔ'a fa yɛ́ nwʋn ewue'n ɔ'a man yɛ́ nwʋn a sa. Bɛ́ mɔ bɛ kulo kɛ bɛ si Zozi sʋ'n (Luku

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Mala nɩn asʋ kpain'n kʋn kpunngelɩ yɩ́ nwʋn ɛbɛlɛ a, yɩ́ nwan: “Kpain, lɩka biala mɔ ɛ 'kɔ'n, mɩn 'kɔ bie.”

ref: Another disciple said to Jesus, “Lord, let me wait till I bury my father.”
text: Yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ anun kʋn kʋsʋ kɔ se yɩ́ kɛ: “Yɛ́ Mɩn, man mɩ́n atɩn maan n gɔ́ n zíe mɩ́n sɩ kʋalaaka nán m'an a.”

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: Yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ hɔ kɔ tinnge yɩ́. Bɛ́ nwan: “Yɛ́ mɩn, de yɛ́ nán yɛ lɛ wu!”

ref: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Zozi nʋn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ fʋlɩ ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun.

ref: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
text: Ɛjɔlɛ'n sinlin bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ́ nwʋn. Bɛ́ nwan: “Sʋanlan benin y'ɔ le ɛhɩ? Ɔ jʋjɔ kele anwʋnman nʋn asue bɔbɔ a, bɛ man yɩ́ anyɩnnzɔ!” Zozi 'ba fʋan menian nnyuan anun wawɛ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ (Maakɩ

ref: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
text: Mɔ Zozi kpɛlɩ asue'n ɔ hɔlɩ juli yɩ́ nzin ɛlɔ'n, Gadala mân nɩn anun, mmelenzua nnyuan fi asielɩɛ nɩn asʋ bɛ kɔ fite fite. Bɛ kɔ a Zozi atɩn ɛkpa. Menian sɔ'n-mɔ, wawɛ ɛtɛ wɔ bɛ́ nun. Bɛ́ nwʋn esulo kpa, ɔ man sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ ngʋala man atɩn'n mɔ bɛ wɔ sʋ nɩn asʋ sɩn.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Ɔ 'ba yɔ sɔ'n, anɩn mgbolike fatule kpili kʋn wɔ mʋa kaan bɛ lɛ didi.

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Wawɛ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ kɔ sɛlɛ Zozi, bɛ kɔ han bɛ kɔ hele yɩ́ kɛ: “Sɛ ɛ 'fʋan yɛ́ a, man yɛ́ atɩn maan yɛ hɔ́ wʋ́lʋ mgbolike ɛhɩ-mɔ anun.”

ref: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
text: Zozi nwan: “Ɛmɔ hɔ́!” Wawɛ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ kɔ fite bɛ kɔ hɔ kɔ wʋlʋ mgbolike'n-mɔ anun. Ɛbɛlɛ ala, mgbolike fatule'n kʋalaa fi bʋka nɩn asʋ bɛ le ɛnwanndie bɛ kɔ hɔ kɔ gua asue nɩn anun bɛ kɔ nʋan.

ref: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
text: Menian'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ nɩan mgbolike'n-mɔ asʋ'n kɔ nwanndi bɛ kɔ hɔ kulo, bɛ kɔ han like'n mɔ ɔ'a yɔ mgbolike'n-mɔ, ɔ nʋn menian'n-mɔ mɔ wawɛ ɛtɛ wɔ bɛ́ nun nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ bɛ kɔ hele menian'n-mɔ.

ref: Jesus got into a boat and crossed back over to the town where he lived.
text: M'ɔ yɔlɩ sɔ'n, Zozi kɔ fʋ ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun, ɔ kɔ kpɛ asue'n ɔ kɔ sa kɔ hɔ yɩ́ kulo nɩn asʋ.

ref: Jesus heard them and answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
text: Mɔ Zozi tɩlɩ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n, yɩ́ nwan: “Bɛ́ mɔ bɛ ndɩ man akpɔlɛ'n yɛ̂ bɛ kpʋnndɛ ayile ɛyɔlɛfʋɛ ɔ, nan nán bɛ́ mɔ bɛ tɩ akpɔlɛ nɩn ɔ.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. Both the skins and the wine will then be safe. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Mark
text: Bɛ nva man nzan fʋfɔlɛ bɛ ngua man kpolo kotoku daba nun. Sɛ bɛ yɔ yɩ́ sɔ a, kpolo kotoku'n 'sua ɔ sɛcɩ yɛ̂ nzan'n kʋsʋ 'butu. Nan nzan fʋfɔlɛ'n, bɛ gua yɩ́ kpolo kotoku fʋfɔlɛ nun. Yɩ́ dɩɛ anɩn kpolo kotoku o, nzan o, bɛ kʋala yɩ́ kʋalaa fa sie.” Zozi 'ba man balasua kʋn anwʋn sa ɛsɛ ɔ 'ba hɩn talua kaan kʋn (Maakɩ

ref: News about this spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men
text: Mɔ talua'n jasʋlɩ'n, ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n hɔlɩ dede ɔ juli mân sɔ'n lɩka kʋalaatin. Zozi 'ba man anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ nnyuan anwʋn sa

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses said to themselves, “Jesus must think he is God!”
text: Anɩn *mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n mmie-mɔ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ. Bɛ kɔ han yɩ́ bɛ́ ti anun kɛ: “Sʋanlan ɛhɩ a jʋjɔ a tia Nyanmɩan.”

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: Zozi nwunlin bɛ́ ajʋnlɩn'n. Yɩ́ nwan: “Nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ ɛmɔ le ajʋnlɩn nvuin sʋa-mɔ ɛmɔ ati anun ɔ?

ref: The man got up and went home.
text: Zozi hanlɩn sɔ ala, sʋanlan'n kɔ jasʋ ɔ kɔ hɔ yɩ́ awulo.

ref: Go and learn what the Scriptures mean when they say, ‘Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others.’ I didn't come to invite good people to be my followers. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Mark
text: Ɛhɛlɛ Ɛjɔlɛ'n lɩka mɔ b'a hɛlɛ yɩ́ kɛ Nyanmɩan nwan: Mɩn kulo kɛ menian'n-mɔ nwun anwunnvoe bɛ́ afian ɔ tala tɛɛ ɛyɩɛ nɩn anwʋn ninnge'n mɔ bɛ yɔ bɛ man mɩ́n'n. Ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n, ɛmɔ hɔ́ súan nán bɛ tɩ́ yɩ́ bʋ kpa.” Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ'n, ɛsɛ yɩ́ nwan: “Nán sɛsɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ ati yɛ̂ m malɩ ɔ, nan ɛtɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ ati ɔ.” Abuala nɩn anwʋn kosuan'n (Maakɩ

ref: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi fi ɛbɛlɛ hɔlɩ, nan mɛlɛ mɔ ɔ 'kpɛ nun sɩn'n, anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ nnyuan kɔ si yɩ́ sʋ, bɛ le ɛtɛtɩan. Bɛ́ nwan: “Belemgbin *Davidi awa, nwun yɛ́ nwʋn anwunnvoe!”

ref: As Jesus and his disciples were on their way, some people brought to him a man who could not talk because a demon was in him.
text: Anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ'n-mɔ hɔlɩ ala, bɛ kɔ fa sʋanlan kʋn bɛ kɔ bɛlɛ Zozi. Sʋanlan sɔ'n, wawɛ ɛtɛ wɔ yɩ́ nun, ɔ man ɔ ngʋala man jʋjɔ.

ref: As Jesus was leaving, he saw a tax collector named Matthew sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.” Matthew got up and went with him.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Zozi fi ɛbɛlɛ ɔ kɔ hɔ. Mɔ ɔ 'sɩn kɔ'n, ɔ kɔ nwun kɛ bian kʋn tɩ lɩka mɔ bɛ dide ajule'n. Bian sɔ'n, bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ Matie. Zozi nwan: “Matie, si mɩ́n sʋ!” Ɔ hanlɩn sɔ a, Matie kɔ jasʋ kɔ si yɩ́ sʋ.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, an official came and knelt in front of him. The man said, “My daughter has just now died! Please come and place your hand on her. Then she will live again.”
text: Anɩn Zozi anʋan wɔ sʋ ɔ tɛ kan ɛjɔlɛ ɔ tɛ kele bɛ́. Zufʋ'n-mɔ kpain'n kʋn walɩ a, ɔ kɔ kʋtʋ Zozi anyunnun ɛbɛlɛ, yɩ́ nwan: “Mɩ́n wa balasua nɩn a wu kɩkaala. Yɩ́ ti, bala nán fa wɔ́ sa fua yɩ́ nwʋn maan ɔ hɩ́n.”

ref: Jesus turned. He saw the woman and said, “Don't worry! You are now healed because of your faith.” At that moment she was healed.
text: Zozi tʋlɩ yɩ́ nyɩn yɩ́ nzin m'ɔ nwunlin bala'n, yɩ́ nwan: “Mɩ́n wa balasua, gua wɔ́ ahʋnlɩn asɩ, nán wɔ́ dedi nɩn a man wɔ́ nwʋn a sa.” Ɛbɛlɛ ala, bala nɩn anwʋn kɔ sa.

ref: After Jesus had forced the demon out, the man started talking. The crowds were so amazed they began saying, “Nothing like this has ever happened in Israel!”
text: Mɔ Zozi tuli wawɛ ɛtɛ'n, sʋanlan'n kɔ jʋjɔ. Ɔ sinlin menian'n-mɔ anwʋn. Bɛ́ nwan: “Yɛ nwunlin man like sʋa bie lé Izalayɛ mân nɩn anun!”

ref: Some followers of John the Baptist came and asked Jesus, “Why do we and the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Ɛhɩ, Zʋan menian'n-mɔ walɩ tʋlɩ Zozi a, bɛ́ nwan: “Yɛ nʋn *Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ yɛ di abuala titi. Nan nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ wɔ́ menian dɩɛ'n-mɔ nni man abuala'n bie'n?”

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom aren't sad while he is still with them. But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Mɔ bɛ bisalɩ kosuan sɔ'n, Zozi kɔ bua bɛ́ kɛ: “Mɛlɛ mɔ atɔnvɔlɛ bian'n nʋn yɩ́ manngʋn-mɔ gua atɔnvɔlɛ nɩn abʋ'n, ɛmɔ jʋnlɩn kɛ menian sɔ'n-mɔ alʋa 'bɔ bɛ́ nwʋn? Cɛcɛ, bɛ́ lʋa mmɔ man bɛ́ nwʋn. Nan cɩan mɔ bɛ kɔ le atɔnvɔlɛ bian'n fi bɛ́ sa nun'n, ɛbɛlɛ nɩn anun yɛ̂ bɛ 'di abuala ɔ.

ref: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: “Sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ nva man ndafuin fʋfɔlɛ nda man ɛtanlan daba nun. Sɛ ɔ yɔ yɩ́ sɔ a, ndafuin fʋfɔlɛ'n 'sua ɛtanlan daba'n biekun. Sian anɩn yɩ́ bɔɛ nɩn a yɔ kpili a tala daba dɩɛ'n.

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ yɩ́ kunnun ɛlɔ, yɩ́ nwan: “Sɛ mɩn nyan yɩ́ taladɩɛ'n bɔbɔ mɩn kan yɩ́ a, mɩ́n nwʋn 'sa.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples got up and went with the man.
text: Bian'n hanlɩn sɔ a, Zozi nʋn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ jasʋ bɛ kɔ si yɩ́ sʋ.

ref: A woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes.
text: Mɔ bɛ 'kɔ'n, anɩn balasua kʋn wɔ menian'n-mɔ anun, ɔ li Zozi anzin. Moja a tu balasua sɔ'n dede afʋɛ bulu nʋn nnyuan. Ɔ kɔ hyʋɩn kɔ kpunnge Zozi, ɔ kɔ han yɩ́ taladɩɛ nɩn anʋan'n.

ref: When Jesus went into the home of the official and saw the musicians and the crowd of mourners,
text: Mɔ Zozi juli Zufʋ kpain nɩn awulo ɛbɛlɛ m'ɔ nwunlin abile mma'n-mɔ nʋn awɔ́lɛ mma'n-mɔ'n, yɩ́ nwan:

ref: he said, “Get out of here! The little girl isn't dead. She is just asleep.” Everyone started laughing at Jesus.
text: “Ɛmɔ fi ɛwa bɛ fíte, nán batʋnman'n wuli man, ɔ lɛ dafɩ.” Zozi hanlɩn sɔ a, menian'n-mɔ kɔ tu kɔ gua bɛ kɔ sɩlɩ yɩ́.

ref: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
text: Mɔ bɛ manlɩn menian'n-mɔ fitelɩ yuelɩ'n, Zozi kɔ wʋlʋ sua nɩn anun ɛlɔ, ɔ kɔ sɔ talua kaan nɩn asa, talua'n kɔ jasʋ.

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
text: Mɔ Zozi juli yɩ́ awulo'n, anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ'n-mɔ kɔ kpunnge yɩ́ nwʋn ɛbɛlɛ, nán ɔ'a bisa bɛ́ kɛ: “Asʋ ɛmɔ de di kɛ mɩn kʋala mɩn man ɛmɔ anwʋn sa ɔ?” Mɔ Zozi bisalɩ bɛ́ kosuan sɔ'n, bɛ́ nwan: “Yuo o! Yɛ́ Mɩn, ɛ kʋala yɩ́ yɔ.”

ref: Jesus touched their eyes and said, “Because of your faith, you will be healed.”
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi kɔ fua bɛ́ nyɩn'n, yɩ́ nwan: “Ɛmɔ dedi nɩn anwʋn, man ɔ yɔ́ sɔ mán ɛmɔ!”

ref: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Ɛbɛlɛ ala, bɛ́ nyɩn'n-mɔ kɔ tike tike. Zozi kɔ jʋjɔ aminle sʋ kɔ hele bɛ́, yɩ́ nwan: “Ɛmɔ tíe yɩ́ kpa, nán bɛ man sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ anzʋ tɩ.”

ref: But they left and talked about him to everyone in that part of the country. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Talk
text: Nan mɔ anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ nnyuan sɔ'n fi ɛbɛlɛ hɔlɩ'n, bɛ kɔ bɔ Zozi anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ ɛbɛlɛ mân sɔ nɩn anun lɩka kʋalaatin. Zozi 'ba man amunlefʋɛ kʋn jʋjɔ

ref: Jesus went to every town and village. He taught in their synagogues and preached the good news about God's kingdom. Jesus also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Zozi nanndɩlɩ kulo mgbili'n-mɔ ɔ nʋn ngangan'n-mɔ asʋ, ɔ hehelɩ menian'n-mɔ ninnge Zufʋ'n-mɔ asɔnɩn sua'n-mɔ anun. Ɔ bɔlɩ Ɛjɔlɛkpa'n m'ɔ tʋ de Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n ɔ helelɩ bɛ́. Bɛ́ mɔ ewue ngacile ngacile kʋalaa wɔ bɛ́ nwʋn'n, Zozi manlɩn bɛ́ nwʋn salɩ.

ref: When he saw the crowds, he felt sorry for them. They were confused and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
text: Mɔ Zozi nwunlin meninsʋnman'n, bɛ́ nwʋn yɔlɩ yɩ́ anwunnvoe. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ b'a fɛ, ɛsɛ bɛ́ nwʋn a bubu bɛ́. Bɛ tɩ kɛ mmʋa mɔ bɛ le man bʋa ɛnɩanfʋɛ ɔ.

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: Ɛhɩ a, ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ: “Ebo'n tɩ kpili, nan kʋsʋ ajunmanfʋ'n-mɔ a nzʋn man.

ref: Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.”
text: Yɩ́ ti, ɛmɔ sɛ́lɛ ebo nɩn asʋ mɩnlɩan'n maan ɔ gúa ajunmanfʋ dɔʋn yɩ́ ebo nɩn asʋ.”

ref: Philip, Bartholomew, Thomas, Matthew the tax collector, James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles.
text: Filipʋ, Baatelemin, Toma, Matie m'ɔ dide ajule'n, Alɩfe awa Zʋakɩ, Tade,

ref: But when someone arrests you, don't worry about what you will say or how you will say it. At that time you will be given the words to say.
text: Sɛ bɛ nʋn ɛmɔ kɔ bɛ́ mɔ bɛ di ɛjɔlɛ nɩn aja sʋ a, mɔ ɛmɔ 'ba han'n, nán bɛ susu yɩ́ nwʋn, ɛsɛ nán bɛ susu kɛ ɛmɔ 'kpɩn kan ɛjɔlɛ'n. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ɛjɔlɛ m'ɔ di mɔ ɛmɔ kan'n 'ba tɔ ɛmɔ anʋan anun tɛmʋn sɔ'n.

ref: Go only to the people of Israel, because they are like a flock of lost sheep.
text: Nan ɛmɔ hɔ́ Izalayɛ amma'n-mɔ anwʋn. Afɩ bɛ tɩ kɛ mmʋa mɔ b'a minlin ɔ.

ref: I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves. So be as wise as snakes and as innocent as doves.
text: “Ɛmɔ nɩ́an, mɩɩn sʋan ɛmɔ kɛ mmʋa wɔ bedi'n-mɔ afian. Ɛhɩka ati, ɛmɔ nɩ́an bɛ́ nwʋn ye kɛ owo-mɔ, ɛsɛ ɛmɔ yɔ́ mɛlɛhʋandɩ kɛ abubule'n-mɔ.

ref: The others were Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus. Instructions for the Twelve Apostles (Mark
text: Simʋn m'ɔ kpʋnndɛ yɩ́ mân'n fanwʋndi'n yɛ̂ Zudasɩ Isikaliɔtɩ mɔ ɔ 'yɩ Zozi man yɩ́ mgbɔfʋɛ'n-mɔ. Zozi 'ba sʋan yɩ́ menian bulu nʋn nnyuan'n (Maakɩ

ref: Jesus sent out the twelve apostles with these instructions: Stay away from the Gentiles and don't go to any Samaritan town.
text: Menian bulu nʋn nnyuan'n mɔ Zozi sʋanlɩn bɛ́ nɩn anɩn. Asannan nán ɔ'a sʋan bɛ́'n, afʋtu m'ɔ tuli bɛ́ nɩn ahɩ, yɩ́ nwan: “Bɛ́ mɔ bɛ tɩ man Zufʋ'n, nán bɛ kɔ bɛ́ nwʋn, ɛsɛ nán bɛ kɔ Samali kulo'n-mɔ asʋ.

ref: So when you go to a town or a village, find someone able and willing to have you as their guest and stay with them until you leave.
text: “Kulo biala mɔ ɛmɔ kɔ hɔ sʋ'n, sɛ ɔ tɩ kpili o, sɛ ɔ tɩ kaan o, ɛmɔ bísa bísa, sʋanlan m'ɔ kulo kɛ ɔ sike ɛmɔ'n, ɛmɔ tánlan yɩ́ awulo ɛbɛlɛ dede ɔ jú cɩan mɔ ɛmɔ fi kulo sɔ nɩn asʋ bɛ 'jasʋ'n.

ref: I promise you the day of judgment will be easier for the towns of Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town. Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ ndɛɛ ebua cɩan nɩn anun, kulo sɔ nɩn asʋ menian'n-mɔ, yalɛ mɔ bɛ 'nwun yɩ́'n, ɔ 'tala Sodɔmʋn nʋn Gomɔɔ kulo'n-mɔ asʋ menian'n-mɔ dɩɛ'n. Ahʋlʋwa'n (Maakɩ

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: “Menian mmie-mɔ 'ba yɩ bɛ́ nianman'n-mɔ man maan bɛ kun bɛ́. Sɩɛ mmie-mɔ kʋsʋ 'ba yɩ bɛ́ mma'n-mɔ man maan bɛ kun bɛ́. Mma mmie-mɔ kʋsʋ 'ba jasʋ bɛ́ sɩ-mɔ akɔmɩn anun maan bɛ kun bɛ́.

ref: Watch out for people who will take you to court and have you beaten in their synagogues.
text: Ɛmɔ nɩ́an bɛ́ nwʋn wɔ menian'n-mɔ dunman nun. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ bɛ 'ba fa ɛmɔ bɛ kɔ bɛ́ mɔ bɛ di ɛjɔlɛ nɩn aja sʋ. Bɛ 'fin ɛmɔ mgbele bɛ́ Nyanmɩan sua'n-mɔ anun.

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
text: Mɩ́n dunman nun, bɛ 'ba fa ɛmɔ bɛ kɔ mân mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn mmelemgbin'n-mɔ aja sʋ maan ɛmɔ di mɩ́n nwʋn adanzɩɛ bɛ kele bɛ́ ɔ nʋn bɛ́ mɔ bɛ tɩ man Zufʋ'n.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: Ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ ɛmɔ 'ba han'n, ɔ fi man ɛmɔ muonun, nan ɛmɔ Asɩ'n Wawɛ'n yɛ̂ ɔ 'fa ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n wula ɛmɔ anʋan ɔ.

ref: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you remain faithful until the end, you will be saved.
text: Mɩ́n dunman nun, menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa 'fa ɛmɔ anwʋn ɛkpɔ. Nan sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ jinlan yɩ́ ja nun kpunndiin dede ɔ kɔ ju yɩ́ ayuelɩɛ'n, sʋanlan sɔ'n, ɔ 'nyan yɩ́ ti.

ref: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
text: Kulo mɔ ɛmɔ kɔ hɔ sʋ mɔ bɛ lɛ kele ɛmɔ ahʋlʋwa'n, ɛmɔ nwánndi bɛ hɔ́ fʋfɔlɛ asʋ. Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ ɛmɔ ngɔ yue man Izalayɛ kulo'n-mɔ kʋalaa asʋ kpanza nán *Mân Baa nɩn a a.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: “Sʋanlan mɔ bɛ lɛ kele yɩ́ like'n, ɔ ndala man sʋanlan'n m'ɔ lɛ kele yɩ́'n. Akʋa'n kʋsʋ ndala man yɩ́ mɩn'n.

ref: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
text: M'ɔ cian'n, y'ɔ le kɛ sʋanlan'n mɔ bɛ lɛ kele yɩ́ like'n, ɔ káci kɛ sʋanlan'n m'ɔ lɛ kele yɩ́ like'n. Yɛ̂ akʋa'n kʋsʋ, ɔ káci kɛ yɩ́ mɩn'n. Sɛ bɛ lɛ fɛlɛ awulo kpain'n muonun *Bɛlɩzebulu a, nán awulo nɩn anun menian'n-mɔ yɛ̂ bɛ ngɔ fɛlɛ bɛ́ kɛ bɛ kulo ɔ! Sʋanlan m'ɔ sɛ kɛ ɛmɔ súlo yɩ́'n (Luku

ref: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
text: Ɛmɔ nán bɛ sulo menian'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ kun anwʋnnaan'n kʋsʋ bɛ ngʋala man wawɛ'n kun'n. Nan ɛmɔ súlo Nyanmɩan mɔ kʋala kɛ ɔ man anwʋnnaan'n nʋn wawɛ'n wu sɩ̂n'n m'ɔ le man ayuelɩɛ nɩn anun'n.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Asʋ bɛ ndɔnɩn man nnʋnman atulufia nganngan nnyuan gua dufule ɔ? Nan kʋsʋ bɛ nnyan man bɛ́ bɛ ngun man bɛ́ mgban. Nunhan kʋn mɔ bɛ kɔ nyan yɩ́ bɛ kɔ hun yɩ́'n, anɩn Nyanmɩan muonun a man yɩ́ nwʋn atɩn.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: Nan ɛmɔ dɩɛ, ɛmɔ ati enyuan'n muonun, Nyanmɩan sɩ yɩ́ nʋan.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: Nán man ɛmɔ sulo like fɩ́ɩ́. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ɛmɔ anwʋn cian kpa tala atulufia dɔʋn kpa. Bɛ́ mɔ bɛ kan kɛ bɛ nzɩ man Zozi'n (Luku

ref: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
text: “Ɛhɩka ati, sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ han yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ anyunnun kɛ ɔ tɩ mɩ́n dɩɛ'n, mɩ́n kʋsʋ mɩn 'kan yɩ́ mɩ́n Sɩ m'ɔ wɔ anwunno nɩn anyunnun kɛ sʋanlan sɔ'n tɩ mɩ́n dɩɛ.

ref: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
text: Nan sɛ sʋanlan kʋn kan yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ anyunnun kɛ ɔ nzɩ man mɩ́n an, mɩ́n kʋsʋ mɩn 'kan yɩ́ mɩ́n Sɩ m'ɔ wɔ anwunno nɩn anyunnun kɛ mɩn nzɩ man sʋanlan sɔ'n! Nán anzʋnunjɔ ɔ, nan ɛlɔɛ ɔ (Luku

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Nán man ɛmɔ jʋnlɩn kɛ m malɩ mân nɩn anzʋnunjɔ ɛman. Nán anzʋnunjɔ yɛ̂ n valɩ malɩ ɔ, nan ɛlɔɛ ɔ.

ref: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
text: M malɩ baa'n nʋn yɩ́ sɩ bɛ́ afian ɛsɛcɩɛ. M malɩ nian'n nʋn yɩ́ wa balasua nɩn afian ɛsɛcɩɛ. Ɛsɛ kʋsʋ m malɩ ahelenian'n-mɔ afian ɛsɛcɩɛ.

ref: Your worst enemies will be in your own family.
text: Awulo kʋn anun menian'n-mɔ 'kpɔ bɛ́ nwʋn.

ref: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: “Sʋanlan m'ɔ kulo yɩ́ sɩ anaan yɩ́ nin tala mɩ́n'n, sʋanlan sɔ'n, anɩn ɔ tɩ man mɩ́n dɩɛ. Sʋanlan kʋsʋ m'ɔ kulo yɩ́ wa belenzua anaan yɩ́ wa balasua tala mɩ́n'n, sʋanlan sɔ'n kʋsʋ, anɩn ɔ tɩ man mɩ́n dɩɛ.

ref: And unless you are willing to take up your cross and follow me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: Sʋanlan mɔ ɔ'a nva man yɩ́ kʋlʋwa'n ɔ'a ndʋ man yɩ́ kɔmɩn sʋ, ɔ'a nzi man mɩ́n sʋ'n, sʋanlan sɔ'n, ɔ tɩ man mɩ́n dɩɛ.

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Sʋanlan m'ɔ kulo kɛ ɔ kpʋnndɛ yɩ́ ngʋan'n, sʋanlan sɔ'n, ɔ 'minlin yɩ́ ngʋan'n. Nan sʋanlan mɔ mɩ́n dunman nun ɔ kɔ minlin yɩ́ ngʋan'n, ɔ 'nyan ngʋan. Ahatua'n (Maakɩ

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ sɔ Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ kʋn nun kɛ bɛ sɔ bɛ́ nun'n, kpɔmanfʋɛ ahatua yɛ ɔ 'nyan yɩ́ ɔ. Sʋanlan kʋsʋ m'ɔ kɔ sɔ sɛsɛfʋɛ kʋn nun kɛ bɛ sɔ bɛ nun'n, sɛsɛfʋɛ ahatua yɛ̂ ɔ 'nyan yɩ́ ɔ.

ref: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
text: “Sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ sɔ ɛmɔ nun'n, anɩn mɩ́n yɛ̂ ɔ'a sɔ mɩ́n nun ɔ. M'ɔ kɔ sɔ mɩ́n nun'n, anɩn sʋanlan'n m'ɔ sʋanlɩn mɩ́n'n nun yɛ̂ ɔ'a sɔ ɔ.

ref: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
text: Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ: sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ man mmatʋnman ɛhɩ-mɔ anun kʋn nzue fɔlɔlɔ mgban nganlannzan kʋn kɔ nʋn, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ɔ tɩ mɩ́n sʋanlan'n, sʋanlan sɔ'n, ɔ 'nyan yɩ́ nwʋn ahatua.”

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Mɔ Zʋan menian'n-mɔ salɩ hɔlɩ'n, Zozi kɔ han Zʋan anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ kɔ hele menian'n-mɔ. Yɩ́ nwan: “Nzukɛ yɛ̂ ɛmɔ hɔlɩ yɩ́ ɛnɩan ɛwâ nɩn anun ɛlɔ'n? Ndɛɛ mɔ anwʋnman'n lɛ fita yɩ́ ɔ?

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: “Afɩ Zʋan walɩ a, ɔ'a nni man yɩ́ nʋan nun ɛsɛ ɔ'a nnʋn man nzan. Menian'n-mɔ nwan: ‘Wawɛ ɛtɛ wɔ yɩ́ nun.’

ref: After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he left and began teaching and preaching in the towns.
text: Mɔ Zozi yuelɩ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n-mɔ kan kele yɩ́ menian bulu nʋn nnyuan'n, ɔ kɔ hɔ kɔ hehele Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ nɩn ɛbɛlɛ kulo kulo'n-mɔ asʋ.

ref: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
text: Tɛmʋn sɔ'n, anɩn b'a tʋ Zʋan fiadɩ. M'ɔ tɩlɩ junman'n mɔ Kilisi lɛ di nɩn angan'n, ɔ kɔ sʋan yɩ́ menian'n mmie-mɔ yɩ́ nwʋn ɛlɔ.

ref: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
text: Bɛ hɔlɩ juli Zozi anwʋn ɛlɔ a, bɛ́ nwan: “Yɛ́ Mɩn, sʋanlan'n mɔ Zʋan hanlɩn yɩ́ ɛjɔlɛ kɛ ɔ 'ba'n y'ɔ le wɔ́ anaan sʋanlan sɔ'n tɛ wɔ sian ɔ?”

ref: The blind are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People with leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
text: Anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ'n-mɔ nwun asɩ. Bubuluwafʋɛ'n-mɔ nanndɩ. Kokobefʋɛ'n-mɔ anwʋn a sa. Anzʋtiliwafʋɛ'n-mɔ tɩ ɛjɔlɛ. Ewue mma'n-mɔ fi ewue nun b'a tinnge. Ɛjɔlɛkpa'n kʋsʋ, bɛ lɛ bɔ bɛ kele ehianfʋɛ'n-mɔ.

ref: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
text: Nyila hán sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ tila yɩ́ dedi'n m'ɔ le yɩ́ mɩ́n nwʋn nɩn anun'n.”

ref: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
text: Anaan nzu like dɩɛ yɛ̂ ɛmɔ hɔlɩ yɩ́ ɛnɩan ɔ? Ɛmɔ hɔlɩ nɩanlɩn sʋanlan m'ɔ wula taladɩɛ kanlanman ɔ? Nan bɛ́ mɔ bɛ wula taladɩɛ nganlanman nganlanman'n, bɛ gua mmelemgbin'n-mɔ awulo.

ref: What did you really go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was. I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
text: Anɩn nzu lɩlɩ yɛ̂ ɛmɔ hɔlɩ yɩ́ ɛnɩan'n? Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ kʋn ɔ? Sakpa, mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ ɔ tala Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ bɔbɔ.

ref: I tell you no one ever born on this earth is greater than John the Baptist. But whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than John.
text: “Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ mân ɛhɩ anun, bɛ wʋlɩ man sʋanlan bie m'ɔ tɩ kpili tala Zʋan ɔ. Nan kʋsʋ sʋanlan m'ɔ tɩ kaan Anwunno Belemgbin Mân nɩn anun'n, ɔ tɩ kpili tala Zʋan.

ref: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.
text: Ɔ fi mɛlɛ mɔ Zʋan mantanlɩn Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ nɩn ɛhan dede m'ɔ ju ɛnɛ'n, Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n lɛ tɛlɛ anwʋnsɛlɛ sʋ kpa. Nan ati anun ɛtɛ mma'n-mɔ a fa yɩ́ nwʋn ɛkpɔ kpa.

ref: And if you believe them, John is Elijah, the prophet you are waiting for.
text: Ɛmɔ lé li kɛ *Eli mɔ bɛ hanlɩn yɩ́ ɛwalɛ nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n y'ɔ le Zʋan.

ref: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
text: ‘Y'a bɔ abile dede ɛmɔ a nzi man. Y'a tʋ ejue y'a bɔ ɛmɔ yalɛ sʋ, kʋsʋ ɛmɔ a nzɩsa man bɛ́ sa b'a ngua man bɛ́ ti b'a nzun man.’

ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: *Mân Baa'n walɩ a, ɔ didi, ɔ nʋn nzan, ɛsɛ menian'n-mɔ nwan: ‘Bɛ nɩ́an, sʋanlan ɛhɩ dɩɛ, yɩ́ junman nɩn ala y'ɔ le alɩɛ elie nʋn nzan ɛnʋan. Yɩ́ manngʋn y'ɔ le bɛ́ mɔ bɛ dide ajule'n, ɔ nʋn ɛtɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ’. Nan mɔ Nyanmɩan a yɔ'n kele kɛ ɔ tɩ sɛsɛ sakpa.” Zozi 'ba tu Galile mân nɩn anun menian'n-mɔ anyunnun (Luku

ref: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
text: Kulo'n-mɔ mɔ Zozi yɔlɩ asinbɛnwʋn ninnge dɔʋn sʋ, mɔ ɛbɛlɛ menian'n-mɔ a nnun man bɛ́ nwʋn b'a ngaci man bɛ́ abalabɔ'n, Zozi tuli bɛ́ nyunnun.

ref: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.
text: Ɛmɔ Kapɛɛnawɔmʋn amma, ɛmɔ kʋsʋ jʋnlɩn kɛ ɛmɔ 'kuku bɛ́ nwʋn sʋ dede bɛ nʋn Nyanmɩan sɛ ɔ? Bɛ 'kan ɛmɔ asɩ dede ɛmɔ kɔ sɩ̂n m'ɔ le man ayuelɩɛ nɩn anun. Afɩ sɛ ɔ tɩ kɛ asinbɛnwʋn ninnge'n mɔ n yɔlɩ yɩ́ ɛmɔ afian ɛbɛlɛ'n, n yɔlɩ yɩ́ Sodɔmʋn an, ahan ɛnɛ nʋn ɛnɛ, kulo sɔ'n tɛ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ.

ref: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
text: Ɛhɩka ati, mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ ndɛɛ ebua cɩan nɩn anun, yalɛ mɔ ɛmɔ 'nwun yɩ́'n, ɔ 'tala Sodɔmʋn dɩɛ'n.” Sɩɛ'n nʋn Baa'n (Luku

ref: At that moment Jesus said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people.
text: Mɛlɛ sɔ nɩn anun, Zozi kukuli yɩ́ nʋan sʋ a, yɩ́ nwan: “Mɩ́n Sɩ, wɔ́ mɔ ɛ tɩ anwunno nʋn asɩɛ nɩn asʋ Mɩnlɩan'n, mɩn 'da wɔ́ asɩ. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ a fa ninnge ɛhɩ-mɔ a fɩa ngɛlɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ, ɔ nʋn menian'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ sɩ ninnge dɔʋn'n, yɛ̂ a yɩ a hele mmatʋnman'n-mɔ.

ref: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
text: “Mɩ́n Sɩ a fa ninnge'n kʋalaa a wula mɩ́n sa nun. Sɛ nán Sɩɛ nɩn an, sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ nzɩ man Baa'n. Yɛ̂ sɛ nán Baa'n nʋn menian'n-mɔ mɔ Baa'n kulo kɛ ɔ yɩ Sɩɛ'n kele bɛ́ a, sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ nzɩ man Sɩɛ'n.

ref: If you are tired from carrying heavy burdens, come to me and I will give you rest.
text: “Ɛmɔ mɔ bɛ sʋ ninnge nʋnnɔɛ mɔ b'a fɛ'n, ɛmɔ bála mɩ́n nwʋn nán mɩn 'ba sike ɛmɔ maan bɛ de bɛ́ ɛnwʋnmɩan.

ref: Take the yoke I give you. Put it on your shoulders and learn from me. I am gentle and humble, and you will find rest.
text: Ɛmɔ sɔ́ mɩ́n ngehele nɩn anun nán ɛmɔ mán n gékele bɛ́ ninnge. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ n dɩ sʋanlan bɛtɛɛ, mɩ́n ti anun tɩ man ɛtɛ, nán mɩn 'ba man ɛmɔ de bɛ́ ɛnwʋnmɩan.

ref: This yoke is easy to bear, and this burden is light.
text: Mɩ́n ngehele'n mɔ mɩɩn kekele ɛmɔ'n, ɔ nyɔ man sɩ, ninnge'n mɔ mɩɩn kekele ɛmɔ'n, ɔ tɩ fɔtɩlɩfɔ”.

ref: Some Pharisees noticed this and said to Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do this!”
text: Mɔ *Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ nwunlin yɩ́ sɔ'n, bɛ kɔ han bɛ kɔ hele Zozi kɛ: “Nɩan, like mɔ yɛ́ mala'n tua yɛ́ kɛ nán yɛ yɔ yɩ́ ɛnwʋnmɩan ele cɩan'n, wɔ́ menian'n-mɔ yɔ.”

ref: The crowds were so amazed they asked, “Could Jesus be the Son of David?”
text: Ɛjɔlɛ'n sinlin menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa anwʋn, bɛ́ nwan: “Asʋ nán belemgbin *Davidi anʋnman nɩn an?”

ref: But when I force out demons by the power of God's Spirit, it proves that God's kingdom has already come to you.
text: Nan sɛ Nyanmɩan Wawɛ'n yɛ̂ mɩn fa mɩn fʋan wawɛ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ a, anɩn ɔ kele kɛ Nyanmɩan Belemgbin elie mɛlɛ nɩn a tʋ ɛmɔ.

ref: Haven't you read in the Law of Moses that the priests are allowed to work in the temple on the Sabbath? But no one says they are guilty of breaking the law of the Sabbath.
text: Asʋ ɛmɔ kɩnngalɩ man yɩ́ Moyizɩ mala nɩn anun lé kɛ tɛɛyɩfʋɛ'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ di junman ɛnwʋnmɩan ele cɩan'n Nyanmɩan awulo nɩn anun'n, bɛ tʋn mala'n, kʋsʋ bɛ mmu man bɛ́ kɛ bɛ yɔ ɛtɛ ɔ?

ref: He went into the house of God, and then they ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are supposed to eat.
text: Ɔ nʋn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ bɛ wʋlʋlɩ Nyanmɩan sua nɩn anun. Kpaʋn'n-mɔ mɔ b'a fa b'a man Nyanmɩan, mɔ yɛ́ mala'n kele kɛ sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ n'ɔ di bie kɛ Nyanmɩan *tɛɛyɩfʋɛ'n-mɔ ala'n, bɛ lili kpaʋn sɔ'n-mɔ.

ref: Don't you know what the Scriptures mean when they say, “Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others?” If you knew what this means, you would not condemn these innocent disciples of mine.
text: Nyanmɩan hanlɩn kɛ: Mɩn kulo kɛ menian'n-mɔ nwun anwunnvoe bɛ́ afian ɔ tala tɛɛ ɛyɩɛ nɩn anwʋn ninnge'n mɔ bɛ yɔ bɛ man mɩ́n'n. Sɛ ɔ tɩ kɛ ɛmɔ tɩ ɛjɔlɛ ɛhɩ abʋ a, ahan ɛmɔ mmua man menian'n-mɔ kpɔfʋndɛɛ.

ref: Jesus left and went into one of their synagogues,
text: Bɛ hanlɩn ɛhɩ m'ɔ yuelɩ'n, Zozi fi ɛbɛlɛ yɩ́ nʋan y'ɔ le bɛ́ asɔnɩn sua nɩn anun.

ref: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.
text: Zozi kacili yɩ́ nyɩn wuluwafʋɛ nɩn anwʋn ɛlɔ a, yɩ́ nwan: “Tɩnngɛ wɔ́ sa nɩn anun.” Bian'n 'tɩnngɛ yɩ́ sa nɩn anun an, asaa kɔ tɩnngɛ fɔ́ʋ́n kɛ nunhan kʋn'n.

ref: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,
text: Mɔ Zozi tɩlɩ kɛ bɛ lɛ kpʋnndɛ yɩ́ bɛ kun yɩ́'n, ɔ fi ɛbɛlɛ lɩka sɔ'n ɔ jasʋlɩ. Mɔ ɔ 'kɔ'n, menian dɔʋn kpa sili yɩ́ sʋ. Ɔ manlɩn nwuluwafʋɛ'n-mɔ kʋalaa anwʋn salɩ.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
text: Ɔ man Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ *Ezayi ɛjɔlɛ'n m'ɔ hanlɩn'n kpɩnlɩn sʋ. Ɔ hanlɩn kɛ Nyanmɩan nwan:

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: Nɩan, mɩ́n akʋa'n mɔ m'an yɩ yɩ́'n, mɩn kulo yɩ́ m'an fa mɩ́n lʋa'n kʋalaa m'an wula yɩ́ nun. Mɩn 'fa mɩ́n Wawɛ'n mɩn wula yɩ́ nun, ɔ 'kan mɩ́n ndɛɛ ebua nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ ɔ kele mân mân'n-mɔ.

ref: If you are not on my side, you are against me. If you don't gather in the harvest with me, you scatter it.
text: “Sʋanlan m'ɔ tɩ man mɩ́n dɩɛ'n, anɩn ɔ kpɔ mɩ́n. Sʋanlan kʋsʋ m'ɔ nʋn mɩn ngohuan man ninnge'n-mɔ anʋan'n, sʋanlan sɔ'n, anɩn ɔ bɔ bɛ́ asanndɩɛ.

ref: Some people brought to Jesus a man who was blind and could not talk because he had a demon in him. Jesus healed the man, and then he was able to talk and see.
text: Ɛhɩ, bɛ kɔ fa sʋanlan kʋn bɛ kɔ bɛlɛ Zozi. Sʋanlan sɔ'n, wawɛ ɛtɛ wɔ yɩ́ nun, ɔ'a man yɩ́ nyɩn nɩn a sin ɛsɛ ɔ a tɔ amunle. Zozi kɔ man sʋanlan sɔ'n kɔ jʋjɔ ɛsɛ ɔ kɔ nwun asɩ.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Kɛmɔ anɩn Zozi sɩ ajʋnlɩn'n m'ɔ wɔ bɛ́ ti nɩn anun'n, ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele bɛ́ kɛ: “Belemgbin mân kʋn mɔ nun menian'n-mɔ kpɛ bɛ́ nyɩn bɛ kʋn ɛlɔɛ'n, mân sɔ'n bɔ. Kulo kʋn, anaan afilie kʋn mɔ nun menian'n-mɔ kpɛ bɛ́ nyɩn bɛ kʋn ɛlɔɛ'n, kulo sɔ'n, anaan afilie sɔ nɩn abʋsʋ bɔ.

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Sɛ ɛmɔ jʋnlɩn kɛ Belɩzebulu yɛ̂ ɔ man mɩ́n tunmin maan mɩn fa mɩn fʋan wawɛ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ a, anɩn ɛmɔ menian'n-mɔ dɩɛ, nwan yɛ̂ ɔ man bɛ́ tunmin maan bɛ fʋan wawɛ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ ɔ? Ɛmɔ menian'n-mɔ kele kɛ ɛmɔ di fʋɔ.

ref: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when the demon doesn't find a place,
text: “Mɛlɛ mɔ wawɛ ɛtɛ kʋn fi sʋanlan kʋn anun kɔ fite'n, ɔ kɔ kpʋnndɛ lɩka wʋwʋsɩɩ'n-mɔ ɔ tanlan de yɩ́ ɛnwʋnmɩan.

ref: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and the person ends up in worse shape than before. That's how it will be with you evil people of today. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Mark
text: ɔ sa yɩ́ nzin ɔ kɔ kpʋnndɛ yɩ́ manngʋn nsʋ mɔ bɛ́ ti anun tɩ ɛtɛ kpa bɛ tala yɩ́ ɔ. Ɔ nʋn bɛ ba a tanlan ɛbɛlɛ. Sian dɩɛ, sʋanlan sɔ'n, ɛsɛcɩɛ m'ɔ kɔ sɛcɩ'n, ɔ tala daba'n. Kɛ ajulisʋ menian ɛtɛfʋɛ ɛhɩ-mɔ asɩtanlan'n 'yɔ yɩ́ nɩn ala anɩn.” Zozi anianman'n-mɔ nʋn yɩ́ nin (Maakɩ

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit. You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces.
text: “Baka kpa nɩn asʋ yɛ̂ bɛ tɩ mma kpa nɩn ɔ. Baka ɛtɛ nɩn asʋ kʋsʋ yɛ̂ bɛ tɩ mma ɛtɛ nɩn ɔ. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ baka kʋn m'ɔ jin'n, sɛ ɔ tɩ kpa o, sɛ ɔ tɩ ɛtɛ o, yɩ́ mma m'ɔ kɔ su'n yɛ̂ bɛ fa bɛ nwun yɩ́ ɔ.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Ati anun ɛtɛ mma kɛ owo, ɔ 'yɔ sɛ nán ɛmɔ a han ɛjɔlɛ kpa? Afɩ ɛjɔlɛ m'ɔ wɔ ahʋnlɩn nɩn anun'n, y'ɔ fite bɛ́ nʋan ɔ.

ref: I promise you on the day of judgment, everyone will have to account for every careless word they have spoken.
text: Mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ: Ndɛɛ ebua cɩan nɩn anun, mgban-mgban ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ menian'n-mɔ hanlɩn'n, bɛ 'nwun yɩ́ nwʋn ɛjɔlɛ.

ref: On that day they will be told they are either innocent or guilty because of the things they have said. A Sign from Heaven (Mark
text: Afɩ ɛjɔlɛ m'ɔ fi wɔ́ nʋan kɔ fite'n, yɩ́ nwʋn yɛ̂ bɛ 'nɩan bɛ bua wɔ́ ndɛɛ ɔ.” Menian'n-mɔ 'ba bisa Zozi asinbɛnwʋn like kʋn (Maakɩ

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ yuelɩ'n, *mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n mmie-mɔ nʋn *Falisifʋɛ'n mmie-mɔ kɔ han kɔ hele Zozi kɛ: “Kpain, yɛ kulo kɛ ɛ yɔ asinbɛnwʋn like kʋn maan yɛ nɩan.”

ref: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi nwan: “Ajulisʋ menian ɛtɛfʋɛ ɛhɩ-mɔ mɔ bɛ nzʋ man Nyanmɩan ananhɔlɛ nun'n lɛ kpʋnndɛ asinbɛnwʋn like bɛ nɩan. Bɛ nyɔ man asinbɛnwʋn like fɩ́ɩ́ kʋ́n bɛ ngele man bɛ́ kɛ Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ Zonasɩ dɩɛ nɩn ala.

ref: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
text: Afɩ kɛmɔ Zonasɩ lili cɩan nsan ejue kpili nɩn akunnun'n, yɩ́ sɔ kʋsʋ ala, *Mân Baa'n 'ba li cɩan nsan asɩɛ nɩn abʋ.

ref: On the day of judgment the people of Nineveh will stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah.
text: Ndɛɛ ebua cɩan nɩn anun, Ninninvʋ amma'n-mɔ 'bu ajulisʋ menian'n-mɔ fʋɔ. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ mɔ Ninninvʋ kulo nɩn asʋ amma'n-mɔ tɩlɩ Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ Zonasɩ hanlɩn'n, bɛ nunlin bɛ́ nwʋn bɛ kacili bɛ́ abalabɔ'n. Nan sʋanlan kʋn wɔ ɛwa, ɔ tɩ kpili tala Zonasɩ.

ref: The Queen of the South will also stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something much greater than Solomon. Return of an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Ndɛɛ ebua cɩan nɩn anun, balasua'n m'ɔ tɩ Saba mân nɩn asʋ belemgbin'n, ɔ 'bu ajulisʋ menian'n-mɔ fʋɔ. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ yɩ́ m'ɔ fi mʋa'n, ɔ walɩ tielɩ Salomʋn bɛngɛlɛfʋɛ ɛjɔlɛ'n m'ɔ kan'n. Nan kʋsʋ sʋanlan kʋn wɔ ɛwa, ɔ tɩ kpili tala Salomʋn. Wawɛ ɛtɛ nɩn esa ɛwalɛ nun (Luku

ref: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside because they wanted to talk with him.
text: Zozi anʋan a ndɔ man, yɩ́ nianman'n-mɔ nʋn yɩ́ nin bɛ kɔ a. Anɩn bɛ jijin gua nɩn asʋ bɛ 'kpʋnndɛ atɩn bɛ kan ɛjɔlɛ bɛ kele yɩ́.

ref: Someone told Jesus, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside and want to talk with you.”
text: Sʋanlan kʋn kɔ han kɔ hele yɩ́ kɛ: “Nɩan, wɔ́ nin nʋn wɔ́ nianman'n-mɔ jijin gua sʋ ɛlɔ bɛ 'kpʋnndɛ kɛ bɛ kan ɛjɔlɛ bɛ kele wɔ́.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Zozi kɔ bua sʋanlan sɔ'n kɛ: “Mɩ́n nin'n y'ɔ le nwan, yɛ̂ mɩn nianman'n-mɔ y'ɔ le nwan-mɔ?”

ref: Then he pointed to his disciples and said, “These are my mother and my brothers!
text: Nan ɔ tɩnngɛlɩ yɩ́ sa yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ anwʋn an, yɩ́ nwan: “Ɛmɔ nɩ́an, mɩ́n nin nʋn mɩ́n nianman'n-mɔ, bɛ́ yɛ̂ bɛ gugua ɛwa nɩn ɔ.

ref: Such large crowds gathered around him that he had to sit in a boat, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Meninsʋnman kɔ a yɩ́ nwʋn ɛbɛlɛ. Ɔ kɔ kpati kɔ fʋ kɔ tanlan ɛlɛɛ kʋn anun asue nɩn anyɩnsʋ yɛ̂ meninsʋnman'n jijin asue nɩn anʋan.

ref: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Ɔ kɔ hehele bɛ́ ninnge dɔʋn kpa anyɩnndala nun, yɩ́ nwan: “Sʋanlan kʋn hɔlɩ yɩ́ *bele mma egua.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Bie kʋsʋ kɔ gua nyɔbʋɛ nɩn asʋ. Ɛbɛlɛ ngɛtɩɛ nɩn a nzʋn man. Ɔ kɔ fifi ndɛ-ndɛ. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ɔ'a ngɔ man asɩɛ bʋ.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
text: Bie kʋsʋ kɔ gua eminlan mmowe nɩn anun. Ɔ kɔ fifi. Eminlan mmowe'n kɔ fʋ kɔ gua sʋ.

ref: Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Why do you use stories to speak to the people?”
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi menian'n-mɔ kpunngelɩ yɩ́ a, bɛ́ nwan: “Nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ ɛ 'kan ɛjɔlɛ kele menian'n-mɔ a, ɛ bu yɩ́ anyɩnndala nun ɔ?”

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more. But people who don't have anything will lose even what little they have.
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ sʋanlan m'ɔ le bie'n, bɛ 'gua sʋ maan ɔ sʋn. Nan sʋanlan m'ɔ le man bie'n, kaan'n muonun m'ɔ le yɩ́'n, bɛ 'tʋa yɩ́ bɛ de.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.
text: Ɔ man bɛ́ nwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ *Ezayi hanlɩn'n kpɩnlɩn sʋ. Nyanmɩan nanndɩlɩ Ezayi sʋ ɔ hanlɩn kɛ: Ɛmɔ 'ba tie yɩ́ kpa, kʋsʋ ɛmɔ ngɔ tɩ man yɩ́ bʋ. Ɛmɔ 'ba nɩan yɩ́ kpa, kʋsʋ ɛmɔ ngɔ nwun man yɩ́.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
