text: Nan mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ: Nyanmɩan mgbɔmanfʋɛ dɔʋn kpa, nʋn Nyanmɩan menian dɔʋn kpa, bɛ hulolɩ kɛ ahan bɛ nwun mɔ ɛmɔ lɛ nɩan'n, nan kʋsʋ b'a nnwun man yɩ́. Ɛsɛ bɛ hulolɩ kɛ ahan bɛ tɩ mɔ ɛmɔ lɛ tie'n, nan kʋsʋ b'a ndɩ man.” Zozi 'ba tu anyɩnndala nɩn abʋ (Maakɩ

ref: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they start worrying about the needs of this life and are fooled by the desire to get rich. So the message gets choked out, and they never produce anything.
text: M'ɔ gualɩ eminlan mmowe nɩn anun'n y'ɔ le sʋanlan ɛhɩ m'ɔ tɩ Anwunno Belemgbin Mân nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n, nan kʋsʋ eyuadɩ nɩn anun ninnge'n-mɔ anwʋn nzusue'n nʋn anyɩnbʋlʋ anyanbɛnwʋn'n cɩcɩ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n. Ɔ man ɔ nzu man mma.

ref: Jesus then told them this story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a farmer scattered good seed in a field.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Zozi kɔ bu anyɩnndala kʋn biekun kɔ hele bɛ́. Yɩ́ nwan: “Ɛ 'nwun like mɔ Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n soman yɩ́'n: Ɔ soman sʋanlan kʋn mɔ ɔ'a gua *bele yɩ́ ebo nɩn asʋ ɔ.

ref: But while everyone was sleeping, an enemy came and scattered weed seeds in the field and then left.
text: Nan kɔngɔɛ mɔ menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa yuelɩ dɩda'n, yɩ́ kpɔfʋɛ kʋn kɔ a kɔ lolua ndile ɛtɛ bele nɩn anun, cuein yɛ̂ ɔ hɔlɩ ɔ.

ref: When the plants came up and began to mature, the farmer's servants could see the weeds.
text: Bele'n fifili dede mɔ ɔ 'ju yɩ́ esúe tɛmʋn'n, ndile ɛtɛ'n kɔ fa ebo'n.

ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
text: “Sʋanlan'n m'ɔ le ebo nɩn ajunmanfʋ'n-mɔ walɩ tʋlɩ yɩ́, bɛ́ nwan: ‘Yɛ́ Mɩn, bele yɛ̂ ɛ lualɩ ɔ. Nan ndile ɛtɛ'n mɔ ɔ'a fifi nun'n, ɔ fi nin?’

ref: “An enemy did this,” he replied. His servants then asked, “Do you want us to go out and pull up the weeds?”
text: “Mɩnlɩan'n nwan: ‘Kpɔfʋɛ bie yɛ̂ ɔ'a li junman sɔ nɩn ɔ.’ “Ɛhɩ a, ajunmanfʋ'n-mɔ kɔ bisa yɩ́ kɛ: ‘Ɛ kulo kɛ yɛ hɔ́ tútu ndile ɛtɛ nɩn ɔ?’

ref: “No!” he answered. “You might also pull up the wheat.
text: “Mɩnlɩan'n nwan: ‘Cɛcɛ, sɛ ɛmɔ tutu a, bɛ 'ba tutu bele'n bɛ bʋka sʋ.

ref: Although it is the smallest of all seeds, it grows larger than any garden plant and becomes a tree. Birds even come and nest on its branches.
text: Ninnge mma'n mɔ bɛ dua'n, yɩ́ y'ɔ tɩ bɛ́ nunhan kaan kpa nɩn ɔ. Nan kʋsʋ sɛ ɔ fifi a, ɔ nyin tala ninnge mma'n-mɔ kʋalaatin. Ɔ kaci dukpa nnʋnman'n-mɔ ba bɛ nwʋn bɛ́ sâ yɩ́ sa mma'n-mɔ asʋ.” Fali ayile nɩn anwʋn anyɩnndala'n (Luku

ref: Jesus told them another story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a farmer plants a mustard seed in a field.
text: Zozi kɔ bu anyɩnndala biekun kɔ hele bɛ́, yɩ́ nwan: “Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n, ɔ tɩ kɛ muntadɩ baa mɔ sʋanlan a fa a hɔ a lua yɩ́ ebo sʋ ɔ.

ref: Jesus used stories when he spoke to the people. In fact, he did not tell them anything without using stories.
text: Ɛjɔlɛ ɛhɩ kʋalaa mɔ ɔ 'kan kele menian'n-mɔ'n, ɔ bu yɩ́ anyɩnndala nun. Sɛ ɔ'a mmun man anyɩnndala a, ɔ ngan man ɛjɔlɛ'n ɔ ngele man bɛ́.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I will use stories to speak my message and to explain things hidden since the creation of the world.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Weeds
text: Ɛhɩ m'ɔ yɔlɩ'n manlɩn ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ'n hanlɩn'n kpɩnlɩn sʋ. Nyanmɩan manlɩn kpɔmanfʋɛ'n hanlɩn kɛ: Mɩn 'bu anyɩnndala nán m'an han ɛjɔlɛ'n m'an hele bɛ́. Sunmin kɛ bɛ bɔlɩ mân'n, ninnge m'ɔ wɔ nvɩalɩɛ nun'n, mɩn 'bɔ yɩ́ nwʋn ɛjɔlɛ. Zozi 'ba tu ndile ɛtɛ nɩn anwʋn anyɩnndala nɩn abʋ

ref: After Jesus left the crowd and went inside, his disciples came to him and said, “Explain to us the story about the weeds in the wheat field.”
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ yuelɩ'n, ɔ gualɩ meninsʋnman nɩn atɩn yɛ̂ ɔ hɔlɩ awulo ɔ. Yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kpunngelɩ yɩ́ a, bɛ́ nwan: “Tu ndile ɛtɛ'n m'ɔ fifi ebo nɩn asʋ nɩn anwʋn anyɩnndala nɩn abʋ kele yɛ́.”

ref: Jesus answered: The one who scattered the good seed is the Son of Man.
text: Zozi nwan: “Sʋanlan'n m'ɔ le gua *bele mma kpa'n y'ɔ le *Mân Baa'n.

ref: The field is the world, and the good seeds are the people who belong to the kingdom. The weeds are those who belong to the evil one,
text: Ebo'n y'ɔ le mân'n. Bele mma kpa'n y'ɔ le bɛ́ mɔ bɛ́ tɩ Anwunno Belemgbin Mân nɩn anun menian'n. Ndile ɛtɛ'n, y'ɔ le bɛ́ mɔ bɛ tɩ Abɔnsanmʋn menian'n.

ref: and the one who scattered them is the devil. The harvest is the end of time, and angels are the ones who bring in the harvest.
text: Kpɔfʋɛ'n mɔ ɔ'a lua ndile ɛtɛ'n y'ɔ le Abɔnsanmʋn. Bele mma nɩn ɛtɩɛ tɛmʋn'n y'ɔ le mân nɩn ayuelɩɛ nun, yɛ̂ bɛ́ mɔ bɛ 'tɩ ebo'n y'ɔ le nyanmɩansʋ mmɔfʋɛ'n-mɔ.

ref: Weeds are gathered and burned. That's how it will be at the end of time.
text: “Kɛmɔ bɛ tutu ndile ɛtɛ nɩan an, bɛ gua yɩ́ sɩ̂n nɩn anun'n, sɛ mân nɩn ayuelɩɛ'n ju a, kɛ bɛ 'yɔ yɩ́ nɩn anɩn.

ref: The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will gather from his kingdom everyone who does wrong or causes others to sin.
text: Mân Baa'n 'sʋan yɩ́ nyanmɩansʋ mmɔfʋɛ'n-mɔ maan bɛ yɩ bɛ́ kʋalaa mɔ bɛ man ɛhɩnlɩn-mɔ tɔ ɛtɛ nɩn anun ɔ nʋn bɛ́ mɔ bɛ yɔ ɛtɛ'n fi yɩ́ Belemgbin Mân nɩn anun.

ref: Then he will throw them into a flaming furnace, where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
text: Bɛ yɩ bɛ́ a, bɛ 'gua bɛ́ sɩ̂n gɔhɩɩn nɩn anun. Ɛbɛlɛ yɛ̂ bɛ 'sun bɛ kʋan bɛ́ je nɩn ɔ.

ref: But everyone who has done right will shine like the sun in their Father's kingdom. If you have ears, pay attention! A Hidden Treasure
text: Nan menian mɔ bɛ́ nyɩn sɔ Nyanmɩan'n, bɛ 'ta bɛ́ Sɩ Belemgbin Mân nɩn anun kɛ sɛnzɛ m'ɔ lɛ ta ɔ. M'ɔ sɩ fasie'n, ɔ fá sie!” Anwʋndɩɛ'n m'ɔ fɩa nɩn anwʋn anyɩnndala'n

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when someone finds a treasure hidden in a field and buries it again. Such a person is happy and goes and sells everything in order to buy that field. A Valuable Pearl
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi nwan: “Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n tɩ kɛ esika kɔkɔlɛ m'ɔ fɩa ebo kʋn asʋ. Sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ fite sʋ'n, yɩ́ kʋsʋ ɔ fa fɩa biekun. Ɔ di fɛ, ɔ tɔnɩn yɩ́ nwʋn ninnge'n kʋalaatin ɔ to ebo sɔ'n. Afile nɩn anwʋn anyɩnndala'n

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a shop owner is looking for fine pearls.
text: “Ɛsɛ Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n tɩ kɛ watafʋɛ kʋn m'ɔ lɛ kpʋnndɛ afile nganlanman to ɔ.

ref: After finding a very valuable one, the owner goes and sells everything in order to buy that pearl. A Fish Net
text: Mɛlɛ m'ɔ kɔ nwun kʋn m'ɔ tɩ kpa'n, ɔ kɔ tɔnɩn yɩ́ nwʋn ninnge'n kʋalaatin ɔ to afile sɔ'n. Dada nɩn anwʋn anyɩnndala'n

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a net is thrown into a lake and catches all kinds of fish.
text: “Ɛsɛ Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n tɩ kɛ dada mɔ bɛ gua yɩ́ jenvie nɩn anun ɔ. Ejue ngacile kʋalaa wʋlʋ nun.

ref: When the net is full, it is dragged to the shore, and the fishermen sit down to separate the fish. They keep the good ones, but throw the bad ones away.
text: Ɔ yi a, bɛ hyʋhyʋɩn gua fie bɛ tanlan asɩ bɛ kpa ejue'n-mɔ anun. Bɛ gua mgbakpa'n-mɔ ece nun yɛ̂ ɛtɛ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ, bɛ ju bɛ gua.

ref: That's how it will be at the end of time. Angels will come and separate the evil people from the ones who have done right.
text: Mân nɩn ayuelɩɛ nun kɛ ɔ 'ba yɔ yɩ́ nɩn anɩn. Nyanmɩansʋ mmɔfʋɛ'n-mɔ 'ba sie menin mgbakpa'n-mɔ ebue kʋn

ref: Then those evil people will be thrown into a flaming furnace, where they will cry and grit their teeth in pain. New and Old Treasures
text: bɛ gua ɛtɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ sɩ̂n gɔhɩɩn nɩn anun. Ɛbɛlɛ yɛ̂ bɛ 'sun bɛ kʋan bɛ́ je nɩn ɔ.” Anwʋndɩɛ nvʋfɔlɛ'n-mɔ nʋn nnaba'n-mɔ

ref: Jesus asked his disciples if they understood all these things. They said, “Yes, we do.”
text: Zozi kɔ bisa yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ: “Asʋ ɛmɔ a tɩ ɛjɔlɛ ɛhɩ-mɔ abʋ ɔ?” Bɛ́ nwan: “Yuo o!”

ref: So he told them, “Every student of the Scriptures who becomes a disciple in the kingdom of heaven is like someone who brings out new and old treasures from the storeroom.” The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Mark
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi nwan: “Ɛhɩka ati, mala nɩn asʋ kpain biala m'ɔ kɔ kaci Anwunno Belemgbin Mân nɩn anun sʋanlan'n, anwʋndɩɛ'n m'ɔ nyan yɩ́'n, ninnge nvʋfɔlɛ wɔ nun yɛ̂ nnaba kʋsʋ wɔ nun.” Nazalɛtɩ amma'n-mɔ nne man Zozi bɛ nni man (Maakɩ

ref: When Jesus had finished telling these stories, he left
text: Mɔ Zozi yuelɩ anyɩnndala ɛhɩ-mɔ bubu'n, ɔ fi ɛbɛlɛ

ref: and went to his hometown. He taught in their synagogue, and the people were so amazed that they asked, “Where does he get all this wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: ɔ kɔ hɔ kulo'n mɔ bɛ talɩ yɩ́ nɩn asʋ. Ɔ kɔ wʋlʋ asɔnɩn sua nɩn anun ɔ kɔ hehele ɛbɛlɛ menian'n-mɔ ninnge, ɛjɔlɛ'n kɔ sin bɛ́ kʋalaa mɔ bɛ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ nɩn anwʋn kpa. Bɛ́ nwan: “Ɔ nyanlin ngɛlɛ ɛhɩ nin? Tunmin benin yɛ̂ ɔ fa yɔ asinbɛnwʋn ninnge ɛhɩ-mɔ ɔ?

ref: Isn't he the son of the carpenter? Isn't Mary his mother, and aren't James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?
text: Nán junnvʋɛ nɩn awa belenzua nɩn ɔ? Nán yɩ́ nin y'ɔ le Mali ɔ? Nán yɩ́ nianman mmelenzua'n-mɔ y'ɔ le Zʋakɩ, Zozɛfʋ, Simʋn nʋn Zudɩ ɔ?

ref: Don't his sisters still live here in our town? How can he do all this?”
text: Yɩ́ nianman mmalasua'n-mɔ kʋalaa, bɛ nnʋn man yɛ́ yɛ̂ ɔ gua ɛwa ɔ? Ɔ nʋn tunmin ɛhɩ kʋalaa fi nin?”

ref: So the people were upset because of what he was doing. But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their own family.”
text: Ajʋnlɩn sɔ'n m'ɔ wɔ bɛ́ ti anun'n maan b'a nnwun man asɩ b'a nne man Zozi b'a nni man. Ɛhɩ a, Zozi kɔ han kɔ hele bɛ́ kɛ: “Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ kʋn, yɩ́ kulo nɩn asʋ nʋn yɩ́ afilie nɩn anun menian'n-mɔ mmu man yɩ́ like.”

ref: And because the people did not have any faith, Jesus did not work many miracles there.
text: Dedi'n mɔ ɛbɛlɛ menian'n-mɔ le man yɩ́ nɩn ati, Zozi a nyɔ man asinbɛnwʋn ninnge dɔʋn.

ref: When Jesus got out of the boat, he saw the large crowd. He felt sorry for them and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Mɔ Zozi fi ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun 'ju'n, ɔ 'tʋ yɩ́ nyɩn an, menian ngʋnmɩn. Bɛ́ nwʋn kɔ yɔ yɩ́ anwunnvoe, ɛsɛ ɔ kɔ man nwuluwafʋɛ'n-mɔ anwʋn kɔ sa.

ref: “Come on!” Jesus said. Peter then got out of the boat and started walking on the water toward him.
text: Mɔ Pɩɛlɩ hanlɩn sɔ'n, Zozi nwan: “Bala!” Pɩɛlɩ fi ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun ɔ kɔ ju, ɔ lɛ kpʋ Zozi.

ref: so much he swore to give her whatever she wanted.
text: dede ɔ kɔ han ndâ kɛ like biala m'ɔ kulo'n, sɛ ɔ bisa a, ɔ 'fa man yɩ́.

ref: and told his officials, “This is John the Baptist! He has come back from death, and that's why he has the power to work these miracles.”
text: ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele yɩ́ ngʋa'n-mɔ kɛ: “Zʋan Batisi dein y'ɔ fi ewue nun ɔ'a tinnge nɩn ɔ! Ɛhɩka ati yɛ̂ ɔ le tunmin ɔ yɔ asinbɛnwʋn ninnge ɔ.”

ref: Herod had earlier arrested John and had him chained and put in prison. He did this because John had told him, “It isn't right for you to take Herodias, the wife of your brother Philip.”
text: Afɩ Zʋan kɔ han kɔ hele belemgbin Elɔdɩ kɛ: “Nannan, Nyanmɩan mala'n mman man wɔ́ atɩn kɛ ɛ fá Elodiadɩ já.”

ref: But the girl's mother told her to say, “Here on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Talua'n kɔ li yɩ́ nin anʋan ɛjɔlɛ nɩn asʋ. Ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele belemgbin Elɔdɩ kɛ: “Baba, fa Zʋan ati'n tʋ apawa nun man mɩ́n kɩkaala!”

ref: It was taken on a serving plate to the girl, and she gave it to her mother.
text: Sʋanlan'n kɔ hɔ kɔ kpɛ Zʋan ati'n ɔ kɔ tʋ yɩ́ apawa nun kɔ bɛlɛ talua'n. Talua'n kʋsʋ kɔ hɔ kɔ fa kɔ man yɩ́ nin.

ref: John's followers took his body and buried it. Then they told Jesus what had happened. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Mark
text: Mɔ Zʋan menian'n-mɔ tɩlɩ yɩ́ sɔ'n, bɛ walɩ falɩ yɩ́ fuin'n bɛ hɔlɩ sielɩ yɩ́. Mɔ bɛ sielɩ yɩ́ yuelɩ'n, bɛ hɔlɩ bɔlɩ nun amannɩɛ bɛ helelɩ Zozi. Zozi 'ba man menian akpɩɩ nnun alɩɛ di (Maakɩ

ref: But when Peter saw how strong the wind was, he was afraid and started sinking. “Save me, Lord!” he shouted.
text: Nan m'ɔ nwunlin kɛ asutue'n tɩ kpili'n, esulo kɔ han yɩ́. Ɛsɛ kɛmɔ anɩn ɔ lɛ nʋan'n, ɔ kɔ tɩan. Yɩ́ nwan: “Yɛ́ Mɩn, de mɩ́n!”

ref: But they said, “We have only five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: Yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ nwan: “Kpaʋn nnun ala ɔ nʋn ejue nnyuan yɛ̂ yɛ le yɩ́ ɛwa ɔ.”

ref: After Jesus heard about John, he crossed Lake Galilee to go to some place where he could be alone. But the crowds found out and followed him on foot from the towns.
text: Mɔ Zozi tɩlɩ amannɩɛ sɔ'n, ɔ fʋlɩ ɛlɛɛ kʋn anun ɔ hɔlɩ lɩka kʋn. Lɩka sɔ'n, sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ nnʋn man ɛbɛlɛ. Nan mɔ menian'n-mɔ tɩlɩ yɩ́ sɔ'n, bɛ fi kulo kulo'n-mɔ asʋ bɛ kɔ fa asue nɩn anʋan atɩn'n bɛ kɔ si Zozi sʋ.

ref: Jesus replied, “They don't have to leave. Why don't you give them something to eat?”
text: Zozi kɔ bua bɛ kɛ: “Ɔ tɩ man cɩnnjɩn kɛ bɛ hɔ́. Nan ɛmɔ muonun mán bɛ́ like bɛ lí.”

ref: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
text: Zozi nwan: “Ɛmɔ fá bɛ bɛ́lɛ mɩ́n.”

ref: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.
text: M'ɔ hanlɩn sɔ yuelɩ'n, ɔ kɔ man meninsʋnman nɩn atɩn kɛ bɛ tɛ́tanlan ndile nɩn asʋ. Ɛhɩ anzin, ɔ kɔ fa kpaʋn nnun'n nʋn ejue nnyuan'n ɔ kɔ tʋ yɩ́ nyɩn anwunno ɔ kɔ la Nyanmɩan asɩ. M'ɔ lalɩ Nyanmɩan asɩ yuelɩ'n, ɔ kɔ bubu kpaʋn'n-mɔ anun ɔ kɔ fa kɔ man yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ. Yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kʋsʋ kɔ hye kɔ man meninsʋnman'n.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted, Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftovers.
text: Bɛ́ nun kʋn biala kɔ li dede yɩ́ kʋ kɔ yi. Kpaʋn'n m'ɔ halɩ'n, Zozi menian'n-mɔ kɔ sɩsa. Ɔ yi ece mma bulu nʋn nnyuan.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and start back across the lake. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Ɛhɩ, menian'n-mɔ yuelɩ like di ɛbɛlɛ ala a, Zozi kɔ man yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ fʋ ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun. Ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele bɛ́ kɛ bɛ kpɛ́ asue'n bɛ lí mʋa ebue kʋn ɛlɔ nán ɔ 'gua meninsʋnman nɩn atɩn.

ref: Then he went up on a mountain where he could be alone and pray. Later in the evening, he was still there.
text: M'ɔ gualɩ bɛ́ atɩn yuelɩ'n, ɔ kɔ fʋ bʋka kʋn asʋ, ɔ kɔ hɔ asɔnɩn ɛyɔlɛ ahanmɩan. Mɔ alɩ́ɛ sanlɩn'n, yɩ́ ngʋnmɩn cein yɛ̂ anɩn ɔ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ ɔ.

ref: By this time the boat was a long way from the shore. It was going against the wind and was being tossed around by the waves.
text: Ɔ 'ba yɔ sɔ'n, anɩn Zozi menian'n-mɔ ɛlɛɛ'n la bunman sʋ. Asutue'n bɔ mgbaha ɔ tu si ɛlɛɛ'n.

ref: A little while before morning, Jesus came walking on the water toward his disciples.
text: Mɔ ɔ 'kɔ alɩbahɩan'n, Zozi nanndɩ asue nɩn anyɩnsʋ ɔ lɛ kpʋ yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ.

ref: When they saw him, they thought he was a ghost. They were terrified and started screaming.
text: Mɔ bɛ nwunlin kɛ ɔ nanndɩ asue nɩn anyɩnsʋ'n, esulo kɔ han bɛ́. Bɛ́ nwan: “Ɛnwʋnmɩn aho! Ɛnwʋnmɩn aho!” Kɛmɔ anɩn bɛ sulo nɩn ati, bɛ lɛ tɛtɩan.

ref: At once, Jesus said to them, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Nan bɛ 'tɛtɩan nɩn ala, Zozi nwan: “Ɛmɔ sɔ́ bɛ́ ahʋnlɩn nán mɩ́n ɔ. Nán bɛ sulo!”

ref: Peter replied, “Lord, if it really is you, tell me to come to you on the water.”
text: Ɛhɩ a, Pɩɛlɩ nwan: “Yɛ́ Mɩn, sɛ wɔ́ sakpa a, man n nánndɩ asue nɩn anyɩnsʋ m gbʋ́ wɔ́.”

ref: At once, Jesus reached out his hand. He helped Peter up and said, “You surely don't have much faith. Why do you doubt?”
text: Ɛbɛlɛ ala, Zozi kɔ tʋ yɩ́ sa ɔ kɔ sɔ yɩ́ nun yɛ̂ yɩ́ nwan: “Pɩɛlɩ, wɔ́ dedi'n tɩ kaan o! Ɔ yɔ sɛ mɔ a nnyan man dedi'n?”

ref: When Jesus and Peter got into the boat, the wind died down.
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ yuelɩ'n, ɔ nʋn Pɩɛlɩ bɛ kɔ fʋ ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun yɛ̂ asutue'n gualɩ asɩ ɔ.

ref: The men in the boat worshiped Jesus and said, “You really are the Son of God!” Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Mark
text: Bɛ́ bʋ nga'n mɔ bɛ́ gua ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun'n kɔ a kɔ kʋtʋ kʋtʋ Zozi anyunnun ɛbɛlɛ. Bɛ́ nwan: “Fɔ́ʋ́n, Nyanmɩan Awa'n y'ɔ le wɔ́!” Zozi 'ba hɔ Zenezalɛtɩ etin nɩn asʋ (Maakɩ

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and came to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Bɛ kpɛlɩ asue'n nán b'a si asɩ Zenezalɛtɩ etin nɩn asʋ.

ref: The people found out he was there, and they sent word to everyone who lived in this part of the country. So they brought all the sick people to Jesus.
text: Ɛbɛlɛ menian'n-mɔ nwunlin Zozi nzɔlɛ. Bɛ hɔlɩ lɩka kʋalaatin bɛ kɔ hɔ kɔ bɔ kpayɛ kɛ Zozi a ju ɛbɛlɛ. Bɛ kɔ sʋa nwuluwafʋɛ'n-mɔ kʋalaatin bɛ kɔ bɛlɛ yɩ́.

ref: They begged him just to let them touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Bɛ kɔ sɛlɛ Zozi kɛ ɔ mán bɛ́ atɩn maan bɛ hán yɩ́ taladɩɛ nɩn anʋan nɩn ala. Bɛ́ kʋalaa mɔ bɛ hanlɩn yɩ́ taladɩɛ nɩn anʋan'n, bɛ́ nwʋn salɩ kpa bɔkɔɔ.

ref: Jesus answered, “Every plant that my Father in heaven did not plant will be pulled up by the roots.
text: Bɛ hanlɩn sɔ a, Zozi nwan: “Baka biala mɔ nán mɩ́n Sɩ'n m'ɔ wɔ anwunno'n yɛ̂ ɔ lualɩ'n, baka sɔ'n, bɛ 'tu yɩ́ bɛ tʋ.

ref: “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? They don't even wash their hands before they eat.”
text: “Yɛ́ nan-mɔ mala'n kele yɛ́ kɛ sɛ yɛ 'di like a, yɛ nwúnnzin yɛ́ sa nɩn anun. Nan nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ wɔ́ menian dɩɛ'n-mɔ nni man ɛjɔlɛ sɔ nɩn asʋ ɔ? Sɛ bɛ 'di like a, bɛ nnwunnzin man bɛ́ sa'n-mɔ anun.”

ref: Jesus answered: Why do you disobey God and follow your own teaching?
text: Zozi kɔ bua bɛ́ kɛ: “Nán ɛmɔ lɩlɩ, nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ ɛmɔ di bɛ́ muonun bɛ́ mala'n-mɔ asʋ bɛ yaci Nyanmɩan dɩɛ nɩn ɔ?

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they have has been offered to God.
text: Nan ɛmɔ dɩɛ, ɛmɔ kan kele menian'n-mɔ kɛ, sɛ sʋanlan kʋn se yɩ́ sɩ anaan yɩ́ nin kɛ: ‘Like'n m'ɔ di mɔ mɩn fa mɩn bʋka wɔ́'n, mɩɩn fa mɩn man Nyanmɩan’, anɩn sʋanlan sɔ'n, ɔ nzɩ man yɩ́ sɩ anaan yɩ́ nin ɛjɔlɛ nun kʋ́n.

ref: Is this any way to show respect to your parents? You ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching.
text: Kɛ ɛmɔ yɔ mɔ ɛmɔ bɔ Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ nɩn abʋsʋ nán ɛmɔ muonun a li bɛ́ ngehele nɩn asʋ nɩn anɩn.

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.” What Really Makes People Unclean (Mark
text: Bɛ́ asɔnɩn'n mɔ bɛ yɔ'n tɩ mgban. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ kulo menian mala yɛ̂ bɛ kekele ɔ, nan nán mɩ́n dɩɛ'n-mɔ ɔ.” Like m'ɔ gua sʋanlan anwʋn efian'n (Maakɩ

ref: Stay away from those Pharisees! They are like blind people leading other blind people, and all of them will fall into a ditch.”
text: Nán bɛ tɛ bɛ́ sʋ, nan bɛ tɩ kɛ anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ-mɔ! Sɛ anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ kʋn di yɩ́ manngʋn anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ taataa a, bɛ́ munnyuan bɛ 'gua kunman nun.”

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
text: Balasua sɔ'n hanlɩn sɔ a, Zozi ngɔ bua man ɛjɔlɛ kpɔlɛ kʋ́n. Yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ hyʋɩn kɔ kpunnge yɩ́, bɛ́ nwan: “Ɔ le ɛtɩan ɔ si yɛ́ sʋ. Yɩ́ ti, bua yɩ́ bie maan ɔ hɔ́.”

ref: The food you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
text: Nán like mɔ sʋanlan di'n y'ɔ gua yɩ́ nwʋn efian ɔ, nan ɛjɔlɛ m'ɔ fi yɩ́ nʋan kɔ fite'n y'ɔ gua yɩ́ nwʋn efian ɔ.”

ref: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
text: Ɛhɩ, yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ hyʋɩn kɔ kpunnge yɩ́, bɛ́ nwan: “Asʋ ɛ sɩ kɛ ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ a han nɩn a wʋlʋ Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ ɔ?”

ref: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
text: Pɩɛlɩ kukuli yɩ́ nʋan sʋ a, yɩ́ nwan: “Tu anyɩnndala ɛhɩ abʋ kele yɛ́.”

ref: Jesus then said: Don't any of you know by now what I am talking about?
text: Zozi nwan: “Ɛmɔ kʋsʋ ngʋala ndɩ man ɛjɔlɛ bʋ ɔ?

ref: Don't you know that the food you put into your mouth goes into your stomach and then out of your body?
text: Ɛmɔ nzɩ man kɛ like mɔ sʋanlan kɔ li'n, ɔ kɔ yɩ́ kunnun, ɔ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ dede ɛsɛ ɔ fite ɔ?

ref: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
text: Nan ɛjɔlɛ m'ɔ fi sʋanlan anʋan kɔ fite'n fi yɩ́ ahʋnlɩn nɩn anun. Yɩ́ y'ɔ gua yɩ́ nwʋn efian ɔ.

ref: Out of your heart come evil thoughts, murder, unfaithfulness in marriage, vulgar deeds, stealing, telling lies, and insulting others.
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ajʋnlɩn ɛtɛ m'ɔ man bɛ di awue, bɛ tʋn ajaa, bɛ bɔ danga, bɛ wua, bɛ di adanzɩyua ɔ nʋn menian anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ ɛtɛ ɛhan, ninnge sɔ'n fi bɛ́ ahʋnlɩn nun.

ref: These are what make you unclean. Eating without washing your hands will not make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Mark
text: Ɛhɩ y'ɔ gua sʋanlan anwʋn efian ɔ. Nan sɛ sʋanlan 'di like nan ɔ'a nnwunnzin man yɩ́ sa nun an, ɔ ngua man yɩ́ nwʋn efian.” Ɛyɛfʋɛ balasua kʋn de Zozi di. (Maakɩ

ref: Jesus said, “I was sent only to the people of Israel! They are like a flock of lost sheep.”
text: Mɔ bɛ hanlɩn sɔ'n, Zozi nwan: “Izalayɛ amma'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ tɩ kɛ mmʋa mɔ b'a minlin'n, bɛ́ ti yɛ̂ m malɩ ɔ.”

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: Meninsʋnman kɔ a yɩ́ nwʋn ɛbɛlɛ. Bɛ 'ba a, bɛ nʋn bɛ́ mɔ bɛ tia bɛ tɔ sʋ, anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ, amunlefʋɛ, bubuluwafʋɛ ɔ nʋn nwuluwafʋɛ dɔʋn kpa. Bɛ falɩ bɛ́ bɛ walɩ Zozi aja sʋ nán ɔ'a man bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ́ nwʋn a sa.

ref: Jesus left and went to the territory near the towns of Tyre and Sidon.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Zozi fi ɛbɛlɛ yɩ́ nʋan y'ɔ le kulo'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ fɛlɛ bɛ́ Tii nʋn Sidʋn etin dɩɛ nɩn asʋ.

ref: Suddenly a Canaanite woman from there came out shouting, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on me! My daughter is full of demons.”
text: Mɔ Zozi juli ɛbɛlɛ'n, Kanaân balasua kʋn m'ɔ tɩ mân sɔ nɩn anun'n, ɔ kɔ a Zozi anwʋn ɛbɛlɛ, ɔ kɔ tɩan nun. Yɩ́ nwan: “Yɛ́ Mɩn, belemgbin *Davidi Awa, nwun mɩ́n nwʋn anwunnvoe! Wawɛ ɛtɛ bie wɔ mɩ́n wa talua nɩn anun. Ɔ kele yɩ́ yalɛ sʋnman.”

ref: The woman came closer. Then she knelt down and begged, “Please help me, Lord!”
text: Nan m'ɔ hanlɩn sɔ'n, balasua'n kɔ a kɔ kʋtʋ yɩ́ nyunnun ɛbɛlɛ, yɩ́ nwan: “Yɛ́ Mɩn, nwun mɩ́n nwʋn anwunnvoe, de mɩ́n!”

ref: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
text: Zozi kɔ han kɔ hele yɩ́ kɛ: “Ɔ tɩ man kpa kɛ bɛ fa mmatʋnman'n-mɔ alɩɛ'n bɛ man njʋa mma'n-mɔ di ɔ.”

ref: “Lord, this is true,” the woman said, “but even puppies get the crumbs that fall from their owner's table.”
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ'n, balasua'n nwan: “Ɔ tɩ ananhɔlɛ, yɛ́ Mɩn. Nan kʋsʋ njʋa mma'n-mɔ sɩsa alɩɛ nɩn abʋ mgbʋkpʋlʋka'n m'ɔ kɔ gua bɛ́ mɩ́n'n-mɔ tɔbɩlɩ nɩn abʋ'n bɛ di.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi kɔ bua yɩ́ kɛ: “Balasua, wɔ́ dedi'n tɩ kpili! Mɔ ɛ lɛ kpʋnndɛ'n, ɔ yɔ́ sɔ man wɔ́.” Mɛlɛ sɔ nɩn ala, yɛ̂ yɩ wa balasua nɩn anwʋn salɩ ɔ. Zozi 'ba man nwuluwafʋɛ dɔʋn anwʋn sa

ref: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
text: Zozi fi ɛlɔ ɔ kɔ sa kɔ a Galile asue nɩn anʋan. Ɔ kɔ fʋ kɔ tanlan bʋka kʋn asʋ.

ref: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
text: Mɔ menian'n-mɔ nwunlin kɛ amunlefʋɛ'n-mɔ lɛ jʋjɔ, bubuluwafʋɛ'n-mɔ lɛ nanndɩ, bɛ́ mɔ bɛ tia bɛ tɔ sʋ'n, bɛ lɛ nanndɩ kpa, anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ'n-mɔ anyɩn'n-mɔ a tike tike'n, ɔ sinlin bɛ́ nwʋn, bɛ kɔ yɩ́ Izalayɛ Nyanmɩan nɩn ayɛ. Zozi 'ba man menian akpɩɩ nnan alɩɛ di (Maakɩ

ref: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
text: Zozi fɛlɛlɩ yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ, yɩ́ nwan: “Meninsʋnman ɛhɩ-mɔ nʋn yɛ wɔ ɛwa yɩ́ cɩan nsan ahɩ. Bɛ le man like fɩ́ɩ́ kʋ́n mɔ bɛ di ɔ. Bɛ́ nwʋn yɔ mɩ́n koun. Mɩn ngulo man kɛ mɩn yaci bɛ́ m man bɛ fa bɛ́ kunnun bɛ kɔ. Sɛ mɩn yɔ yɩ́ sɔ a, bɛ 'gɔlɔ atunnun.”

ref: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ'n, yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ bisa yɩ́ kɛ: “Ebo ɛhɩ anun, ninhan yɛ̂ yɛ 'nyan like m'ɔ sa menian nwala ɛhɩ di ɔ?”

ref: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
text: Zozi kɔ bisa bɛ́ kɛ: “Ɛmɔ le kpaʋn nyɛ?” Bɛ́ nwan: “Yɛ le kpaʋn nsʋ, nʋn ejue nganngan bie.”

ref: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi kɔ man meninsʋnman'n kɔ tɛtanlan asɩ.

ref: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
text: Mɔ bɛ tɛtanlanlɩn asɩ yuelɩ'n, ɔ kɔ fa kpaʋn nsʋ'n nʋn ejue'n-mɔ ɔ kɔ la Nyanmɩan asɩ. Ɔ kɔ bubu nun ɔ kɔ man yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ bɛ hyé bɛ mán bɛ́.

ref: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
text: Bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ kɔ li dede bɛ́ kʋ kɔ yi. Yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ sɩsa yɩ́ bʋ'n m'ɔ halɩ'n, ɔ tɩ ece mma nsʋ.

ref: After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into a boat and sailed across the lake. He came to shore near the town of Magadan.
text: M'ɔ gualɩ meninsʋnman nɩn atɩn yuelɩ'n, ɔ fʋlɩ ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun, ɔ hɔlɩ Magadan mân nɩn anun.

ref: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
text: Ɔ yɔ sɛ mɔ ɛmɔ ndɩ man yɩ́ bʋ kɛ nán kpaʋn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ yɛ̂ mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ'n? Nan mɩ́n nwan: Ɛmɔ nɩ́an bɛ́ nwʋn kpa wɔ *Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ nʋn *Sadusifʋɛ'n-mɔ fali ayile nɩn anwʋn.”

ref: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Zozi hɔlɩ Sezale etin dɩɛ nɩn asʋ Filipʋ mân nɩn anun ɛlɔ. M'ɔ nʋn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ juli ɛlɔ'n, ɔ kɔ bisa bɛ́ kɛ: “Menian'n-mɔ nwan sʋanlan benin y'ɔ le *Mân Baa'n?”

ref: Finally, the disciples understood that Jesus wasn't talking about the yeast used to make bread, but about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees. Who Is Jesus? (Mark
text: M'ɔ yɔlɩ sɔ'n, yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ tɩ yɩ́ bʋ kɛ nán ayile'n mɔ bɛ fa bɛ fɔtɔ fali nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ y'ɔ lɛ kan ɔ. Nan Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ nʋn Sadusifʋɛ'n-mɔ like'n mɔ bɛ kekele nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ yɛ̂ anɩn ɔ lɛ kan ɔ. Pɩɛlɩ 'ba han kɛ Zozi y'ɔ tɩ Izalayɛ Ngʋandefʋɛ nɩn ɔ (Maakɩ

ref: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
text: Mɔ bɛ hanlɩn sɔ'n, Zozi nwan: “Nɔsʋba mɔ nyanmɩan nɩn anyunnun kɔ bʋlʋ'n, anɩn ɛmɔ nwan eyua'n 'ba fi ɛhɩnman.

ref: But if the sky is red and gloomy in the morning, you say it is going to rain. You can tell what the weather will be like by looking at the sky. But you don't understand what is happening now.
text: Ngɛlɛmɔ kʋsʋ mɔ nyanmɩan nɩn anyunnun kɔ lu'n, anɩn ɛmɔ nwan esue'n 'ba tɔ. Ɛmɔ sɩ nyanmɩan nɩn anyunnun ninnge'n-mɔ nzɔlɛ, nan kʋsʋ ɛmɔ nzɩ man mɛlɛ'n mɔ yɛ wɔ nun'n nzɔlɛ.

ref: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
text: Kosuan sɔ'n mɔ Zozi bisalɩ nɩn anwʋn, bɛ́ nwan: “Mmie-mɔ nwan Zʋan Batisi y'ɔ le wɔ́, mmie-mɔ nwan *Eli y'ɔ le wɔ́, mmie-mɔ nwan Zolomin y'ɔ le wɔ́ anaan Nyanmɩan mgbɔmanfʋɛ'n-mɔ, bɛ́ bie y'ɔ le wɔ́.”

ref: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
text: Ɔ fi cɩan sɔ'n m'ɔ kɔ'n, Zozi kɔ bɔ yɩ́ bʋ, ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ: “Ɔ sɛ kɛ n gɔ́ Zoluzalɛmʋn. N gɔ́ n nwún yalɛ Zufʋ mgbain'n-mɔ, ɔ nʋn Nyanmɩan *tɛɛyɩfʋɛ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn *mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ asa nun. Bɛ 'ba hun mɩ́n, nan yɩ́ cɩan nsan nɩn asʋ, n vi ewue nun mɩn 'tinnge.”

ref: Jesus then warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
text: Zozi kɔ se bɛ́ kɛ: “Ɛmɔ nɩ́an bɛ́ nwʋn kpa wɔ *Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ nʋn *Sadusifʋɛ'n-mɔ fali ayile nɩn anwʋn.”

ref: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
text: Mɔ menian'n-mɔ tɩlɩ yɩ́ sɔ'n, bɛ kɔ han yɩ́ bɛ́ afian ɛlɔ kɛ: “Kpaʋn'n mɔ y'a nva man'n, yɩ́ ti y'ɔ man ɔ lɛ kan sɔ ɔ.”

ref: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
text: *Mân Baa'n fi yɩ́ Sɩ anunminnyanmʋn nɩn anun ɔ nʋn nyanmɩansʋ mmɔfʋɛ'n-mɔ 'ba a. Sɛ ɔ ba a, ɔ 'nɩan sʋanlan kʋn biala junman'n m'ɔ lili nɩn anwʋn ɔ tua yɩ́ kalɛ.

ref: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
text: Mɩ́n kʋsʋ mɩɩn kan mɩn kele wɔ́ kɛ ɛ li Pɩɛlɩ . Ɛbʋɛ sɔ nɩn asʋ yɛ̂ mɩn 'si mɩ́n Asɔnɩn nɩn ɔ. Ewue muonun tunmin'n m'ɔ le yɩ́'n ngʋala man like fɩ́ɩ́ yɔ yɩ́ nyunnun.

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
text: Zozi nwan: “Nan ɛmɔ lɩlɩ, ɛmɔ jʋnlɩn kɛ nwan sʋanlan dɩɛ y'ɔ le mɩ́n?”

ref: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
text: Simʋn Pɩɛlɩ nwan: “Kilisi'n, Izalayɛ Ngʋandefʋɛ'n y'ɔ le wɔ́. Ɛ tɩ Tɩasɩfʋɛ Nyanmɩan nɩn Awa.”

ref: Jesus told him: Simon, son of Jonah, you are blessed! You didn't discover this on your own. It was shown to you by my Father in heaven.
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi nwan: “Zonasɩ awa Simʋn, nyila hán wɔ́. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ nán kulo sʋanlan yɛ̂ ɔ'a man a nwun yɩ́ sɔ ɔ. Nan mɩ́n Sɩ m'ɔ wɔ anwunno ɛlɔ'n yɛ̂ ɔ'a man a nwun yɩ́ ɔ.

ref: I will give you the keys to the kingdom of heaven, and God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth. But he will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: Mɩn 'fa Anwunno Belemgbin Mân nɩn anʋan sannvɛ'n mɩn wula wɔ́ sa nun. Sʋanlan biala mɔ asɩɛ nɩn asʋ ɛwa ɛ kɔ tike anʋan'n kɔ man yɩ́'n, anɩn b'a tike b'a man yɩ́ anwunno ɛlɔ. Yɛ̂ sʋanlan biala kʋsʋ mɔ asɩɛ nɩn asʋ ɛwa a ndike man anʋan nɩn a mman man yɩ́'n, anɩn b'a ndike b'a mman man yɩ́ anwunno ɛlɔ.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples not to tell anyone he was the Messiah. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Mark
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ yuelɩ'n, ɔ totualɩ yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ nán bɛ kan bɛ kele sʋanlan kɛ Kilisi'n, Izalayɛ Ngʋandefʋɛ'n y'ɔ le yɩ́. Zozi 'ba han yɩ́ ewue'n nʋn yɩ́ etinnge nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ (Maakɩ

ref: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
text: Ɛhɩ, Pɩɛlɩ kɔ fa yɩ́ kɔ hɔ ahanmɩan, ɔ kɔ tu yɩ́ nyunnun. Yɩ́ nwan: “Yɛ́ Mɩn, Nyanmɩan kpála gúa! Like fɩ́ɩ́ ngɔ tʋ man wɔ́.”

ref: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Zozi kɔ han kɔ hele yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ: “Sʋanlan m'ɔ kulo kɛ ɔ si mɩ́n sʋ'n, n'ɔ susu yɩ́ sʋanlan baka nɩn anwʋn. Ɔ fá yɩ́ kʋlʋwa'n ɔ tʋ́ yɩ́ kɔmɩn sʋ, nán ɔ sí mɩ́n sʋ.

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ sʋanlan m'ɔ kulo kɛ ɔ kpʋnndɛ yɩ́ ngʋan'n, ɔ 'minlin yɩ́ ngʋan sɔ'n. Nan sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ si mɩ́n sʋ dede ɔ kɔ wu mɩ́n dunman nun'n, sʋanlan sɔ'n 'nyan ngʋan.

ref: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? What would you give to get back your soul?
text: Sɛ sʋanlan kʋn nyan asɩɛ asʋ ɛwa ninnge'n kʋalaa nan sɛ ɔ minlin yɩ́ ngʋan nɩn an, anɩn nzu nvasʋɛ dɩɛ yɛ̂ ɔ 'nyan yɩ́ sʋ ɔ? Nzu like dɩɛ yɛ̂ sʋanlan kʋn kʋala kɛ ɔ yɔ ɔ de yɩ́ ngʋan ɔ?

ref: I promise you some of those standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming with his kingdom.
text: Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ: bɛ́ mɔ b'a yia ɛwa'n, mmie-mɔ ngɔ wu man, bɛ 'ba nwun Mân Baa'n kɛ ɔ lɛ ba ɔ di belemgbin.”

ref: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
text: Ɛbɛlɛ, Zozi kɔ kaci bɛ́ nyɩnsʋ. Yɩ́ nyɩn nɩn anun kɔ ta kɛ sɛnzɛ, yɩ́ taladɩɛ'n m'ɔ wula'n kɔ yɔ fufue hyɩɛɩn hyɩɛɩn kpa kɛ kanlannɩɛ.

ref: Then the disciples understood Jesus was talking to them about John the Baptist. Jesus Heals a Boy (Mark
text: Yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ tɩlɩ yɩ́ bʋ kɛ Zʋan Batisi yɛ̂ anɩn ɔ lɛ kan yɩ́ nwʋn ɛjɔlɛ nɩn ɔ. Zozi 'ba tu kpafɛlɛ kʋn anwʋn wawɛ ɛtɛ'n (Maakɩ

ref: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin cɩan nsian, Zozi kɔ fa Pɩɛlɩ, Zʋakɩ nʋn yɩ́ nianman Zʋan. Ɔ nʋn bɛ́ menian nsan nɩn ala bɛ kɔ tɩ bɛ́ nwʋn bʋka kannganlaun kʋn asʋ.

ref: All at once Moses and Elijah were there talking with Jesus.
text: Zozi menian nsan'n 'nɩan an, anɩn Moyizɩ nʋn Eli nʋn Zozi bɛ lɛ jʋjɔ.

ref: So Peter said to him, “Lord, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
text: Mɔ Pɩɛlɩ nwunlin yɩ́ sɔ'n, ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele Zozi kɛ: “Yɛ́ Mɩn, ɔ tɩ kpa kɛ yɛ wɔ ɛwa. Sɛ ɛ kulo a, mɩn 'bɔ *tantɩ nsan. Wɔ́ dɩɛ kʋn, Moyizɩ dɩɛ kʋn yɛ̂ Eli kʋsʋ dɩɛ kʋn.”

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
text: Anɩn Pɩɛlɩ anʋan wɔ sʋ ɔ tɛ jʋjɔ, bɔlɛ́'n tɩ kɛ kanlannɩɛ, ɔ kɔ a kɔ hala bɛ́ sʋ. Bɔlɛ́ nɩn anun, anɩɛ kʋn kɔ te, yɩ́ nwan: “Sʋanlan ɛhɩ tɩ mɩ́n ehulo Baa. M'an fa mɩ́n lʋa'n kʋalaa m'an wula yɩ́ nun. Ɛmɔ tíe yɩ́!”

ref: When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid they fell flat on the ground.
text: Mɔ yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ tɩlɩ anɩɛ sɔ'n, esulo kpili kpa kɔ han bɛ́ dede bɛ kɔ butu butu bɛ́ nyɩn asɩ.

ref: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
text: Zozi kɔ a kɔ kpusu kpusu bɛ́, yɩ́ nwan: “Nán bɛ sulo, bɛ jásʋ.”

ref: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
text: Bɛ 'kuku bɛ́ ti sʋ a, sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ nnʋn man ɛbɛlɛ kɛ Zozi angʋnmɩn ala.

ref: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ yuelɩ'n, yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ bisa yɩ́ kɛ: “Ɔ yɔ sɛ mɔ *mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ kan kɛ Eli yɛ̂ ɔ 'di mʋa ba'n?”

ref: Jesus told them, “Elijah certainly will come and get everything ready.
text: Zozi nwan: “Ɔ tɩ sɔ, Eli 'di mʋa ba ɔ sesie ninnge'n kʋalaa kpɛ kpɛ.

ref: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
text: Nan kʋsʋ mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ Eli a a dada angʋnmɩn. Menian'n-mɔ a nnwun man yɩ́ nzɔlɛ, bɛ yɔlɩ yɩ́ kɛ bɛ́ kunnun kulo. Mân Baa'n muonun, kɛ bɛ 'ba hele yɩ́ yalɛ nɩn anɩn.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples returned to the crowd. A man knelt in front of him
text: Mɔ bɛ walɩ dede bɛ juli meninsʋnman nɩn anwʋn ɛbɛlɛ'n, bian kʋn kɔ a kɔ kʋtʋ Zozi anyunnun, yɩ́ nwan:

ref: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
text: “Yɛ́ Mɩ́n, nwun mɩ́n wa belenzua nɩn anwʋn anwunnvoe. Ɔ kpɛ, ɔ lɛ nwun yalɛ sʋnman, ɔ tɔ sɩ̂n nun, bie kʋsʋ a, ɔ tɔ asue nun.

ref: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
text: M'an fa yɩ́ m'an bɛlɛ wɔ́ menian'n-mɔ, nan b'a ngʋala man like fɩ́ɩ́ yɔ man yɩ́.”

ref: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
text: Zozi nwan: “Ati anun ɛtɛ mma mɔ bɛ nne man Nyanmɩan bɛ nni man, mɩn nʋn ɛmɔ yɛ tánlan ɛwa dede ɔ hɔ́ jú mɛlɛ benin nán ɛmɔ a nyan dedi? N díla mɩ́n ahʋnlɩn n zʋ́ ɛmɔ dede ɔ jú mɛlɛ benin? Bɛ fá batʋnman'n bɛ bɛ́lɛ mɩ́n ɛwa.”

ref: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
text: Bɛ kɔ fa batʋnman'n bɛ kɔ bɛlɛ Zozi. Ɔ kɔ jʋjɔ aminle sʋ ɔ kɔ hele wawɛ ɛtɛ'n. Ɔ fi yɩ́ nun kɔ fite. Ɛbɛlɛ ala, batʋnman nɩn anwʋn kɔ sa.

ref: Later the disciples went to Jesus in private and asked him, “Why couldn't we force out the demon?”
text: M'ɔ yɔlɩ sɔ'n, Zozi menian'n-mɔ kɔ fa bɛ́ ti bɛ kɔ bɔ yɩ́ nwʋn, bɛ́ nwan: “Nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ y'a ngʋala man wawɛ ɛtɛ ɛhɩ tu'n?”

ref: Jesus replied: It is because you don't have enough faith! But I can promise you this. If you had faith no larger than a mustard seed, you could tell this mountain to move from here to there. And it would. Everything would be possible for you. Jesus Again Speaks about His Death (Mark
text: Nan m'ɔ tɩ kɛ wawɛ ɛtɛ ɛhɩ sʋa'n, mmʋtʋɛ ɛyɔlɛ nʋn abuala yɛ̂ bɛ fa bɛ tu yɩ́ ɔ.”] Zozi 'ba bɔ yɩ́ ewue ɔ nʋn yɩ́ etinnge nɩn asʋ biekun (Maakɩ

ref: While Jesus and his disciples were going from place to place in Galilee, he told them, “The Son of Man will be handed over to people
text: Ɛhɩ, anɩn bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ wɔ Galile mân nɩn anun ɛlɔ. Ɛlɛhʋn kʋn Zozi kɔ fa kɔ tʋ bɛ́ nyunnun kɛ: “Bɛ 'ba yɩ *Mân Baa'n bɛ man menian'n-mɔ

ref: who will kill him. But three days later he will rise to life.” All of this made the disciples very sad. Paying the Temple Tax
text: maan bɛ kun yɩ́. Sɛ ɔ wu a, yɩ́ cɩan nsan nɩn asʋ, ɔ fi ewue nun ɔ 'tinnge.” Yɩ́ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n manlɩn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ anwʋn bubuli bɛ́ kpa. Asɔnɩn sua nɩn anwʋn ajule'n

ref: When Jesus and the others arrived in Capernaum, the collectors for the temple tax came to Peter and asked, “Does your teacher pay the temple tax?”
text: Mɔ bɛ juli Kapɛɛnawɔmʋn'n, bɛ́ mɔ bɛ dide asɔnɩn sua nɩn anwʋn ajule'n kɔ a kɔ tʋ Pɩɛlɩ, bɛ́ nwan: “Ɛmɔ amɩn'n muonun, ɔ ndua man asɔnɩn sua nɩn anwʋn ajule'n bie ɔ?”

ref: “Yes, he does,” Peter answered. After they had returned home, Jesus went up to Peter and asked him, “Simon, what do you think? Do the kings of this earth collect taxes and fees from their own people or from foreigners?”
text: Pɩɛlɩ nwan: “Ɔ tua bie.” Mɔ Pɩɛlɩ bualɩ bɛ́ yuelɩ'n, ɔ kɔ wʋlʋ sua nɩn anun. Ɔ nʋn mɔ ɔ 'wʋlʋ sua nɩn anun'n, Zozi kɔ li mʋa kɔ bisa yɩ́ kɛ: “Simʋn, ɔ tɩ sɛ wɔ́ ɛlɔ? Mân ɛhɩ anun, nwan-mɔ y'ɔ di kɛ bɛ tua ajule bɛ man mmelemgbin'n-mɔ ɔ? Bɛ́ mɔ bɛ le bɛ́ kulo nɩn naan, nyɔfʋɛ'n-mɔ ɔ?”

ref: Peter answered, “From foreigners.” Jesus replied, “Then their own people don't have to pay.
text: Pɩɛlɩ nwan: “Nyɔfʋɛ'n-mɔ yɛ̂ bɛ tua ɔ.” Ɛhɩ a, Zozi nwan: “Bɛ́ mɔ bɛ le bɛ́ kulo'n, bɛ ndua man bie.

ref: But we don't want to cause trouble. So go cast a line into the lake and pull out the first fish you hook. Open its mouth, and you will find a coin. Use it to pay your taxes and mine.”
text: Nan kʋsʋ kɛmɔ yɛ ngulo man kɛ yɛ dɔ bɛ́ ngasi'n, yɩ́ ti kɔ asue nɩn anʋan ɛlɔ kɔ tʋ kʋba. Ejue mɔ ɛ kɔ li mʋa kɔ hyɩ yɩ́'n, tɛlɛ yɩ́ nʋan nɩn anun. Ɛ 'ba nwun kɛ esika wɔ nun, ɔ sa mɩ́n nʋn wɔ́ yɛ́ ajule'n tua. Fa kɔ tua.”

ref: If your eye causes you to sin, poke it out and get rid of it. You would be better off to go into life with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fires of hell. The Lost Sheep (Luke
text: Sɛ wɔ́ nyɩn kʋn y'ɔ man ɛ tɔ ɛtɛ nɩn anun an, anɩn tu tʋ mʋa. Afɩ sɛ ɔ ka wɔ́ nyɩn kʋn nán ɛ nyan ngʋan nɩn an, ɔ tɩ kpa tala kɛ ɛ kɔ fa wɔ́ nyɩn nnyuan nɩn ɛ kɔ hɔ sɩ̂n'n m'ɔ nnun man lé nɩn anun'n. Bʋa'n mɔ ɔ'a minlin nɩn anwʋn anyɩnndala'n (Luku

ref: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
text: Kɛ n nwunlin wɔ́ nwʋn anwunnvoe'n, wɔ́ kʋsʋ ɔ di kɛ ɛ nwun wɔ́ manngʋn nɩn anwʋn anwunnvoe.’

ref: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
text: Sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ bɛbɛlɛ yɩ́ nwʋn asɩ dede ɔ kɔ kaci kɛ batʋnman ɛhɩ'n, yɩ́ y'ɔ tɩ kpili Anwunno Belemgbin Mân nɩn anun ɛlɔ ɔ.

ref: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark
text: Mɩ́n dunman nun, sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ le batʋnman kʋn m'ɔ tɩ kɛ ɛhɩ'n, anɩn mɩ́n muonun yɛ̂ ɔ'a le mɩ́n ɔ. Ɛtɛ ɛyɔlɛ'n (Maakɩ

ref: If the follower refuses to listen to them, report the matter to the church. Anyone who refuses to listen to the church must be treated like an unbeliever or a tax collector. Allowing and Not Allowing
text: Nan sɛ bɛ kan nán ɔ'a ndie man an, bɔ asɔnɩn mma'n-mɔ amannɩɛ. Sɛ bɛ́ kʋsʋ bɛ kan nán ɔ'a ndie man an, bu yɩ́ kɛ ɔ tɩ sʋanlan m'ɔ nzɩ man Nyanmɩan mɔ yɩ́ ti tɩ ɛtɛ kɛ Zufʋ m'ɔ dide ajule man Lɔmʋn amma'n-mɔ'n.

ref: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
text: “Mmatʋnman ɛhɩ-mɔ mɔ bɛ de mɩ́n bɛ di'n, sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ man bɛ́ nun kʋn kɔ tɔ ɛtɛ nɩn anun'n, sʋanlan sɔ'n, ɔ di kɛ bɛ fa ɛbʋɛ kpili kpa bɛ mɩnnda yɩ́ kɔmɩn bɛ ju yɩ́ bɛ tʋ jenvie nɩn anun.

ref: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, chop it off and throw it away! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the fire that never goes out.
text: Yɩ́ ti, sɛ wɔ́ sa kʋn anaan wɔ́ ja kʋn y'ɔ man ɛ tɔ ɛtɛ nɩn anun an, anɩn ko tʋ mʋa. Afɩ sɛ ɔ ka wɔ́ sa kʋn anaan wɔ́ ja kʋn nán ɛ nyan ngʋan nɩn an, ɔ tɩ kpa tala kɛ ɛ kɔ fa wɔ́ sa nnyuan'n, anaan wɔ́ ja nnyuan nɩn ɛ kɔ hɔ sɩ̂n'n m'ɔ nnun man lé nɩn anun'n.

ref: If one of my followers sins against you, go and point out what was wrong. But do it in private, just between the two of you. If that person listens, you have won back a follower.
text: “Sɛ wɔ́ nianman bie yɔ wɔ́ ɛtɛ a, kɔ tʋ yɩ́, fɛlɛ yɩ́ ahanmɩan kan kele yɩ́. Sɛ ɔ nwun yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ a, anɩn wɔ́ sa a han wɔ́ nianman'n.

ref: Don't be cruel to any of these little ones! I promise you their angels are always with my Father in heaven.
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ, Mân Baa'n walɩ kɛ ɔ 'ba kpʋnndɛ bɛ́ mɔ b'a minlin'n nán ɔ de bɛ́ ngʋan.]

ref: That's how it is with your Father in heaven. He doesn't want any of these little ones to be lost. When Someone Sins (Luke
text: Ɛmɔ Asɩ'n m'ɔ wɔ anwunno'n, kɛ ɔ tɩ yɩ́ nɩn ala anɩn. Ɔ ngulo man kɛ mmatʋnman ɛhɩ-mɔ bie minlin. Wɔ́ nianman kʋn yɔ wɔ́ ɛtɛ a, atɩn m'ɔ fata kɛ ɛ bu sʋ'n

ref: But if that one refuses to listen, take along one or two others. The Scriptures teach that every complaint must be proven true by two or more witnesses.
text: Sɛ ɛ kan nán ɔ'a ndɛ man wɔ́ sʋ a, fɛlɛ sʋanlan kʋn anaan menian nnyuan ɛmɔ hɔ́ sésie ɛjɔlɛ'n maan bɛ lí nun adanzɩɛ. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ menian nnyuan anaan menian nsan anʋan ɛjɔlɛ'n yɛ̂ bɛ bu yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ ɔ.

ref: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
text: “Ɛsɛ mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ, asɩɛ nɩn asʋ ɛwa, sɛ menian nnyuan anʋan sɛ bɛ́ nwʋn bɛ bisa like biala wɔ bɛ́ mmʋtʋɛ nɩn anun an, mɩ́n Sɩ m'ɔ wɔ anwunno ɛlɔ'n 'fa man bɛ́.

ref: Whenever two or three of you come together in my name, I am there with you. An Official Who Refused To Forgive
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ lɩka biala mɔ menian nnyuan anaan nsan kɔ yia wɔ mɩ́n dunman nun'n, n wɔ bɛ́ afian ɛbɛlɛ.” Akʋa'n mɔ ɔ'a nva man yɩ́ manngʋn nɩn anwʋn sa ɔ'a nje man yɩ́ nɩn anwʋn anyɩnndala'n

ref: This story will show you what the kingdom of heaven is like: One day a king decided to call in his officials and ask them to give an account of what they owed him.
text: “Ɛhɩ ati, ɛ 'nwun like mɔ Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n soman yɩ́'n. Belemgbin kʋn nwan ɔ 'dide yɩ́ esika'n kʋalaa mɔ yɩ́ ngʋa'n-mɔ tua yɩ́'n.

ref: As he was doing this, one official was brought in who owed him
text: Kɛ ɔ bɔlɩ yɩ́ bʋ mɔ ɔ 'tɩtɩ mgbʋndan'n, bɛ nʋn yɩ́ ngʋa'n-mɔ anun kʋn kɔ a, ɔ tua yɩ́ kotoku akpɩɩ kɔ yɔ nyɛ.

ref: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
text: Akʋa sɔ'n le man like fɩ́ɩ́ m'ɔ fa tua kalɛ nɩn ɔ. Yɩ́ mɩn'n kɔ man atɩn kɛ bɛ tɔ́nɩn yɩ́ nʋn yɩ́ yɩ, nʋn yɩ́ mma'n-mɔ nʋn bɛ́ nwʋn ninnge'n kʋalaatin bɛ fá bɛ túa kalɛ'n.

ref: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
text: “Mɔ yɩ́ mɩn'n hanlɩn sɔ'n, akʋa'n kɔ tu kɔ tɔ yɩ́ ja nun ɔ kɔ bʋtʋ yɩ́ nwʋn ɔ kɔ hele yɩ́ kɛ: ‘Mɩ́n mɩn, si abʋtalɛ, nán wɔ́ esika'n, m 'ba tua yɩ́ kʋalaa mɩn man wɔ́.’

ref: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
text: Akʋa'n hanlɩn ɛhɩ a, yɩ́ mɩn'n kɔ nwun yɩ́ nwʋn anwunnvoe, ɔ kɔ fa kalɛ'n ɔ kɔ hye yɩ́ ɔ kɔ yaci yɩ́ maan ɔ kɔ hɔ.

ref: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
text: “Yɩ́ manngʋn'n kɔ tu kɔ tɔ yɩ́ ja nun ɔ kɔ bʋtʋ yɩ́ nwʋn ɔ kɔ hele yɩ́ kɛ: ‘Anianman, si abʋtalɛ, nán wɔ́ esika'n, mɩn 'ba tua mɩn man wɔ́.’

ref: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
text: “Ɔ hanlɩn ɛhɩ a, akʋa'n ngɔ tie man, ɔ kɔ man bɛ kɔ hyɩ yɩ́ manngʋn'n bɛ kɔ tʋ yɩ́ fiadɩ dede ɔ kɔ ju cɩan mɔ ɔ kɔ tua yɩ́ kalɛ'n.

ref: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
text: Mɔ ngʋa ɛhɩnlɩn-mɔ nwunlin yɩ́ nyɔlɩɛ sɔ'n, bɛ́ nwʋn kɔ bubu bɛ́, bɛ kɔ hɔ kɔ han kɔ hele bɛ́ mɩn'n.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: “Nan mɔ mɩnlɩan'n tɩlɩ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n, ɔ kɔ man bɛ kɔ fɛlɛ akʋa sɔ'n, ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele yɩ́ kɛ: ‘Akʋa ati anun ɛtɛ! M'an fa wɔ́ kalɛ'n kʋalaa m'an hye wɔ́. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ a bʋtʋ wɔ́ nwʋn a hele mɩ́n.

ref: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
text: Belemgbin'n kɔ fɛ ɛya kpa, ɔ kɔ tʋ akʋa'n kʋsʋ fiadɩ dede ɔ kɔ ju cɩan m'ɔ kɔ tua yɩ́ kalɛ'n.”

ref: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
text: Mɔ Zozi yuelɩ yɩ́ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n-mɔ kan'n, ɔ fi Galile mân nɩn anun ɛbɛlɛ ɔ kɔ hɔ Zude mân nɩn anun, asue Zuludɩn nɩn anzin ɛlɔ ebue kʋn.

ref: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
text: Ninnge dɔʋn wɔ ɛbɛlɛ, ɔ jɩjɩ melenzua'n mmie-mɔ, ɔ man bɛ ngʋala man bala ja. Mmie-mɔ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ, bɛ 'wʋ bɛ́ a, anɩn bɛ́ nwʋn tɩ man kpa. Mmie-mɔ kʋsʋ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ, bɛ sa bɛ́. Mmie-mɔ kʋsʋ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ, Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n dunman nun, bɛ nja man bala. Sʋanlan m'ɔ sɩ fasie'n, ɔ fá síe!” (Maakɩ

ref: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
text: Meninsʋnman sili yɩ́ sʋ. Bɛ 'ba a, bɛ nʋn nwuluwafʋɛ. Zozi manlɩn bɛ́ nwuluwafʋɛ'n-mɔ anwʋn salɩ.

ref: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
text: Anɩn *Falisifʋɛ mmie-mɔ wɔ meninsʋnman nɩn anun. Bɛ kɔ kpunnge Zozi anwʋn ɛbɛlɛ. Kɛmɔ bɛ kulo kɛ bɛ sua yɩ́ ngaa nɩn ati, bɛ kɔ bisa yɩ́ kɛ: “Kpain, asʋ yɛ́ mala'n man atɩn kɛ belenzua kʋn kʋala kɛ ɔ sɩn atɩn ngacile biala asʋ ɔ yila yɩ́ yɩ ɔ?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
text: Bɛ bisalɩ sɔ a, Zozi nwan: “Asʋ ɛmɔ kɩnngalɩ man yɩ́ Ɛhɛlɛ Ɛjɔlɛ nɩn anun lé kɛ abʋbɔ nɩn anun, Bɔfʋɛ'n bɔlɩ belenzua nʋn balasua.

ref: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
text: M'ɔ bɔlɩ bɛ́ yuelɩ'n, yɩ́ nwan: Ɛhɩka ati, belenzua'n fi yɩ́ sɩ nʋn yɩ́ nin abʋ anwʋn 'ba jasʋ. Ɔ 'fa yɩ́ nwʋn bɔ yɩ́ yɩ, nán bɛ́ munnyuan'n bɛ yɔ sʋanlan munmuan kʋn.

ref: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
text: Yɩ́ sɔ ɛhɩ, anɩn bɛ tɩ man menian nnyuan kʋ́n, nan b'a yɔ sʋanlan munmuan kʋn. Like mɔ Nyanmɩan kɔ han kɔ bɔ nun'n, kulo sʋanlan n'ɔ tɩ nun.”

ref: The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why did Moses say a man could write out divorce papers and send his wife away?”
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ'n, Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ kɔ bisa yɩ́ kɛ: “Anɩn ɔ yɔ sɛ mɔ Moyizɩ manlɩn atɩn kɛ belenzua hɛ́lɛ eyila kalata ɔ mán yɩ́ yɩ nán ɔ yíla yɩ́'n?”

ref: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
text: Zozi nwan: “Moyizɩ manlɩn atɩn kɛ ɛmɔ yíla bɛ́ yɩ-mɔ. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ɛmɔ ati anun tɩ ɛtɛ. Nan abʋbɔ nɩn anun'n, anɩn ɔ tɩ man sɔ.

ref: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
text: Mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ sʋanlan mɔ yɩ́ yɩ a mmɔ man bian m'ɔ kɔ yila yɩ́ ɔ kɔ ja balasua fʋfɔlɛ'n, sʋanlan sɔ'n, anɩn ɔ'a tʋn ajaa.”

ref: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
text: Mɔ bɛ hanlɩn sɔ'n, Zozi nwan: “Nán menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa yɛ̂ bɛ tɩ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ nɩn abʋ ɔ. Nan bɛ́ mɔ Nyanmɩan a man bɛ́ yɩ́ nwʋn tunmin'n yɛ̂ bɛ kʋala bɛ tɩ yɩ́ bʋ ɔ.

ref: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ yuelɩ'n, menian mmie-mɔ falɩ mmatʋnman nganngan bɛ bɛlɛlɩ yɩ́ kɛ ɔ fá yɩ́ sa ɔ fúa bɛ́ nwʋn nán ɔ yéyila bɛ́ sʋ. Nan mɔ yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ nwunlin yɩ́ sɔ'n, bɛ jʋjɔlɩ bɛ́.

ref: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
text: Ɛhɩ, sʋanlan kʋn walɩ tʋlɩ Zozi, yɩ́ nwan: “Ngehelefʋɛ, nzu like dɩɛ kpa yɛ̂ n yɔ́ nán m'an nyan ngʋan m'ɔ le man ayuelɩɛ nɩn ɔ?”

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Kpafɛlɛ'n kɔ bisa Zozi kɛ: “Anɩn mala benin-mɔ?” Ɛhɩ a, Zozi kɔ bua yɩ́ kɛ: “N'ɛ kun sʋanlan, n'ɛ tʋn ajaa, n'ɛ wua, n'ɛ di ato ɛ tʋ wɔ́ manngʋn bie abʋ,

ref: After Jesus had placed his hands on the children, he left. A Rich Young Man (Mark
text: Zozi falɩ yɩ́ sa'n-mɔ fualɩ bɛ́ nwʋn yuelɩ, yɩ́ nzin y'ɔ fi ɛbɛlɛ ɔ hɔlɩ ɔ. Kpafɛlɛ esikafʋɛ nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n (Maakɩ

ref: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
text: M'ɔ hanlɩn sɔ'n, Zozi kɔ bisa yɩ́ kɛ: “Nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ ɛ lɛ bisa mɩ́n like m'ɔ tɩ kpa nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ ɔ? Nyanmɩan angʋnmɩn cein y'ɔ tɩ kpa ɔ. Sɛ ɛ kulo kɛ ɛ nyan ngʋan m'ɔ le man ayuelɩɛ nɩn an, anɩn di mala'n-mɔ asʋ.”

ref: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi nwan: “Nán bɛ totua mmatʋnman'n-mɔ atɩn, bɛ yáci bɛ́ maan bɛ bála mɩ́n nwʋn. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n, bɛ́ mɔ bɛ tɩ kɛ mmatʋnman ɛhɩ-mɔ'n y'ɔ tɩ bɛ́ dɩɛ ɔ.”

ref: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
text: Nan kʋsʋ, bɛ́ mɔ kɩkaala bɛ li mʋa'n, bɛ́ dɔʋn bɛ 'ba ha sian. Yɛ̂ bɛ́ dɔʋn kʋsʋ mɔ bɛ halɩ sian'n, bɛ 'ba li mʋa.

ref: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: man wɔ́ nyɩn sɔ́ wɔ́ sɩ nʋn wɔ́ nin, afɩ kulo wɔ́ manngʋn kɛ wɔ́ muonun wɔ́ nwʋn.”

ref: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ'n, kpafɛlɛ'n nwan: “M'an li mala ɛhɩ-mɔ kʋalaa asʋ. Nan nzukɛ like dɩɛ biekun y'ɔ sɛ kɛ n yɔ́ ɔ?”

ref: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
text: Zozi nwan: “Sɛ ɛ kulo kɛ ɛ yue kpa yɔ bɔkɔɔ a, anɩn kɔ tɔnɩn wɔ́ ninnge'n kʋalaa mɔ ɛ le yɩ́'n. Esika'n mɔ ɛ kɔ nyan yɩ́'n, fa man ehianfʋɛ'n-mɔ, nán ɛ 'ba nyan anwʋndɩɛ dɔʋn anwunno ɛlɔ. Sɛ ɛ yɔ yɩ́ sɔ yue a, bala nán si mɩ́n sʋ.”

ref: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
text: Kpafɛlɛ sɔ'n kʋsʋ a nyan yɩ́ nwʋn kpa. Nan m'ɔ tɩlɩ ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ Zozi hanlɩn helelɩ yɩ́'n, yɩ́ lʋa bɔlɩ yɩ́ nwʋn ɔ kɔ hɔ.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi kɔ han kɔ hele yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ: “Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ nyan yɩ́ nwʋn'n, Anwunno Belemgbin Mân nɩn anun ɛlɔ ɛwʋlʋɛ yɔ sɩ kpa man yɩ́.

ref: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
text: Ɛsɛ mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ biekun kɛ: ɔ yɔ sɩ man naan kpili'n mɔ bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ sʋamo'n kɛ ɔ wʋ́lʋ dɔlɔwa bɔɛ nun. Nan ɔ yɔ sɩ kpa man esikafʋɛ kʋn kɛ ɔ wʋ́lʋ Nyanmɩan Belemgbin Mân nɩn anun.”

ref: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
text: Mɔ yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ tɩlɩ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n, ɔ sinlin bɛ́ nwʋn kpa. Bɛ́ nwan: “Sɛ ɔ tɩ sɔ a, anɩn nwan sʋanlan dɩɛ yɛ̂ ɔ kʋala Anwunno Belemgbin Mân nɩn anun kɔ ɔ?”

ref: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
text: Zozi nɩanlɩn bɛ́ dede a, yɩ́ nwan: “Ɔ yɔ sɩ man menian'n-mɔ, nan ɔ nyɔ sɩ mman man Nyanmɩan.”

ref: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”
text: Ɛhɩ, Pɩɛlɩ kɔ jasʋ, yɩ́ nwan: “Yɛ́ mɔ y'a yaci yɛ́ ninnge'n kʋalaatin mɔ yɛ si wɔ́ sʋ'n, anɩn yɛ́ kʋsʋ yɛ́ dɩɛ tɩ sɛ?”

ref: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Zozi nwan: “Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ mɛlɛ mɔ *Mân Baa'n 'ba tanlan yɩ́ anunminnyanmʋn belemgbin bia nɩn asʋ mân fʋfɔlɛ nɩn anun'n, ɛmɔ menian bulu nʋn nnyuan'n mɔ ɛmɔ si mɩ́n sʋ'n, ɛmɔ 'ba tanlan belemgbin bia sʋ bɛ bua Izalayɛ nvilie bulu nʋn nnyuan'n ndɛɛ.

ref: All who have given up home or brothers and sisters or father and mother or children or land for me will be given
text: Ɛsɛ sʋanlan biala mɔ mɩ́n ti, ɔ kɔ yaci yɩ́ awulo, yɩ́ nianman mmelenzua anaan mmalasua, yɩ́ sɩ anaan yɩ́ nin, yɩ́ mma, anaan yɩ́ ebo, ɔ 'nyan yɩ́ nwʋn sʋ fâ ɛya, ɛsɛ ɔ 'nyan nyanmɩansʋ ngʋan m'ɔ le man ayuelɩɛ'n.

ref: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
text: “Ɛ 'nwun like mɔ Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n soman yɩ́'n: Ɛlɛhʋn kʋn mɩnlɩan kʋn kɔ jasʋ ngɛlɛmɔ tutun kpa, ɔ 'kɔ a kpʋnndɛ menian maan bɛ di junman yɩ́ divɩn ebo nɩn asʋ.

ref: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
text: Bɛ́ m'ɔ nwunlin bɛ́'n, ɔ nʋn bɛ́ kɔ li cɩan kʋn junman nɩn anwʋn gua. Y'ɔ nʋn bɛ́ hɔlɩ yɩ́ divɩn ebo nɩn asʋ ɔ.

ref: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
text: “Ngɛlɛmɔ dɔɛ ngʋanlan ɔ kɔ fite gua nɩn asʋ ɛlɔ biekun. Ɔ 'nɩan an, menian mmiekun-mɔ gugua gua nɩn asʋ, bɛ le man junman bɛ nni man.

ref: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
text: Ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele bɛ́ kɛ: ‘Ɛmɔ kʋsʋ hɔ́ lí junman mɩ́n ebo nɩn asʋ nán yɩ́ nwʋn ahatua m'ɔ sɛ fata'n, mɩ́n 'fa mɩn man ɛmɔ.’

ref: So they went. At noon and again about three in the afternoon he returned to the market. And each time he made the same agreement with others who were loafing around with nothing to do.
text: “Ɔ hanlɩn an, menian'n-mɔ kɔ tie bɛ kɔ hɔ junman nɩn elie yɩ́ ebo nɩn asʋ. Sɛnzɛjinlan nʋn nɔsʋba dɔɛ nsan, bian'n kɔ fite biekun, ɔ kɔ nwun menian mmiekun-mɔ nán ɔ'a fa bɛ́.

ref: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
text: Mɔ bɛ 'kɔ a bɔ nɔsʋba dɔɛ nnun'n, ɔ kɔ nwun mmiekun-mɔ gua nɩn asʋ, nán ɔ'a bisa bɛ́ kɛ: ‘Nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ ɛmɔ gua ɛwa mgban sʋa mɔ ɛmɔ nni man junman fɩ́ɩ́'n?’

ref: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
text: “Bɛ́ nwan: ‘Sʋanlan manlɩn man yɛ́ junman ti ɔ.’ “Bian'n nwan: ‘O, anɩn ɛmɔ hɔ́ lí junman mɩ́n divɩn ebo nɩn asʋ.’

ref: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
text: “Nɔsʋba mɔ bɛ kpɔnzʋlɩ'n, ɔ kɔ fɛlɛ kpain'n m'ɔ nɩan yɩ́ junman nɩn asʋ'n, yɩ́ nwan: ‘Fɛfɛlɛ kpafʋ'n-mɔ kʋalaatin nán tua bɛ́ esika'n man bɛ́. Ɛ 'tua, di mʋa bɔ yɩ́ bʋ bɛ́ mɔ n valɩ bɛ́ nɔsʋba nun'n dede nán ɔ'a tʋ bɛ́ mɔ n valɩ bɛ́ ngɛlɛmɔ nun'n.’

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: “Bɛ kɔ fɛfɛlɛ bɛ́ mɔ bɛ falɩ bɛ́ dɔɛ nnun'n, bɛ kɔ tua bɛ́ cɩan kʋn junman esika'n bɛ kɔ man bɛ́.

ref: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
text: Bɛ 'de bɛ́ esika nɩn an, anɩn bɛ lɛ jʋjɔ bian'n bɛ́ konvin abʋ.

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: Bɛ́ nwan: ‘Kpafʋ ɛhɩ-mɔ mɔ b'a li junman dɔhyʋɩlɩ kʋnmgba'n, esika'n mɔ a tua bɛ́'n nʋn yɛ́ dɩɛ'n sɛ, yɛ́ mɔ yɛ jin eyua heleman ɛhɩ anun y'a li junman dede alɩɛ nɩn a fa a san'n!’

ref: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
text: Fa wɔ́ esika'n nán kɔ. Cɩan kʋn junman esika'n mɔ mɩɩn fa mɩn man wɔ́'n, yɩ́ kʋnmgba'n yɛ̂ mɩn 'fa mɩn man bɛ́ mɔ n valɩ bɛ́ dɔɛ nnun nɩn ɔ.

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: Mɩ́n esika ɔ, kɛ mɩn kulo kɛ mɩn yɔ yɩ́'n yɛ̂ mɩn yɔ ɔ. Anaan kɛmɔ m'an yɔ sian mma'n-mɔ kpa'n, ɔ yɔ wɔ́ ɛya ɔ?’”

ref: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi kɔ han kɔ tʋ sʋ kɛ: “Bɛ́ mɔ bɛ li sian'n, bɛ 'di mʋa yɛ̂ bɛ́ mɔ bɛ li mʋa'n, bɛ 'di sian.” Zozi 'ba bɔ yɩ́ ewue'n nʋn yɩ́ etinnge nɩn asʋ biekun (Maakɩ

ref: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
text: Zozi kɔ jinlan ɔ kɔ fɛlɛ bɛ́, ɔ kɔ bisa bɛ́ kɛ: “Nzu yɛ̂ ɛmɔ kulo kɛ n yɔ́ m mán ɛmɔ ɔ?”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
text: Bɛ́ nwʋn kɔ yɔ Zozi koun. Ɔ kɔ fa yɩ́ sa'n kɔ fua bɛ́ nyɩn'n. Ɛbɛlɛ ala, bɛ kɔ nwun asɩ yɛ̂ bɛ sili yɩ́ sʋ ɔ.

ref: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Menian'n-mɔ kɔ totua bɛ́ kɛ bɛ múan bɛ́ nʋan. Bɛ 'totua bɛ́ a, anɩn bɛlɛ mɔ bɛ lɛ tɩan nun kpa, bɛ́ nwan: “Yɛ́ Mɩn, belemgbin Davidi Awa, nwun yɛ́ nwʋn anwunnvoe!”

ref: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he took his twelve disciples aside and told them in private:
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, mɔ Zozi 'kɔ Zoluzalɛmʋn'n, atunnun, ɔ kɔ fɛlɛ yɩ́ menian bulu nʋn nnyuan nɩn ahanmɩan, ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele bɛ́ kɛ:

ref: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark
text: Bɛ 'fa yɩ́ bɛ man bɛ́ mɔ bɛ tɩ man Zufʋ'n maan bɛ gʋlʋ yɩ́ nwʋn, bɛ fin yɩ́ mgbele bɛ bʋbɔ yɩ́ kʋlʋwa nɩn asʋ. Ɔ wu a, yɩ́ cɩan nsan nɩn asʋ, ɔ 'tinnge.” Zʋakɩ nʋn Zʋan anin'n 'ba sɛlɛ Zozi like (Maakɩ

ref: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
text: Ɛhɩ, Zebede amma'n-mɔ anin nʋn bɛ́ walɩ Zozi anwʋn ɛbɛlɛ. Ɔ kɔ kʋtʋ yɩ́ nyunnun ɔ kɔ sɛlɛ yɩ́ kɛ ɔ yɔ́ like kʋn mán yɩ́.

ref: Jesus asked her what she wanted, and she said, “When you come into your kingdom, please let one of my sons sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
text: Zozi kɔ bisa yɩ́ kɛ: “Nzukɛ yɛ̂ ɛ kulo kɛ n yɔ́ m mán wɔ́ ɔ?” Balasua'n nwan: “Mɩ́n mma nnyuan ɛhɩ-mɔ mɔ bɛ wɔ ɛwa'n, cɩan mɔ ɛ kɔ kaci belemgbin'n, man kʋn tánlan wɔ́ sa fama sʋ, man kʋn'n kʋsʋ tánlan wɔ́ sa bɛ̂ sʋ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Not one of you knows what you are asking. Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from?” James and John said, “Yes, we are!”
text: Mɔ balasua'n hanlɩn ɛhɩ'n, Zozi nwan: “Like'n mɔ ɛmɔ lɛ kpʋnndɛ'n, ɛmɔ nzɩ man. Asʋ kangɔ'n mɔ mɩn 'ba nʋn nun'n, ɛmɔ kɔ hʋala nun nʋn ɔ?” Bɛ́ nwan: “Yuo, yɛ kɔ hʋala nun nʋn.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
text: Zozi nwan: “Ɔ tɩ ananhɔlɛ kɛ ɛmɔ 'ba nʋn mɩ́n kangɔ nɩn anun, nan kʋsʋ m'ɔ tɩ mɩ́n fama sʋ, anaan mɩ́n bɛ̂ sʋ ɛtanlan'n, nán mɩ́n yɛ̂ mɩn fa menian'n-mɔ mɩn sie ɛbɛlɛ ɔ. Nan bɛ́ mɔ mɩ́n Sɩ a sesie a man bɛ́'n yɛ̂ ɔ 'fa bɛ́ sie ɛbɛlɛ ɔ.”

ref: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with the two brothers.
text: Mɔ yɩ́ menian bulu ɛhɩnlɩn-mɔ tɩlɩ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n, bɛ fɛlɩ anianman nnyuan nɩn anwʋn ɛya.

ref: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know foreign rulers like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over everyone they rule.
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi kɔ fɛlɛ bɛ́ kʋalaatin ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele bɛ́ kɛ: “Ɛmɔ sɩ kɛ mân nɩn anun mmelemgbin'n-mɔ, ɔ nʋn bɛ́ mgbain'n-mɔ, bɛ kele bɛ́ menian'n-mɔ yalɛ bɛ sisi bɛ́.

ref: But don't act like them. If you want to be great, you must be the servant of all the others.
text: Nan ɛmɔ afian ɛwa dɩɛ, ɔ tɩ man sɔ. Ɛmɔ afian, m'ɔ kulo kɛ ɔ kaci kpain'n, ɔ káci ɛmɔ akʋa.

ref: And if you want to be first, you must be the slave of the rest.
text: Ɛmɔ nunhan m'ɔ kulo kɛ ɔ di ɛmɔ anyunnun'n, ɔ yɔ́ ɛmɔ kanga.

ref: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men (Mark
text: Ɛhɩ ati, *Mân Baa'n, ɔ'a mma man kɛ ɔ 'ba maan bɛ sʋ yɩ́, nan yɩ́ muonun ɔ walɩ menian'n-mɔ ɛsʋ́ɛ nán ɔ'a fa yɩ́ ngʋan'n ɔ'a le menian dɔʋn ati.” Zozi 'ba man anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ nnyuan nwun asɩ (Maakɩ

ref: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
text: Mɔ bɛ fi Zeliko kulo nɩn anun bɛ 'fite'n, menian dɔʋn kpa sili bɛ́ sʋ.

ref: Two blind men were sitting beside the road. And when they heard that Jesus was coming their way, they shouted, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Anɩn anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ nnyuan gua atɩn nɩn anʋan ɛbɛlɛ. Mɔ bɛ tɩlɩ kɛ Zozi lɛ sɩn'n, bɛ kɔ tɩan nun, bɛ́ nwan: “Yɛ́ Mɩn, belemgbin *Davidi Awa, nwun yɛ́ nwʋn anwunnvoe!”

ref: They answered, “Lord, we want to see!”
text: Bɛ́ nwan: “Yɛ́ Mɩn, yɔ maan yɛ nwún asɩ.”

ref: Jesus said: I will tell you a story about a man who had two sons. Then you can tell me what you think. The father went to the older son and said, “Go work in the vineyard today!”
text: Ɛsɛ Zozi nwan: “Ɛmɔ tíe. Anɩn bian kʋn le yɩ́ mma nnyuan. Cɩan kʋn, ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele nunhan kpain'n kɛ: ‘Mɩ́n wa, ɛnɛ, kɔ divɩn ebo nɩn asʋ ɛlɔ kɔ li junman.’

ref: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
text: “Nan mɛlɛ mɔ bɛ nwunlin bian nɩn awa'n, bɛ́ nwan: ‘Bɛ nɩ́an, bian ɛhɩ m'ɔ lɛ ba'n, cɩan bie dede yɩ́ dein yɛ̂ ɔ 'di ajâ'n ɔ fa ebo nɩn ɔ. Yɩ́ ti, bɛ mán yɛ hún yɩ́ yɛ fá ebo'n.’

ref: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
text: Sɛ sʋanlan bie bisa ɛmɔ ɛjɔlɛ a, ɛmɔ hán kɛ: ‘Yɛ́ Mɩn a hian yɩ́ nwʋn.’ Ɛmɔ kan sɔ a, bɛ 'ba yaci ɛmɔ maan bɛ fa bɛ́ bɛ ba.”

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Ɔ man ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ'n hanlɩn'n kpɩnlɩn sʋ:

ref: “Announce to the people of Jerusalem: ‘Your king is coming to you! He is humble and rides on a donkey. He comes on the colt of a donkey.’ ”
text: Nyanmɩan nwan: Kan kele Siʋn amma'n-mɔ, ɛmɔ nɩ́an, ɛmɔ belemgbin'n tɩ afunlunmun sʋ. Yɩ́ ahʋnlɩn la asɩ ɔ lɛ ba ɛmɔ anwʋn ɛlɔ ɔ tɩ afunlunmun baa kʋn asʋ.

ref: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
text: Mɔ Zozi manlɩn bɛ́ ngalɩɛ sɔ'n yuelɩ'n, yɛ̂ yɩ́ menian nnyuan'n hɔlɩ ɔ. Like'n mɔ Zozi helelɩ bɛ́ kɛ bɛ yɔ́'n, bɛ yɔlɩ.

ref: They brought the donkey and its colt and laid some clothes on their backs. Then Jesus got on.
text: Bɛ kɔ fa afunlunmun'n nʋn yɩ́ wa'n bɛ kɔ a. Bɛ kɔ yɩyɩ bɛ́ taladɩɛ'n-mɔ bɛ kɔ gua bɛ́ sʋ, yɛ̂ Zozi fʋlɩ tanlanlɩn afunlunmun baa nɩn asʋ ɔ.

ref: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”
text: Mɔ Zozi 'wʋlʋ Zoluzalɛmʋn kulo nɩn anun'n, kulo bɔndɩn'n kɔ kpusu. Menian'n-mɔ kɔ bisa kɛ: “Nwan sʋanlan dɩɛ ɔ?”

ref: But the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were angry when they saw his miracles and heard the children shouting praises to the Son of David.
text: Nan mɔ Nyanmɩan *tɛɛyɩfʋɛ'n-mɔ, ɔ nʋn *mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ nwunlin asinbɛnwʋn ninnge'n mɔ Zozi yɔlɩ'n, ɔ nʋn mmatʋnman'n-mɔ mɔ anɩn bɛ lɛ tɩan Nyanmɩan awulo nɩn anun bɛ́ kan kɛ: “Anunminnyanmʋn hán belemgbin *Davidi Awa'n!”, Zozi anwʋn kɔ yɔ bɛ́ ɛya.

ref: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
text: Mɔ Zozi buli anyɩnndala sɔ'n, Nyanmɩan *tɛɛyɩfʋɛ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn *Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ nwunlin kɛ bɛ́ nwʋn ɛjɔlɛ yɛ̂ Zozi lɛ kan ɔ.

ref: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship.’ But you have turned it into a place where robbers hide.”
text: M'ɔ hanlɩn bɛ́ ninnge'n-mɔ gualɩ yuelɩ'n, yɩ́ nwan: “Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ'n lɛ kele kɛ: Bɛ 'ba fɛlɛ mɩ́n sua'n mmʋtʋɛ sua. Nan ɛmɔ dɩɛ, ɛmɔ a fa a kaci awue sua!”

ref: If you have faith when you pray, you will be given whatever you ask for.” A Question about Jesus' Authority (Mark
text: Sɛ ɛmɔ le dedi a, like biala mɔ ɛmɔ kɔ bisa yɩ́ mmʋtʋɛ nɩn anun'n, ɛmɔ asa 'kan yɩ́.” Zozi tunmin'n m'ɔ le yɩ́'n (Maakɩ

ref: The men said to Jesus, “Don't you hear what those children are saying?” “Yes, I do!” Jesus answered. “Don't you know that the Scriptures say, ‘Children and infants will sing praises’?”
text: Ɛhɩ, bɛ kɔ bisa Zozi kɛ: “Asʋ ɛ tɩ ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ bɛ lɛ kan'n?” Zozi nwan: “Yuo o. Ɛhɛlɛ Ɛjɔlɛ nwan: A fa ayɛ ɛyɩɛ nɩn a wula mmatʋnman'n-mɔ nʋn adɔman'n-mɔ anʋan. Ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n-mɔ, asʋ ɛmɔ kɩnngalɩ man yɩ́ lé ɔ?”

ref: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
text: M'ɔ hanlɩn sɔ yuelɩ'n, ɔ kɔ yaci bɛ́ ɛbɛlɛ ɔ fi kulo nɩn anun ɔ kɔ fite ɔ kɔ hɔ kɔ la Betanin kulo nɩn asʋ. Zozi 'ba bɔ figi baka kʋn sasan (Maakɩ

ref: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
text: Zozi nwan: “Mɩn 'bisa ɛmɔ kosuan kʋn. Sɛ ɛmɔ tu yɩ́ nʋan an, mɩ́n kʋsʋ mɩn 'kele ɛmɔ sʋanlan'n m'ɔ man mɩ́n tunmin maan mɩn yɔ ninnge sʋa-mɔ'n.

ref: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
text: Alɩ́ɛ hɩnlɩn mɔ Zozi 'sa ba Zoluzalɛmʋn'n, ɛhɔɛ kɔ hun yɩ́.

ref: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
text: Zozi nwan: “Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ sɛ ɛmɔ le dedi mɔ ɛmɔ dedi'n jin yɩ́ ja nun'n, mɔ m'an yɔ figi baka ɛhɩ'n, ɛmɔ 'ba hʋala yɩ́ yɔ, ɛsɛ bɔbɔ sɛ ɛmɔ se bʋka ɛhɩ kɛ: ‘Tu wɔ́ bʋ ɛwa, tutu kɔ tɔ jenvie nɩn anun’ an, ɔ 'kɔ.

ref: So they told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus said, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” A Story about Two Sons
text: Ɛhɩ a, bɛ kɔ bua Zozi kɛ: “Yɛ nzɩ man.” Zozi kʋsʋ nwan: “Sɛ ɛmɔ nzɩ man an, mɩ́n kʋsʋ mɩn ngan man sʋanlan'n m'ɔ man mɩ́n tunmin maan mɩn yɔ ninnge sɔ'n-mɔ'n mɩn ngele man ɛmɔ.” Mmatʋnman nnyuan nɩn anwʋn anyɩnndala'n

ref: Jesus told the chief priests and leaders to listen to this story: A land owner once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
text: “Ɛmɔ tíe anyɩnndala kʋn biekun. Anɩn bian kʋn le yɩ́ divɩn ebo. Ɔ kɔ le yɩ́ nwʋn. Ɔ kɔ fun kunman kʋn m'ɔ hyuhyuin yɩ́ nzan'n gua nun ɔ. Ɔ kɔ si sua hyilili kʋn sʋ. Sua sɔ'n tɩ kannganlaun. Bɛ fʋ sʋ bɛ sisa ebo'n. M'ɔ yuelɩ'n, ɔ kɔ fa ebo'n kɔ wula kpafʋ-mɔ asa nun. Yɛ̂ ɔ hɔlɩ yɩ́ atɩn ɔ.

ref: Jesus had gone into the temple and was teaching when the chief priests and the leaders of the people came up to him. They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
text: Zozi kɔ wʋlʋ Nyanmɩan awulo nɩn anun, ɔ kɔ hehele menian'n-mɔ Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ'n. Nyanmɩan *tɛɛyɩfʋɛ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn Zufʋ mgbain'n-mɔ kpʋlɩ Zozi, bɛ́ nwan: “Tunmin benin yɛ̂ ɛ le yɩ́ ɛ yɔ ninnge sʋa-mɔ ɔ? Nwan sʋanlan dɩɛ y'ɔ manlɩn wɔ́ yɩ́ nwʋn atɩn ɔ?”

ref: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?” They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
text: Nyanmɩan y'ɔ sʋanlɩn Zʋan kɛ ɔ sɔ́nɩn menian'n-mɔ ɔ, anaan kulo menian'n-mɔ yɛ̂ bɛ sʋanlɩn yɩ́ ɔ?” Mɔ Zozi bisalɩ bɛ́ kosuan sɔ'n, akpʋlʋwa tɔlɩ bɛ́ afian ɛlɔ. Bɛ́ nwan: “Sɛ yɛ kan kɛ Nyanmɩan yɛ̂ ɔ sʋanlɩn yɩ́ a, ɔ 'ba bisa yɛ́ kɛ: ‘Nan nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ ɛmɔ a nne man yɩ́ b'a nni man'n?’

ref: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
text: Nan sɛ yɛ kan kɛ: ‘Menian'n-mɔ yɛ̂ bɛ sʋanlan yɩ́’ a, meninsʋnman'n 'fɛ yɛ́ nwʋn ɛya. Afɩ bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ de bɛ di kɛ Zʋan tɩ Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ.”

ref: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
text: “Batʋnman'n tua se yɩ́ sɩ kɛ: ‘Mɩn ngɔ man.’ Ɔ tɩ ɛbɛlɛ dede yɩ́ nzin hyɛlɩ, ɔ kɔ jasʋ ɔ kɔ hɔ ebo nɩn asʋ.

ref: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.
text: “Sɩɛ'n kɔ han ɛjɔlɛ kʋnmgba sɔ'n ɔ kɔ hele yɩ́ wa kutua'n. Ɔ hanlɩn an, yɩ́ wa'n nwan: ‘Baba, m'an tɩ, mɩn 'kɔ.’ Baa'n ngɔ hɔ man ebo nɩn asʋ.

ref: Which one of the sons obeyed his father? “The older one,” the chief priests and leaders answered. Then Jesus told them: You can be sure tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you ever will!
text: “Anɩn mmatʋnman nnyuan'n, benin y'ɔ yɔlɩ yɩ́ sɩ ehulo like ɔ?” Bɛ́ nwan: “Nunhan kpain nɩn ɔ.” Ɛhɩ, Zozi nwan: “Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ, bɛ́ mɔ bɛ dide ajule'n, ɔ nʋn tutu'n-mɔ, bɛ 'di mʋa ɛmɔ anyunnun Nyanmɩan Belemgbin Mân nɩn anun ɛlɔ.

ref: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ Zʋan walɩ, ɔ helelɩ ɛmɔ atɩn kpa'n, nan ɛmɔ a nva man sʋ. Nan bɛ́ mɔ bɛ dide ajule'n, ɔ nʋn tutu'n-mɔ, bɛ́ dɩɛ, bɛ lelɩ yɩ́ bɛ lili. Ɛmɔ a nwun kɛ menian sɔ'n-mɔ kʋalaa a le yɩ́ a li. Nan ɛmɔ dɩɛ, ɛmɔ a nnun man bɛ́ nwʋn b'a ngaci man bɛ́ abalabɔ'n, b'a nne man yɩ́ b'a nni man.” Divɩn ebo nɩn anwʋn anyɩnndala'n (Maakɩ

ref: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
text: Ɔ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ dede mɔ divɩn nɩn ɛtɩɛ'n tɛmʋn juli'n, bian'n kɔ sʋan yɩ́ ngʋa'n-mɔ kɛ bɛ hɔ́ kpafʋ'n-mɔ anwʋn ɛlɔ, bɛ hɔ́ lé yɩ́ dɩɛ'n bɛ bɛ́lɛ yɩ́.

ref: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
text: “Kpafʋ'n-mɔ kɔ hyɩ bian'n ngʋa'n-mɔ bɛ kɔ bʋ kʋn, bɛ kɔ hun kʋn, bɛ kɔ tʋtʋ nunhan kʋn kʋsʋ ɛbʋɛ bɛ kɔ hun yɩ́.

ref: He then sent more servants than he did the first time. But the renters treated them in the same way.
text: Ɛsɛ bian'n kɔ sʋan yɩ́ ngʋa'n mmiekun-mɔ dɔʋn bɛ talalɩ alimʋa dɩɛ'n-mɔ. Ɛsɛ kpafʋ-mɔ kɔ yɔ bɛ́ kɛ bɛ yɔlɩ ɛhɩnlɩn-mɔ'n.

ref: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
text: Sian dɩɛ, bian'n kɔ sʋan yɩ́ muonun yɩ́ wa'n bɛ́ nwʋn ɛlɔ. Yɩ́ nwan: ‘Kɛmɔ ɔ tɩ mɩ́n kunnun baa'n, mɩn de mɩn di kɛ bɛ 'ba man yɩ́ anyɩnnzɔ.’

ref: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
text: Ɛhɩ a, bɛ kɔ hyɩ bian nɩn awa'n bɛ nʋn yɩ́ kɔ hɔ ebo nɩn anʋan ɛlɔ bɛ kɔ hun yɩ́.

ref: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
text: “Nan sɛ bian'n m'ɔ le ebo'n ba a, kpafʋ sɔ'n-mɔ, nzu yɛ̂ ɛmɔ de di kɛ ɔ 'yɔ bɛ́ ɔ?”

ref: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
text: Bɛ́ nwan: “Ɔ nnwun man bɛ́ nwʋn anwunnvoe, ɔ 'kun kpafʋ ati anun ɛtɛ sɔ'n-mɔ ɔ fa yɩ́ ebo'n wula menian nvʋfɔlɛ asa nun. Tɛmʋn'n ju nán bɛ 'tɩ ebo nɩn an, anɩn bɛ yɩ yɩ́ dɩɛ bɛ bɛlɛ yɩ́.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
text: Zozi nwan: “Ɛhɛlɛ Ɛjɔlɛ nɩn anun ɛjɔlɛ kpɔlɛ ɛhɩ, asʋ ɛmɔ kɩnngalɩ man yɩ́ lé ɔ? Ɛbʋɛ'n mɔ bɛ́ mɔ bɛ lɛ si sua nɩn a ju a tʋ'n, yɩ́ yɛ̂ ɔ'a kaci ayialɩɛ nɩn asʋ ɛbʋɛ cɩnnjɩn kpa nɩn ɔ. Like mɔ yɛ́ Mɩn a yɔ nɩn anɩn. Ɔ tɩ ɛnyɩnmɩan like wɔ yɛ́ nyɩnsʋ!”

ref: I tell you God's kingdom will be taken from you and given to people who will do what he demands.
text: Zozi kɔ han kɔ tʋ sʋ kɛ: “Ɛhɩ ati, mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ Nyanmɩan Belemgbin Mân'n, bɛ 'tʋa ɛmɔ bɛ de bɛ man menian mɔ b'a nun bɛ́ nwʋn mɔ bɛ bɔ bala m'ɔ sɔ Nyanmɩan anyɩn'n. [

ref: Anyone who stumbles over this stone will be crushed, and anyone it falls on will be smashed to pieces.”
text: Sʋanlan biala m'ɔ kɔ tɔ ɛbʋɛ sɔ nɩn asʋ'n, yɩ́ nun 'tɩtɩ. Sʋanlan kʋsʋ mɔ ɛbʋɛ sɔ'n kɔ tɔ yɩ́ sʋ'n, ɔ 'nunkun yɩ́.”]

ref: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
text: Ɛhɩ ati, bɛ kpʋnndɛlɩ atɩn mɔ bɛ 'sɩn sʋ bɛ cɩ yɩ́. Nan kʋsʋ, meninsʋnman'n dunman nun, bɛ sulo, bɛ kɔ yaci bɛ kɔ hɔ. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ menian'n-mɔ bu Zozi kɛ Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ.

ref: Many are invited, but only a few are chosen. Paying Taxes (Mark
text: Zozi kɔ han kɔ tʋ sʋ kɛ: “B'a fɛlɛ menian dɔʋn, nan kʋsʋ kaan bie yɛ̂ bɛ 'fa bɛ́ ɔ.” Sezaa ajule'n (Maakɩ

ref: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
text: Ɛhɩ, *Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ hɔlɩ yialɩ bɛ kpʋnndɛlɩ kosuan mɔ sɛ bɛ bisa Zozi a, ɔ kʋala kɛ ɔ fʋn ɔ tɔ bɛ́ ngaa nɩn anun'n.

ref: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
text: “Kpain, ɛ 'nwun ɛjɔlɛ mɔ Moyizɩ a han'n: ‘Sɛ belenzua kʋn ja bala nán ɔ nʋn yɩ́ b'a nwʋ man baa fɩ́ɩ́ dede nán belenzua'n wu a, yɩ́ nianman'n di kɛ ɔ ja bala'n, nán ɔ nʋn yɩ́ wʋ baa bɛ man yɩ́ nianman'n mɔ ɔ'a wu'n.’

ref: Once again Jesus used stories to teach the people:
text: Zozi buli anyɩnndala mmiekun-mɔ dɔʋn ɔ helelɩ bɛ́. Yɩ́ nwan:

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
text: “Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n tɩ kɛ belemgbin kʋn m'ɔ lɛ man yɩ́ wa belenzua kʋn fa yɩ́ yɩ atɔnvɔlɛ ɔ.

ref: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
text: Ɔ kɔ sʋan yɩ́ ngʋa'n-mɔ kɛ bɛ hɔ́ fɛ́lɛ bɛ́ m'ɔ tʋlɩ yɩ́ sa fɛlɛlɩ bɛ́'n kɛ b'a sesie alɩɛ nɩn a yue. Bɛ hɔlɩ fɛlɛlɩ bɛ́ a, menian sɔ'n-mɔ fɩ́ɩ́ ngɔ a man.

ref: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
text: “Ɔ kɔ sʋan yɩ́ ngʋa'n mmiekun-mɔ, yɩ́ nʋan la bɛ́ nʋan nun, yɩ́ nwan: ‘Ɛmɔ sé bɛ́ kɛ: M'an hun mɩ́n nnalɛ'n-mɔ nʋn naan'n-mɔ mɔ b'a lɔ kpa'n. M'an sesie alɩɛ nɩn a yue. Yɩ́ ti, ɛmɔ bála atɔnvɔlɛ nɩn abʋ.’

ref: But the guests did not pay any attention. Some of them left for their farms, and some went to their places of business.
text: “Bɛ hɔlɩ fɛlɛlɩ bɛ́ a, menian sɔ'n-mɔ ngɔ kpinlin belemgbin anwʋn. Ɛhɩ jasʋ a, ɔ lɛ kɔ yɩ́ ebolo, ɛhɩ jasʋ a, ɔ lɛ kɔ a tɔnɩn yɩ́ ninnge.

ref: Others grabbed the servants, then beat them up and killed them.
text: Mmie-mɔ kʋsʋ kɔ sɔ ngʋa'n-mɔ bɛ kɔ hele bɛ́ yalɛ dede bɛ kɔ hun bɛ́.

ref: This made the king so furious that he sent an army to kill those murderers and burn down their city.
text: Mɔ belemgbin'n tɩlɩ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n, ɔ kɔ fɛ ɛya. Ɔ kɔ sʋan yɩ́ nzalafʋ'n-mɔ kɛ bɛ hɔ́ hún kodiawu sɔ'n-mɔ ɛsɛ bɛ yála bɛ́ kulo'n.

ref: Then he said to the servants, “It is time for the wedding banquet, and the invited guests don't deserve to come.
text: “Mɔ ɛhɩ tɩlɩ yɩ́ nwʋn'n, ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele yɩ́ ngʋa'n-mɔ kɛ: ‘Atɔnvɔlɛ alɩɛ nɩn a bɩn. Nan bɛ́ mɔ n vɛlɛlɩ bɛ́'n, bɛ́ fɩ́ɩ́ b'a mma man.

ref: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
text: Yɩ́ ti, ɛmɔ hɔ́ gua nɩn asʋ ɛlɔ, sʋanlan biala mɔ ɛmɔ kɔ nwun yɩ́'n, ɛmɔ fɛ́lɛ yɩ́.’

ref: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
text: “Belemgbin'n kɔ wʋlʋ sua nɩn anun, ɔ kɔ nɛnɩan bɛ́ mɔ bɛ gugua tɔbɩlɩ nɩn anwʋn bɛ lɛ didi'n. Ɔ 'nɩan an, anɩn sʋanlan kʋn tɩ ɛbɛlɛ. Sʋanlan sɔ'n wula man atɔnvɔlɛ taladɩɛ'n bie.

ref: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
text: Bɛ kɔ sʋan bɛ́ menian'n mmie-mɔ, ɔ nʋn belemgbin *Elɔdɩ menian dɩɛ'n mmie-mɔ Zozi anwʋn ɛlɔ, kɛ bɛ hɔ́ bisa yɩ́ kɛ: “Kpain, yɛ sɩ kɛ wɔ́ dɩɛ ɛ kan ananhɔlɛ. Like mɔ Nyanmɩan kulo kɛ sʋanlan yɔ'n, ɛ kele yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ. Ajʋnlɩn mɔ menian'n-mɔ kɔ fa'n, ɛ nzulo man yɩ́ nzin. Afɩ ɛ nnɩan man sʋanlan nyunnun ɛ mmua man yɩ́ ndɛɛ.

ref: Let me see one of the coins used for paying taxes.” They brought him a silver coin,
text: Esika'n mɔ ɛmɔ fa tua Sezaa ajule'n, ɛmɔ fá bie bɛ mán n nɩ́an.” Bɛ kɔ fa kʋn bɛ kɔ bɛlɛ yɩ́.

ref: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
text: Bɛ́ nwan: “Belemgbin Sezaa ɔ.” Ɛhɩ a, Zozi nwan: “Like m'ɔ tɩ́ Sezaa dɩɛ'n, ɛmɔ fá mán Sezaa yɛ̂ m'ɔ tɩ Nyanmɩan kʋsʋ dɩɛ'n, ɛmɔ fá mán Nyanmɩan.”

ref: His answer surprised them so much that they walked away. Life in the Future World (Mark
text: Zozi mmua sɔ'n mɔ bɛ tɩlɩ'n sinlin bɛ́ nwʋn. Bɛ yacili yɩ́ ɛbɛlɛ yɛ̂ bɛ hɔlɩ ɔ. Ewuetinnge nɩn anwʋn kosuan'n (Maakɩ

ref: At last the woman died.
text: Yɩ́ nzin, balasua'n kʋsʋ kɔ wu.

ref: All the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets are based on these two commandments. About David's Son (Mark
text: Mala nnyuan ɛhɩ asʋ yɛ̂ Moyizɩ nʋn Nyanmɩan mgbɔmanfʋɛ'n-mɔ ngehele'n kʋalaa jin ɔ.” Kilisi'n nʋn belemgbin Davidi (Maakɩ

ref: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
text: Cɩan kʋnmgba sɔ'n, *Sadusifʋɛ'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ́ nwan ewuetinnge nnʋn man ɛbɛlɛ'n, bɛ́ mmie-mɔ kɔ a Zozi anwʋn ɛbɛlɛ. Bɛ kɔ bisa yɩ́ kosuan kɛ:

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Bɛ́ kosuan sɔ nɩn anwʋn, Zozi kɔ bua bɛ́ kɛ: “Ɛmɔ a fʋn, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ɛmɔ nzɩ man Ɛhɛlɛ Ɛjɔlɛ'n nʋn Nyanmɩan tunmin'n.

ref: When God raises people to life, they won't marry. They will be like the angels in heaven.
text: Afɩ ewuetinnge cɩan'n, mmelenzua'n-mɔ ngɔ ja man mmalasua kʋ́n, mmalasua'n-mɔ kʋsʋ ngɔ ja man mmelenzua kʋ́n. Nan bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ 'kaci kɛ nyanmɩansʋ mmɔfʋɛ'n-mɔ.

ref: And as for people being raised to life, God was speaking to you when he said,
