text: Ewuetinnge nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ Nyanmɩan Kpili hanlɩn helelɩ ɛmɔ'n, ɛmɔ kɩnngalɩ man yɩ́ lé ɔ? Ɔ hanlɩn kɛ:

ref: “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living.
text: Abalahamʋn nʋn Izakɩ nʋn Zʋakɔbʋ, bɛ́ Nyanmɩan'n y'ɔ le mɩ́n. Ngʋan mma'n-mɔ, bɛ́ Nyanmɩan'n y'ɔ le yɩ́, nan ɔ tɩ man ewue mma Nyanmɩan.”

ref: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
text: Mɔ meninsʋnman'n tɩlɩ Zozi ngehele sɔ'n, ɔ sinlin bɛ́ nwʋn. Mala'n m'ɔ tɩ kpili tala bɛ́ kʋalaa'n (Maakɩ

ref: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
text: Mɔ *Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ tɩlɩ kɛ Zozi a hʋala *Sadusifʋɛ'n-mɔ'n, bɛ kɔ yia ɛbɛlɛ.

ref: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
text: “Kpain, mala'n nunhan benin y'ɔ tɩ kpili tala bɛ́ kʋalaa ɔ?”

ref: This is the first and most important commandment.
text: Mala m'ɔ li mʋa m'ɔ tɩ kpili tala bɛ́ kʋalaa nɩn anɩn.

ref: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: M'ɔ tian yɩ́ nnyuan, m'ɔ nʋn alimʋa dɩɛ'n tɩ kʋn'n, y'ɔ le ɛhɩ: Ɔ di kɛ ɛ kulo wɔ́ manngʋn kɛ wɔ́ muonun wɔ́ nwʋn.

ref: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
text: Anɩn *Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ ati a yia. Zozi kɔ bisa bɛ́ kosuan kɛ:

ref: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
text: Yɛ́ Mɩn a han a hele mɩ́n Mɩn kɛ: Tanlan mɩ́n fama sʋ, nán mɩn 'ba fa wɔ́ mgbɔfʋɛ'n-mɔ mɩn gua wɔ́ ja abʋ.

ref: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
text: “Ɛmɔ jʋnlɩn kɛ nwan y'ɔ le *Kilisi'n? Ɔ tɩ nwan awa?” Bɛ kɔ bua yɩ́ kɛ: “Ɔ tɩ belemgbin *Davidi awa.”

ref: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,
text: Zozi nwan: “Nan anɩn ɔ yɔ sɛ mɔ Wawɛ Nwannzan-nwannzan'n manlɩn Davidi fɛlɛlɩ yɩ́ ‘mɩ́n Mɩn'n’? Afɩ Davidi nwan:

ref: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
text: Sɛ Davidi fɛlɛ yɩ́ ‘mɩ́n Mɩn’ an, anɩn ɔ yɔ sɛ mɔ ɛsɛ Kilisi'n tɩ Davidi awa'n?”

ref: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
text: Sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ a ngʋala man ɛjɔlɛ kpɔlɛ kʋn bua. Ɔ fi ɛbɛlɛ, sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ a ngʋala man Zozi kosuan fɩ́ɩ́ kʋ́n bisa.

ref: And now your temple will be deserted.
text: Ɛmɔ nɩ́an, ɛmɔ awulo'n lɛ ba ha to kpa bɔkɔɔ.

ref: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi nwan: “Zoluzalɛmʋn, Zoluzalɛmʋn, wɔ́ mɔ ɛ kun Nyanmɩan mgbɔmanfʋɛ'n-mɔ ɛ tʋtʋ ɛbʋɛ ɛ kun bɛ́ mɔ Nyanmɩan kɔ sʋan bɛ́ wɔ́ nwʋn'n, n gulolɩ yɩ́ sʋnman kɛ mɩn kokuan wɔ́ mma'n-mɔ anʋan mɩ́n nwʋn kɛ akɔ ɛta kokuan yɩ́ mma'n-mɔ anʋan yɩ́ ndɛba'n-mɔ abʋ'n, nan kʋsʋ ɛmɔ a ndie man!

ref: Anyone who swears by the altar also swears by everything on it.
text: Sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ han tɛɛyɩɛ mʋcɩa'n, anɩn ɔ'a han yɩ́ nʋn sʋ ninnge'n kʋalaatin.

ref: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi kɔ han kɔ hele meninsʋnman'n nʋn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ:

ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
text: “*Mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn *Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ yɛ̂ bɛ tu *Moyizɩ mala nɩn abʋ bɛ kele menian'n-mɔ ɔ.

ref: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
text: Ɛmɔ mán bɛ́ nyɩn sɔ́ bɛ́. Like biala mɔ bɛ kɔ hele kɛ ɛmɔ yɔ́'n, ɛmɔ yɔ́. Nan kʋsʋ nán bɛ nɩan bɛ́ nyɔlɩɛ dɩɛ nɩn asʋ bɛ yɔ bie. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ mala'n mɔ bɛ́ muonun bɛ kele'n, bɛ nni man sʋ.

ref: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
text: Bɛ cɩcɩ ninnge nʋnnɔɛ bɛ sʋa menian'n-mɔ, nan kʋsʋ bɛ́ muonun bɛ nva man bɛ́ sa bɛtɩa kʋn bɛ mmʋka man bɛ́.

ref: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
text: Bɛ yɔ bɛ́ ninnge dɩɛ'n-mɔ kʋalaa maan menian'n-mɔ nwun bɛ́ nzɔlɛ. Bɛ yɩyɩ Ɛhɛlɛ Ɛjɔlɛ nɩn anun ɛjɔlɛ'n dɔʋn bɛ bɔ yɩ́ kɛ sɛbɛ ndɛtɛlɛ ndɛtɛlɛ bɛ bʋbɔ bɛ́ nwʋnman nɩn asʋ anaan bɛ́ sa. Bɛ yɔ bɛ́ taladɩɛ'n-mɔ anʋan mgbɛsɛ-mgbɛsɛ'n-mɔ ndɩnndɩɩn kpa.

ref: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
text: Bɛ kulo kɛ sɛ menian'n-mɔ yia bagua nun nán sɛ bɛ 'bisa bɛ́ ahɩn an, bɛ kʋ́tʋ, ɛsɛ bɛ fɛ́lɛ bɛ́ ‘mɩnlɩan’.

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: “Ɛmɔ dɩɛ, nán bɛ man sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ fɛlɛ ɛmɔ ‘Mɩnlɩan’. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ɛmɔ kʋalaa tɩ anianman, ɛsɛ ɛmɔ kʋalaa Amɩn'n tɩ baa kʋnmgba.

ref: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
text: Asɩɛ nɩn asʋ ɛwa, nán bɛ fɛlɛ sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ ‘Sɩɛ’. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ Sɩɛ kʋnmgba cein'n mɔ ɛmɔ le yɩ́'n, y'ɔ le nunhan'n m'ɔ wɔ anwunno ɛlɔ'n.

ref: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
text: Nán bɛ man sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ fɛlɛ ɛmɔ ‘Kpain’. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ɛmɔ Kpain'n tɩ baa kʋnmgba cein, y'ɔ le Kilisi.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
text: “Ɛmɔ mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn ɛmɔ Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ, apoo mma, munnzue hán ɛmɔ! Ɛmɔ tʋa kunlannvʋɛ mmalasua'n-mɔ bɛ de bɛ́ ninnge'n kʋalaatin nán ɛmɔ a kpɛ bɛ́ nyɩn b'a yɔ asɔnɩn ndɩnndɩɩn maan menian'n-mɔ nwun kɛ ɛmɔ tɩ kpa. Ɛmɔ nyɔlɩɛ sɔ nɩn anwʋn kalɛ mɔ Nyanmɩan 'ba tua ɛmɔ'n, ɔ tɩ man nzɛmɩndɛ.]

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
text: “Ɛmɔ mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn ɛmɔ Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ, apoo mma, munnzue hán ɛmɔ. Ɛmɔ nanndɩ asɩɛ'n lɩka kʋalaatin nʋn jenvie nɩn anyɩnsʋ bɛ kpʋnndɛ sʋanlan maan ɔ kaci yɩ́ nwʋn kɛ Zufʋ'n-mɔ. Ɛmɔ nyan yɩ́ yue a, ɛmɔ man ɔ kaci sɩ̂n'n m'ɔ nnun man lé nɩn anun sʋanlan fâ nnyuan tala ɛmɔ.

ref: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
text: “Anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ mɔ bɛ kekele menian atɩn, munnzue hán ɛmɔ! Ɛmɔ nwan: ‘Sɛ sʋanlan kʋn kan asɔnɩn sua nɩn ati a, anɩn ɔ'a nyɔ man like fɩ́ɩ́. Nan sɛ ɔ kan esika kɔkɔlɛ'n m'ɔ wɔ nun nɩn ati a, bɛlɛ mɔ anɩn ɔ'a han ndâ ɔ.’

ref: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
text: Ngʋasɩafʋɛ anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ! Esika kɔkɔlɛ'n nʋn Nyanmɩan sua'n m'ɔ man esika kɔkɔlɛ'n yɔ nwannzan-nwannzan'n, benin y'ɔ tɩ cɩnnjɩn kpa ɔ?

ref: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
text: Ɛsɛ ɛmɔ nwan: ‘Sɛ sʋanlan kʋn kan *tɛɛyɩɛ mʋcɩa nɩn ati a, anɩn ɔ'a nyɔ man like fɩ́ɩ́. Nan sɛ ɔ kan afɔlɩɛ'n m'ɔ wɔ tɛɛyɩɛ mʋcɩa nɩn asʋ nɩn an, bɛlɛ mɔ anɩn ɔ'a han ndâ ɔ.’

ref: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
text: Anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ! Tɛɛyɩɛ mʋcɩa'n nʋn sʋ ninnge'n mɔ tɛɛyɩɛ mʋcɩa'n man ɔ yɔ nwannzan-nwannzan'n, benin y'ɔ tɩ cɩnnjɩn kpa ɔ?

ref: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
text: Sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ han Nyanmɩan sua'n, anɩn ɔ'a han yɩ́ nʋn sʋanlan'n m'ɔ wɔ nun nɩn ati.

ref: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
text: Sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ han Nyanmɩan belemgbin bia'n m'ɔ wɔ nyanmɩansʋ'n, anɩn ɔ'a han Nyanmɩan m'ɔ tɩ sʋ nɩn ati ɔ'a bʋka sʋ.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
text: “Ɛmɔ mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn ɛmɔ Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ, apoo mma, munnzue hán ɛmɔ! Mmaka mma kɛ mantɩ, anɛtɩ nʋn kunmɩn'n, ɛmɔ bu nun bulu, bɛ tu kʋn bɛ man Nyanmɩan. Nan kʋsʋ mân sɛsɛ ebue'n, anwunnvoe enwuan'n nʋn ananhɔlɛ elie'n m'ɔ tɩ mala nɩn anwʋn like cɩnnjɩn'n, ɛmɔ bubu bɛ́ nyɩn bɛ gua sʋ. Like m'ɔ sɛ kɛ ɛmɔ yɔ́ nɩn anɩn. Ɛsɛ ɔ nni man kɛ ɛmɔ bubu bɛ́ nyɩn bɛ gua yɩ́ bʋ nga nɩn asʋ.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
text: “Ɛmɔ mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn ɛmɔ Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ, apoo mma, munnzue hán ɛmɔ! Ɛmɔ nwunnzin kangɔ'n nʋn talie nɩn anzin'n, nan kʋsʋ yɩ́ kunnun tɩ awue nʋn ati anun ɛtɛ ninnge ngʋnmɩn.

ref: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
text: Falisifʋɛ anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ, di mʋa nwunnzin kangɔ'n nʋn talie nɩn akunnun nán a nwunnzin yɩ́ nzin'n kʋsʋ.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
text: “Ɛmɔ mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn ɛmɔ Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ, apoo mma, munnzue hán ɛmɔ! Ɛmɔ soman tomo mɔ b'a han yɩ́ ayile fufue kanlanman ɔ. Nan kʋsʋ yɩ́ kʋ nɩn anun tɩ mmowe ngʋnmɩn nʋn ninnge kpɔlɔlɩwa ngacile kʋalaatin.

ref: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
text: Kɛ ɛmɔ kʋsʋ tɩ yɩ́ nɩn anɩn. Menian'n-mɔ nwun ɛmɔ a, bɛ bu ɛmɔ meninkpa. Nan kʋsʋ apoo mma y'ɔ le ɛmɔ, ajʋnlɩn ɛtɛ ɛtɛ ngʋnmɩn wɔ ɛmɔ ati anun.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
text: “Ɛmɔ *mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ, nʋn ɛmɔ *Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ, apoo mma, munnzue hán ɛmɔ! Nyanmɩan mgbɔmanfʋɛ'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ kɔ wu'n, ɛmɔ tʋ bɛ́ tomo kanlanman kpa.

ref: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
text: Ɛmɔ nwan: ‘Sɛ ɔ tɩ kɛ yɛ́ nan-mɔ bɛlɛ sʋ anɩn yɛ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ a, ahan yɛ́ nʋn bɛ́, yɛ́ bʋ a nyɔ man kʋ́n y'a ngun man Nyanmɩan mgbɔmanfʋɛ'n-mɔ.’

ref: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
text: Ɛmɔ muonun di ɛmɔ anwʋn adanzɩɛ kɛ bɛ́ mɔ bɛ hunlin Nyanmɩan mgbɔmanfʋɛ'n-mɔ, bɛ́ bʋsʋ menian y'ɔ le ɛmɔ.

ref: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
text: “Ati ɛtɛ mma kɛ owo! Ɔ yɔ sɛ mɔ ɛmɔ jʋnlɩn kɛ ɛmɔ ati ngɔ ha man sɩ̂n'n m'ɔ le man ayuelɩɛ nɩn anun ɔ?

ref: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
text: Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ menian sɔ'n-mɔ kʋalaatin mɔ bɛ lili bɛ́ awue bɛ hunlin bɛ́'n, bɛ́ ewue nɩn anwʋn jɔwɔlɛ'n 'kpolo ajulisʋ menian ɛhɩ-mɔ ati.” Zozi 'ba yɩ awɔ́lɛ gua Zoluzalɛmʋn amma'n-mɔ anwʋn (Luku

ref: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: Afɩ mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ ɛmɔ ngɔ nwun man mɩ́n nwʋn kʋ́n lé dede ɔ 'ju cɩan mɔ ɛmɔ 'se kɛ: ‘Nyila hán sʋanlan'n m'ɔ lɛ ba wɔ yɛ́ Mɩn dunman nun'n.’”

ref: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
text: Zozi fi Nyanmɩan awulo nɩn anun ɔ kɔ fite. M'ɔ 'kɔ'n, yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ kpunnge yɩ́ kɛ ɔ tʋ́ yɩ́ nyɩn ɔ nɩ́an Nyanmɩan awulo nɩn anun sua'n-mɔ.

ref: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi nwan: “Ɛhɩ kʋalaa mɔ ɛmɔ nwun yɩ́ ɛwa'n, mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ bɛ 'ba bubu yɩ́ bɛ gua. Ɛbʋɛ baa kʋn ngɔ tanlan man yɩ́ manngʋn asʋ.” Munnzue nʋn ahʋlʋwa'n (Maakɩ

ref: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ menian dɔʋn kpa, bɛ 'ba nanndɩ mɩ́n dunman nɩn asʋ nán bɛ́ nun kʋn biala 'ba se kɛ: ‘*Kilisi'n, Belemgbin Ngʋandefʋɛ'n, y'ɔ le mɩ́n.’ Bɛ 'laka menian dɔʋn.

ref: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
text: Ɛmɔ 'ba tɩ kɛ menian'n-mɔ lɛ kan ɛlɔɛ ɛjɔlɛ ɛmɔ anwʋn ɛbɛlɛ ala, ɛsɛ bɛ 'ba han bɛ kele ɛmɔ kɛ mmie-mɔ lɛ kʋn mʋa ɛlɔ. Nán bɛ man bɛ́ kunnun tɩtɩ bɛ́, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ɔ sɛ kɛ ɔ yɔ́ sɔ, kʋsʋ anɩn nán mân nɩn ayuelɩɛ nɩn anɩn.

ref: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
text: Zozi kɔ hɔ kɔ tanlan asɩ *Olivʋ bʋka nɩn asʋ. Yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ a yɩ́ nwʋn ɛbɛlɛ, nán b'a bisa yɩ́ kɛ: “Anɩn cɩan benin yɛ̂ nyɔbʋɛ sɔ'n-mɔ 'kan gua asɩ ɔ? Wɔ́ ɛwalɛ cɩan nɩn anwʋn nzɔlɛ'n y'ɔ le benin? Mân'n kʋsʋ ayuelɩɛ cɩan'n y'ɔ le benin? Kan kele yɛ́.”

ref: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
text: Mân kʋn 'ba jasʋ ɔ nʋn mân kʋn bɛ kʋn ɛlɔɛ. Belemgbin mân kʋn 'ba jasʋ ɔ nʋn belemgbin mân kʋn bɛ kʋn ɛlɔɛ. Ɛhɔɛ 'ba kpɩn eyuadɩ'n lɩka mmie-mɔ, ɛsɛ asɩɛ'n 'ba kpusu wɔ lɩka ngacile dɔʋn.

ref: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
text: Mɛlɛ sɔ nɩn anun, mmalasua'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ lɛ nwʋnnzɛ'n, ɔ nʋn bɛ́ mɔ bɛ lɛ nʋan mma'n, bɛ́ yakɔ!

ref: Jesus answered: Don't let anyone fool you.
text: Zozi nwan: “Ɛmɔ nɩ́an bɛ́ nwʋn kpa nán sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ a nnaka man ɛmɔ.

ref: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
text: “Ɛhɩ, menian'n-mɔ 'ba yɩ ɛmɔ man maan bɛ kele ɛmɔ ahʋlʋwa ɛsɛ bɛ 'kun ɛmɔ. Mɩ́n dunman nun, menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa 'ba kpɔ ɛmɔ.

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
text: Ato mgbɔmanfʋɛ dɔʋn 'ba fite fite bɛ laka menian dɔʋn.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: Mɛlɛ sɔ nɩn anun, sɛ sʋanlan bie se ɛmɔ kɛ: ‘Ɛmɔ nɩ́an, Kilisi'n wɔ ɛwa!’ anaan ‘Ɔ wɔ ɛlɔ!’ a, nán bɛ de bɛ di.

ref: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
text: Ɛmɔ sɛ́lɛ Nyanmɩan kpa nán ɛmɔ ɛnwanndie cɩan nɩn a nyia man fɔsɩɛ nun, anaan ɛnwʋnmɩan ele cɩan.

ref: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
text: “Ɛmɔ 'ba nwun like ɛtɛ kpili'n m'ɔ bɔ alʋa mɔ Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ Danɩɛ hanlɩn yɩ́ nwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n kɛ bɛ 'ba sie yɩ́ lɩka nwannzan-nwannzan nɩn anun.

ref: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
text: Yɩ́ ti, bɛ́ mɔ bɛ wɔ Zude mân nɩn anun'n, bɛ nwánndi bɛ hɔ́ mmʋka'n-mɔ asʋ.

ref: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
text: Sʋanlan m'ɔ kɔ hɔ ebolo'n, n'ɔ sa ba fa taladɩɛ.

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Sɛ Nyanmɩan muonun a mgbɛ man tɛmʋn sɔ nɩn asʋ a, sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ nnyan man yɩ́ ti. Nan yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ mɔ ɔ'a kpa bɛ́ ɔ'a yɔ yɩ́ dɩɛ'n-mɔ dunman nun, ɔ'a kpɛ cɩan sɔ'n-mɔ asʋ.

ref: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
text: Ɛmɔ kʋsʋ, mɛlɛ mɔ ɛmɔ kɔ nwun ɛhɩ kʋalaa'n, anɩn ɛmɔ sɩ kɛ Mân Baa nɩn ɛwalɛ nɩn a ju a yue ɔ'a mantan ɛmɔ kʋkʋ.

ref: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
text: Nowe bɛlɛ sʋ, ɛjɔlɛ'n m'ɔ walɩ'n, yɩ́ kʋnmgba nɩn ala yɛ̂ ɔ 'ba a Mân Baa nɩn ɛwalɛ nun ɔ.

ref: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
text: “M'ɔ tʋ de cɩan'n, anaan dɔɛ'n, sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ nzɩ man. Anwunno ɛlɔ mmɔfʋɛ'n-mɔ o, Mân Baa nɩn o, bɛ́ fɩ́ɩ́ bɛ nzɩ man. Nan Sɩɛ nɩn angʋnmɩn y'ɔ sɩ ɔ.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
text: Nowe bɛlɛ sʋ, asannan nán esue kpili'n m'ɔ nʋanlɩn mân nɩn a tɔ'n, anɩn menian'n-mɔ lɛ didi, bɛ lɛ nʋn nzan, bɛ lɛ ja bɛ́ nwʋn, bɛ lɛ fa bɛ́ mma mmalasua'n-mɔ atɔnvɔlɛ, dede ɔ juli mɛlɛ mɔ Nowe fʋlɩ meli nɩn anun'n.

ref: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
text: Bɛ gua ɛbɛlɛ, ɛjɔlɛ fɩ́ɩ́ a nvalɩ bɛ́ nwʋn, dede esue kpili'n kɔ tɔ, bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ kɔ nʋan. Mân Baa'n kʋsʋ ɛwalɛ nun, kɛ ɛjɔlɛ'n 'ba yɔ yɩ́ nɩn ala anɩn.

ref: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Mɛlɛ sɔ nɩn anun, mmelenzua nnyuan mɔ bɛ kɔ hɔ ebolo bɛ lɛ di junman lɩka kʋn'n, bɛ 'fa kʋn bɛ yaci kʋn.

ref: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
text: “Yɩ́ sɔ ati, ɛmɔ tánlan bɛ sísa, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ, ɛmɔ nzɩ man cɩan'n mɔ bɛ́ Mɩn 'ba ɔ.

ref: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
text: Ɛmɔ nwún yɩ́ kɛ awulo kpain'n, sɛ ɔ sɩ dɔɛ'n mɔ alɩ́ɛ san an, awofʋɛ'n ba ɔ wua yɩ́ a, ahan ɔ 'tanlan asɩ ɔ sisa, ɔ nyaci man yɩ́ maan ɔ nwʋlʋ man yɩ́ sua nɩn anun.

ref: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
text: Ɛhɩ ati, ɛmɔ kʋsʋ, ɛmɔ bɔ́bʋa bɛ́ nwʋn, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ, Mân Baa'n 'ba a dɔɛ mɔ ɛmɔ nzusu man nɩn asʋ. Akʋa kpa'n yɛ̂ akʋa ɛtɛ'n (Luku

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
text: Nan sɛ akʋa sɔ'n tɩ sʋanlan ɛtɛ a, ɔ 'kan yɩ́ ti anun kɛ: ‘Mɩ́n mɩn nnyan walɩ man.’

ref: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
text: “Akʋa ngɛlɛfʋɛ ananhɔlɛfʋɛ'n, y'ɔ le nwan? Y'ɔ le akʋa'n mɔ yɩ́ mɩn a fa junman a wula yɩ́ sa nun kɛ ɔ nɩ́an yɩ́ ngʋa ɛhɩnlɩnka-mɔ asʋ, ɔ mán bɛ́ alɩɛ mɛlɛ mɔ dɔɛ'n kɔ ju'n.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: Nyila hán akʋa sɔ'n mɔ yɩ́ mɩn 'sa ba a, ɔ 'ba tʋ yɩ́ junman nɩn asʋ'n!

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ, akʋa sɔ'n, yɩ́ mɩn'n 'fa yɩ́ ninnge'n kʋalaatin wula yɩ́ sa nun.

ref: Suppose this evil servant starts beating the other servants and eats and drinks with people who are drunk.
text: Ɔ 'tɩ tɔ yɩ́ manngʋn'n-mɔ mɔ ɔ nʋn bɛ di junman nɩn anwʋn ɔ bili bɛ́, ɔ nʋn kɔnʋnmɩnnzan'n-mɔ bɛ 'didi ɛsɛ bɛ 'nʋn nzan.

ref: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
text: Cɩan m'ɔ njʋnlɩn man yɩ́ nwʋn ɔ nʋn dɔɛ m'ɔ nzusu man'n, yɛ̂ yɩ́ mɩn'n 'ba a ɔ.

ref: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
text: Ɔ 'kele akʋa sɔ'n yalɛ kpa, ɔ 'yɔ yɩ kɛ bɛ yɔ apoo mma-mɔ'n. Ɛbɛlɛ nɩn anun, ɔ 'ba sun ɛsɛ ɔ 'kʋan yɩ́ je'n.

ref: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
text: Ngʋasɩafʋɛ'n-mɔ kɔ fa bɛ́ nganlannɩɛ'n-mɔ, bɛ ngɔ fa man ngo bɛ ngɔ sa man bɛ́ nwʋn.

ref: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
text: Nan bɛngɛlɛfʋɛ nnun nɩn an, bɛ kɔ fa bɛ́ nganlannɩɛ'n-mɔ ɔ nʋn ngo ɛsɛ̂ nun bɛ kɔ sa bɛ́ nwʋn.

ref: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
text: “Mɔ alɩ́ɛ'n buli nun'n, bɛ 'tie a, anɩn sʋanlan bie lɛ tɩan, yɩ́ nwan: ‘Ɛ 'nwun atɔnvɔlɛ bian'n! Bɛ fíte bɛ hɔ́ kpá yɩ́ atɩn!’

ref: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
text: “Ɛbɛlɛ ala, ndalie bulu'n kɔ jasʋ jasʋ, bɛ yɔ bɛ́ nganlannɩɛ'n-mɔ kpa.

ref: the foolish ones said to the others, “Let us have some of your oil! Our lamps are going out.”
text: Ngʋasɩafʋɛ'n-mɔ kɔ se bɛngɛlɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ kɛ: ‘Ɛmɔ mán yɛ́ bɛ́ ngo'n kaan, nán yɛ́ nganlannɩɛ'n-mɔ lɛ nun.’

ref: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
text: “Bɛngɛlɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ kɔ bua bɛ́ kɛ: ‘Cɛcɛ, ɛmɔ ngɔ nyan man yɩ́, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ, ɔ nza man yɛ́ nʋn ɛmɔ. Ɛmɔ hɔ́ bɛ́ mɔ bɛ tɔnɩn yɩ́'n-mɔ ɛlɔ, bɛ hɔ́ tó bɛ́ dɩɛ.’

ref: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi kɔ han kɔ tʋ sʋ kɛ: “Yɩ́ sɔ ati, ɛmɔ sísa, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ɛmɔ nzɩ man cɩan'n, anaan dɔɛ'n.” Ngʋa nsan nɩn anwʋn anyɩnndala'n (Luku

ref: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
text: “Bɛ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ dede ɔ hyɛlɩ kpa, bɛ́ mɩn'n kɔ a, ɔ kɔ fɛfɛlɛ bɛ́ kɛ esika'n m'ɔ falɩ manlɩn bɛ́'n, bɛ bála a bu yɩ́ mgbʋnndan.

ref: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
text: Ahan ɛ falɩ mɩn esika'n manlɩn bankɩ mma'n-mɔ sielɩ. Yɩ́ dɩɛ n vi ɛlɔ mɩn ba a, anɩn mɩn yɩ mɩ́n esika'n nʋn yɩ́ ngua nun'n.

ref: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
text: “Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n soman bian kʋn m'ɔ lɛ ba tu atɩn. Ɔ kɔ fɛfɛlɛ yɩ́ ngʋa'n-mɔ ɔ kɔ fa yɩ́ esika'n kɔ wowula bɛ́ sa nun.

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: “Yɩ́ mɩn'n nwan: ‘Akʋa kpa, mo! Ɛ tɩ ananhɔlɛfʋɛ kpa. Like kaan ɛhɩ mɔ m'an fa m'an man wɔ́'n, a yɔ yɩ́ kpa. Yɩ́ ti, mɩn 'fa ninnge dɔʋn mɩn wula wɔ́ sa nun. Bala tanlan mɩ́n nwʋn ɛwa maan yɛ lí fɛ.’

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: “Yɩ́ mɩn'n nwan: ‘Akʋa kpa, mo! Ɛ tɩ ananhɔlɛfʋɛ kpa. Like kaan ɛhɩ mɔ m'an fa m'an man wɔ́'n, a yɔ yɩ́ kpa. Yɩ́ ti, mɩn 'fa ninnge dɔʋn mɩn wula wɔ́ sa nun. Bala tanlan mɩ́n nwʋn ɛwa maan yɛ lí fɛ.’

ref: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
text: Esulo hanlɩn mɩ́n, n gɔ n vunlin wɔ́ esika'n n wulalɩ asɩɛ bʋ. Ɛ 'nwun wɔ́ esika'n, de wɔ́ like.’

ref: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
text: “Yɩ́ mɩn'n nwan: ‘Akʋa ati anun ɛtɛ ɛfʋanfʋɛ, ɛ sɩ kɛ mɩn man ɛmɔ di junman mgban mɩn nyan sʋ nvasʋɛ'n.

ref: Then the master said, “Now your money will be taken away and given to the servant with
text: Yɩ́ ti, ɛmɔ tʋ́a yɩ́ bɛ lé esika kɔkɔlɛ mma ɛya kʋn'n m'ɔ wɔ yɩ́ sa nun'n, bɛ fá bɛ mán akʋa'n m'ɔ le akpɩɩ kʋn'n.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ sʋanlan m'ɔ le bie'n, bɛ 'man yɩ́ dɔʋn biekun. Nan sʋanlan kʋsʋ m'ɔ le man fɩ́ɩ́'n, bɛ 'tʋa yɩ́ bɛ de kaan'n m'ɔ le'n.

ref: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
text: Nán akʋa mgban ɛhɩ, bɛ jú yɩ́ bɛ tʋ́ awosin nɩn anun ɛlɔ. Ɛbɛlɛ yɛ̂ ɔ 'sun ɛsɛ ɔ 'kʋan yɩ́ je nɩn ɔ.’ Kulo menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa ndɛɛ ebua'n

ref: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
text: “Mɛlɛ mɔ *Mân Baa'n ba a anunminnyanmʋn nɩn anun'n ɔ nʋn nyanmɩansʋ mmɔfʋɛ'n-mɔ kʋalaatin'n, ɔ 'tanlan yɩ́ anunminnyanmʋn belemgbin bia nɩn asʋ.

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: Asɩɛ nɩn asʋ menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa 'ba yia yɩ́ nyunnun ɛbɛlɛ. Ɔ 'kpa bɛ́ nun kɛ bʋa ɛnɩanfʋɛ'n kpa yɩ́ mmʋa'n ɔ sie bɛ́ ngʋnmɩn ɛsɛ ɔ sie yɩ́ esile'n-mɔ kʋsʋ angʋnmɩn'n.

ref: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
text: Ɔ 'fa mmʋa'n-mɔ sie yɩ́ sa fama sʋ, ɔ 'fa esile'n-mɔ kʋsʋ sie yɩ́ sa bɛ̂ sʋ.

ref: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
text: “Ɛhɩ anzin, belemgbin'n 'ba se bɛ́ mɔ bɛ wɔ yɩ́ sa fama sʋ'n kɛ: ‘Ɛmɔ mɔ mɩ́n Sɩ a yeyila bɛ́'n, ɛmɔ bála a fa Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n. Nyanmɩan a fa a sie ɛmɔ ajâ. Ɔ sesielɩ manlɩn ɛmɔ sunmin kɛ ɔ bɔlɩ mân'n.

ref: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ mɛlɛ mɔ anɩn ɛhɔɛ lɛ kun mɩ́n'n, ɛmɔ manlɩn mɩ́n like n nili. Mɔ nzuhoe 'kun mɩ́n'n, ɛmɔ manlɩn mɩ́n nzue n nʋnlɩn. Mɔ m malɩ ɛyɛfʋɛ ɛmɔ kulo nɩn asʋ'n, ɛmɔ sikelɩ mɩ́n.

ref: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”

ref: The king will answer, “Whenever you did it for any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you did it for me.”
text: “Belemgbin'n 'ba se bɛ́ kɛ: ‘Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ yɩ́ kʋalaa mɔ ɛmɔ yɔlɩ manlɩn mɩ́n nianman-mɔ nunhan kutua'n bie'n, mɩ́n yɛ̂ ɛmɔ yɔlɩ manlɩn mɩ́n ɔ.’

ref: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
text: “Nan afɩ belemgbin'n 'ba se bɛ́ mɔ bɛ gua yɩ́ sa bɛ̂ sʋ'n kɛ: ‘Ɛmɔ mɔ b'a fa munnzue'n, ɛmɔ jásʋ mɩ́n nwʋn ɛwa. Ɛmɔ hɔ́ sɩ̂n'n m'ɔ nnun man lé, mɔ b'a sesie b'a man Abɔnsanmʋn nʋn yɩ́ mmɔfʋɛ'n-mɔ nɩn anun.

ref: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ mɛlɛ mɔ anɩn ɛhɔɛ lɛ kun mɩ́n'n, ɛmɔ a mman man mɩ́n like m'an nni man. Mɔ nzuhoe 'kun mɩ́n'n, ɛmɔ a mman man mɩ́n nzue m'an nnʋn man.

ref: I was a stranger, but you did not welcome me, and I was naked, but you did not give me any clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail, but you did not take care of me.”
text: Mɔ m malɩ ɛyɛfʋɛ ɛmɔ kulo nɩn asʋ'n, ɛmɔ a nzike man mɩ́n. Mɔ anɩn mɩ́n nwʋn gua sʋ'n, ɛmɔ a mman man mɩ́n taladɩɛ m'an nwula man. Mɔ n vʋkɛlɩ'n, ɛsɛ mɔ bɛ tʋlɩ mɩ́n fiadɩ'n, ɛmɔ a nnɩan man mɩ́n bʋsʋ.’

ref: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
text: “Belemgbin'n 'ba bua bɛ́ kɛ: ‘Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ yɩ́ kʋalaa mɔ ɛmɔ a nyɔ a mman man mɩ́n nianman ngutua ɛhɩ-mɔ anun kʋn bie'n, anɩn mɩ́n yɛ̂ ɛmɔ a nyɔ a mman man mɩ́n ɔ.’

ref: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
text: “Menian sɔ'n-mɔ, bɛ 'kɔ lɩka mɔ yalɛ elie'n le man ayuelɩɛ'n. Nan sɛsɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ dɩɛ, bɛ 'nyan nyanmɩansʋ ngʋan m'ɔ le man ayuelɩɛ'n.”

ref: “You know two days from now will be Passover. This is when the Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies and nailed to a cross.”

ref: When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, “This man was with Jesus from Nazareth.”
text: Ɛhɩ, ɔ fi ɛbɛlɛ ɔ kɔ jasʋ ɔ kɔ hɔ kɔ jinlan awulo nɩn anʋan nɩn anun ɛlɔ. Afanɩan kʋn biekun kɔ nwun yɩ́. Ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele bɛ́ mɔ bɛ gua ɛbɛlɛ'n kɛ: “Zozi m'ɔ fi Nazalɛtɩ'n, anɩn bian ɛhɩ si yɩ́ sʋ bie.”

ref: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there.
text: Bɛ́ mɔ bɛ hyɩlɩ Zozi'n, bɛ nʋn yɩ́ hɔlɩ Nyanmɩan *tɛɛyɩfʋɛ kpain'n mɔ bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ *Kayifʋ nɩn awulo. Ɛbɛlɛ yɛ̂ *mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn Zufʋ mgbain'n-mɔ hɔlɩ yialɩ ɔ.

ref: But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.
text: Mɔ bɛ 'kɔ'n, anɩn Pɩɛlɩ lɛ di Zozi fannvanndʋ dede bɛ juli Nyanmɩan tɛɛyɩfʋɛ kpain nɩn awulo. Pɩɛlɩ kɔ wʋlʋ awulo nɩn anun, ɔ kɔ tanlan nzalafʋ'n-mɔ anwʋn ɛbɛlɛ, ɔ 'nɩan kɛ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n 'ba kpɩn gua yɩ́ bʋ'n.

ref: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.

text: Bɛ kpʋnndɛlɩ, kʋsʋ bɛ ngɔ nyan man bie sunnzun mɔ adanzɩyua dɔʋn tʋnlɩn yɩ́ ɛsʋan ɔ. Yɩ́ bɛ-yue-asɩɛ, menian nnyuan walɩ a, bɛ́ nwan:

text: Ɛhɩ, bɛ kɔ tʋtʋ Zozi anyunnun ngɛsɛlɛ, ɛsɛ bɛ kɔ tʋtʋ yɩ́ kɔtɔlɛ. Mmie-mɔ fɩa yɩ́ nyɩn bɛ kpaci yɩ́ nyɩn nɩn asʋ,

ref: and said, “You think you are the Messiah! So tell us who hit you!” Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Mark
text: anɩn bɛ́ nwan: “Wɔ́ mɔ ɛ tɩ Kilisi'n, kele yɛ́ sʋanlan'n mɔ ɔ'a kpaci wɔ́ nyɩn nɩn asʋ'n!” Pɩɛlɩ 'ba se kɛ ɔ nzɩ man Zozi (Maakɩ

ref: Again Peter denied it, and this time he swore, “I don't even know that man!”
text: Ɛhɩ, Pɩɛlɩ nwan: “Mɩn kan ndâ kɛ mɩ́n dɩɛ, mɩn nzɩ man sʋanlan sɔ'n!”

ref: While Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, a servant girl came up to him and said, “You were with Jesus from Galilee.”
text: Ɔ 'ba yɔ sɔ'n, anɩn Pɩɛlɩ tɩ awulo nɩn abʋ ɛlɔ. Nyanmɩan *tɛɛyɩfʋɛ kpain nɩn afanɩan kʋn kɔ a Pɩɛlɩ anwʋn ɛbɛlɛ, yɩ́ nwan: “Wɔ́ kʋsʋ wɔ́, anɩn ɛ si Zozi m'ɔ fi Galile mân nɩn anun nɩn asʋ bie.”

ref: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
text: M'ɔ hanlɩn sɔ'n, Pɩɛlɩ suli akpʋlʋwa bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ́ nyunnun ɛbɛlɛ. Yɩ́ nwan: “Sʋanlan'n mɔ ɛ lɛ kan yɩ́ ɛjɔlɛ'n, mɩn nzɩ man yɩ́.”

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Ɔ hyɛlɩ kaan an, bɛ́ mɔ bɛ gua ɛbɛlɛ'n kɔ kpunnge Pɩɛlɩ, bɛ́ nwan: “Ɔ tɩ ananhɔlɛ, ɛ wɔ menian sɔ'n-mɔ anun bie. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ wɔ́ ɛjʋjɔlɛ'n kele lɩka mɔ ɛ fi'n.”

ref: and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, “Before a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.” Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
text: Pɩɛlɩ ati kɔ kacɩ ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ Zozi hanlɩn helelɩ yɩ́'n. Zozi nwan: “Ɔ nʋn dede mɔ akɔ'n 'bɔ'n, ɛ 'kan yɩ́ ngʋkʋn kpɛ̂ nsan kɛ ɛ nzɩ man mɩ́n.” M'ɔ yɔlɩ sɔ'n, Pɩɛlɩ fitelɩ ɔ sunlin sɛlɛ kpa.

ref: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
text: Ɔ kɔ bisa bɛ́ sɔ, afɩ ɔ sɩ yɩ́ fɔ́ʋ́n kɛ bɛ kpɔ Zozi, yɩ́ ti yɛ̂ b'a hyɩ yɩ́ b'a man yɩ́ ɔ.

ref: and said, “I have sinned by betraying a man who has never done anything wrong.” “So what? That's your problem,” they replied.
text: Yɩ́ nwan: “M'an yɔ ɛtɛ, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ m'an tʋn sʋanlan ɛsʋan maan b'a hun yɩ́.” Mɔ Zudasɩ hanlɩn sɔ'n, bɛ́ nwan: “Ɔ nvalɩ man yɛ́ nwʋn. Wɔ́ ngʋnmɩn wɔ́ ɛjɔlɛ ɔ!”

ref: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.
text: Bɛ hanlɩn sɔ mɔ bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ́ nʋan sɛlɩ yɩ́ nwʋn'n, bɛ falɩ esika sɔ'n bɛ tolɩ sʋanlan'n m'ɔ nwʋn ninnge nɩn ebo'n. Bɛ falɩ asɩɛ sɔ'n bɛ yɔlɩ nyɔfʋɛ asielɩɛ.

ref: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
text: Ɛhɩ ati, ɛnɛ nʋn ɛnɛ, bɛ tɛ fɛlɛ lɩka sɔ'n “Moja Ebo”.

ref: So the words of the prophet Jeremiah came true, “They took the thirty silver coins, the price of a person among the people of Israel.
text: Ɔ man ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ Nyanmɩan falɩ wulalɩ kpɔmanfʋɛ Zolomin anʋan'n kpɩnlɩn sʋ. Kpɔmanfʋɛ Zolomin nwan: Jɛtɛ fufue mma abulasan, esika mɔ Izalayɛ amma'n-mɔ anʋan sɛlɩ yɩ́ nwʋn bɛ falɩ bɛ lili yɩ́ gua nɩn anɩn.

ref: They paid it for a potter's field, as the Lord had commanded me.” Pilate Questions Jesus (Mark
text: Esika sɔ'n b'a fa b'a to sʋanlan'n m'ɔ nwʋn ninnge nɩn ebo'n kɛ yɛ́ Mɩn hanlɩn helelɩ mɩ́n'n. Pilatɩ 'ba bisa Zozi kosuan (Maakɩ

ref: Jesus was brought before Pilate the governor, who asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words!” Jesus answered.
text: Bɛ falɩ Zozi bɛ hɔlɩ kʋnmanna nɩn aja sʋ. Ɔ kɔ bisa Zozi kɛ: “Zufʋ'n-mɔ belemgbin'n y'ɔ le wɔ́ ɔ?” Zozi nwan: “Yɛ̂ wɔ́ nʋan wɔ sʋ ɛ lɛ kan nɩn ɔ.”

ref: And when the chief priests and leaders brought their charges against him, he did not say a thing.
text: Nan mɔ Nyanmɩan *tɛɛyɩfʋɛ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn Zufʋ mgbain'n-mɔ 'tʋn Zozi ɛsʋan'n, Zozi ngɔ tɛ man bɛ́ sʋ.

ref: But Jesus did not say anything, and the governor was greatly amazed. The Death Sentence (Mark
text: Zozi ngɔ bua ɛjɔlɛ kpɔlɛ kʋn sʋa. M'ɔ man ɛjɔlɛ'n sinlin kʋnmanna nɩn anwʋn kpili nɩn anɩn. Pilatɩ 'ba man bɛ kun Zozi (Maakɩ

ref: People who passed by said terrible things about Jesus. They shook their heads and
text: Bɛ́ mɔ bɛ 'sɩn ɛbɛlɛ'n, bɛ kɔsɔ kɔsɔ bɛ́ ti, anɩn bɛ lɛ kpɛ yɩ́ nzʋba.

ref: Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, “I won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!”
text: Anɩn menian'n-mɔ lɛ tɛtɩan sʋnman. Pilatɩ nɩan an, ɔ ngʋala man like fɩ́ɩ́ kʋ́n yɔ. Ɔ kɔ jasʋ ɔ kɔ fa nzue ɔ kɔ nwunnzin yɩ́ sa menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa anyunnun ɛbɛlɛ. Yɩ́ nwan: “Sʋanlan ɛhɩ ewue'n, mɩ́n nwʋn nnʋn man nun, m'an yɩ mɩ́n sa nun! Ɛmɔ angʋnmɩn, bɛ́ ɛjɔlɛ ɔ!”

ref: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
text: Bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ́ nwan: “Yuo o! Yɛ́ nʋn yɛ́ mma'n-mɔ, man yɩ́ ewue nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ kpólo yɛ́!”

ref: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
text: Bɛ kɔ nwʋn mmowe abotile bɛ kɔ bɔ yɩ́ ti. Bɛ kɔ fa ndɛɛ kpɔman bɛ kɔ wula yɩ́ sa fama nɩn anun. Mɔ bɛ yɔlɩ sɔ yuelɩ'n, bɛ kɔ kʋtʋ yɩ́ nyunnun ɛbɛlɛ bɛ kɔ yɛyala yɩ́ nwʋn. Bɛ́ nwan: “Zufʋ'n-mɔ belemgbin, ahɩn o!”

ref: He trusted God, so let God save him, if he wants to. He even said he was God's Son.”
text: Ɔ'a susu yɩ́ ti ɔ'a gua Nyanmɩan asʋ. Yɩ́ nwan: ‘Nyanmɩan Awa y'ɔ le mɩ́n’. Sɛ Nyanmɩan kulo yɩ́ a, anɩn ɔ lé yɩ́ ti kɩkaala!”

ref: When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross.
text: Bɛ yɛyalalɩ yɩ́ nwʋn dede mɔ bɛ́ kunnun jɔlɩ'n, bɛ kɔ yɩ́ taladɩɛ kɔkɔlɛ'n mɔ bɛ falɩ bɛ wulalɩ yɩ́'n. Bɛ kɔ fa yɩ́ muonun yɩ́ taladɩɛ dɩɛ'n bɛ kɔ wula yɩ́ yɛ̂ bɛ nʋn yɩ́ hɔlɩ bɛ bʋbɔlɩ yɩ́ kʋlʋwa sʋ ɔ. Bɛ 'ba bʋbɔ Zozi kʋlʋwa sʋ (Maakɩ

ref: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
text: Ɛbɛlɛ, bɛ kɔ fa divɩn bɛ kɔ man Zozi kɛ ɔ nʋ́n. Divɩn sɔ'n, bɛ gualɩ nun ayile m'ɔ nʋn an, ɔ nnwun man yalɛ'n sʋnman. Ɔ bɔlɩ sʋ, nan ɔ'a ngulo kɛ ɔ nʋn.

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
text: Mɔ bɛ bʋbɔlɩ yɩ́ kʋlʋwa nɩn asʋ yuelɩ'n, bɛ kɔ hyʋɩn tondo bɛ kɔ hye yɩ́ taladɩɛ'n-mɔ bɛ kɔ fa.

ref: Then they sat down to guard him.
text: Ɛsɛ bɛ kɔ tɛtanlan yɩ́ nwʋn ɛbɛlɛ bɛ kɔ sisa yɩ́.

ref: Above his head they put a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, “This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.”
text: Like mɔ yɩ́ ti yɛ̂ bɛ lɛ kun yɩ́'n, b'a hɛlɛ b'a bɔ yɩ́ ti sʋ ɛbɛlɛ kɛ: “Sʋanlan ɛhɩ li Zozi. Ɔ tɩ Zufʋ'n-mɔ belemgbin.”

ref: shouted, “So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days! If you are God's Son, save yourself and come down from the cross!”
text: Bɛ́ nwan: “Wɔ́ mɔ wɔ́ nwan ɛ 'bu asɔnɩn sua'n, nán ɛ fa cɩan nsan ɛ si yɩ́ fʋfɔlɛ'n, de wɔ́ nwʋn e! Sɛ wɔ́ nwan ɛ tɩ Nyanmɩan Awa sakpa a, fi kʋlʋwa nɩn asʋ ju!”

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
text: Nan ɛhɩnlɩn-mɔ nwan: “Jinlan maan yɛ nɩ́an sɛ Eli kɔ a kɔ le yɩ́ a!”

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
text: Mɔ sɛnzɛ'n jinlanlɩn bɛ́ ti afian'n, awosin wulalɩ mân nɩn anun lɩka kʋalaa dede ɔ juli nɔsʋba dɔɛ nsan.

ref: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
text: Mɔ bɛ 'kɔ a bɔ dɔɛ nsan'n, Zozi kɔ tɩan nun sɛlɛ kpa. Yɩ́ nwan: “Eli, Eli, lama sabakɩtanin?” Ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n, yɩ́ bʋ y'ɔ le kɛ “Mɩ́n Nyanmɩan, mɩ́n Nyanmɩan, nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ a yɩ mɩ́n ɔ?”

ref: One of them at once ran and grabbed a sponge. He soaked it in wine, then put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus.
text: Ɛbɛlɛ ala, bɛ́ nunhan kʋn kɔ nwanndi ɔ kɔ hɔ kɔ fa eminlan ɔ kɔ bɔ divɩn nyinnyanlɩnwa anun. Ɔ kɔ fa kɔ san baka kʋn ati ɔ kɔ tɩnngɛ yɩ́ Zozi anʋan kɛ ɔ nʋ́n.

ref: At once the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, and rocks split apart.
text: Mɛlɛ sɔ'n, mgbɛnun ɛtanlan'n m'ɔ ja asɔnɩn sua nɩn anun'n kɔ sua yɩ́ anwunno dede ɔ kɔ ju yɩ́ asɩ. Asɩɛ'n kɔ kpusu, nyɔbʋɛ mgbili'n-mɔ anun kɔ kpacɩ kpacɩ.

ref: Graves opened, and many of God's people were raised to life.
text: Sɛka'n-mɔ asʋ kɔ tike tike. Nyanmɩan anwʋntɩɛ mma'n-mɔ dɔʋn mɔ bɛ wuli'n kɔ tinnge tinnge.

ref: They left their graves, and after Jesus had risen to life, they went into the holy city, where they were seen by many people.
text: Mɔ Zozi fi ewue nun tinngelɩ'n, bɛ́ kʋalaa mɔ bɛ fi sɛka'n-mɔ anun bɛ fitelɩ'n, bɛ hɔlɩ kulo kpili nwannzan-nwannzan nɩn asʋ bɛ yɩlɩ bɛ́ nwʋn bɛ helelɩ menian dɔʋn kpa.

ref: The officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus felt the earthquake and saw everything else that happened. They were frightened and said, “This man really was God's Son!”
text: Mɔ nzalafʋ kpain'n nʋn yɩ́ nzalafʋ'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ́ nʋn yɩ́ lɛ sisa Zozi'n nwunlin kɛ asɩɛ nɩn a kpusu nʋn yɩ́ nzin ɛjɔlɛ'n, esulo hanlɩn bɛ́ sɛlɛ kpa. Bɛ́ nwan: “Kannzʋ ɔ tɩ Nyanmɩan Awa sakpa!”

ref: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
text: Anɩn mmalasua dɔʋn kpa kʋsʋ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ, bɛ jin mʋa bɛ lɛ nɩan Zozi. Bɛ́ yɛ̂ bɛ sili Zozi sʋ sunmin Galile mân nɩn anun bɛ sʋlɩ yɩ́ ɔ.

ref: went and asked for Jesus' body. Pilate gave orders for it to be given to Joseph,
text: Ɔ kɔ hɔ kɔ tʋ Pilatɩ ɔ kɔ sɛlɛ yɩ́ kɛ ɔ 'fa Zozi fuin'n ɔ 'kɔ a sie yɩ́. Ɛhɩ, Pilatɩ kɔ man atɩn kɛ bɛ fá bɛ mán yɩ́.

ref: who took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
text: Ɔ kɔ le fuin'n ɔ kɔ fa kisaan fʋfɔlɛ kʋn ɔ kɔ fʋfɔ yɩ́ nwʋn.

ref: Then Joseph put the body in his own tomb that had been cut into solid rock and had never been used. He rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb and went away.
text: Yɩ́ muonun yɩ́ sɛka mɔ ɔ'a man b'a kpɛ yɩ́ ɛbʋta kʋn anun'n, ɔ kɔ sie Zozi nun. M'ɔ sielɩ yɩ́ yuelɩ'n, ɔ kunndolɩ ɛbʋɛ kpili kʋn ɔ nyinlin sɛka nɩn anʋan'n yɛ̂ ɔ salɩ hɔlɩ ɔ.

ref: All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
text: Anɩn Mali m'ɔ fi Magɩdala'n, ɔ nʋn Mali kʋn'n bɛ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ. Bɛ tɩtɩ asɩ sɛka nɩn anyunnun. Sɛka nɩn esisa'n

ref: On the next day, which was a Sabbath, the chief priests and the Pharisees went together to Pilate.
text: Zufʋ'n-mɔ bɔbʋalɩ bɛ́ nwʋn bɛ sielɩ. Mɔ yɩ́ alɩ́ɛ hɩnlɩn m'ɔ tɩ ɛnwʋnmɩan ele cɩan'n, Nyanmɩan *tɛɛyɩfʋɛ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn *Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ kɔ hɔ kɔ tʋ Pilatɩ. Bɛ́ nwan:

ref: They said, “Sir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death.
text: “Nannan, akʋa atofʋɛ sɔ nɩn ɛjɔlɛ kʋn mɔ anɩn yɩ́ nyɩn wɔ sʋ m'ɔ hanlɩn'n, yɛ́ ti a kpɩn sʋ. Yɩ́ nwan: ‘Sɛ mɩn wu a, yɩ́ cɩan nsan, mɩn 'tinnge.’

ref: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
text: Yɩ́ ti, man atɩn maan bɛ hɔ́ sísa sɛka'n dede cɩan nsan. Ɔ'a nyɔ man sɔ a, yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ 'kɔ a sa yɩ́ bɛ laka menian'n-mɔ kɛ: ‘Ɔ fi ewue nun ɔ'a tinnge!’ Afɩ, sian ato mɔ bɛ 'di'n, ɔ 'tala daba dɩɛ'n.”

ref: Pilate said to them, “All right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.”
text: Ɛhɩ, Pilatɩ nwan: “Ɛ 'nwun nzalafʋ, Ɛmɔ fá bɛ́ hɔ́, maan bɛ hɔ́ sísa yɩ́ kɛ mɔ ɛmɔ kulo ɔ.”

ref: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi menian bulu nʋn kʋn'n hɔlɩ Galile mân nɩn anun ɛlɔ bʋka'n mɔ Zozi helelɩ bɛ́ nɩn asʋ.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
text: Nyanmɩan tɛɛyɩfʋɛ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn Zufʋ mgbain'n-mɔ kɔ yia. Mɔ bɛ́ nʋan sɛlɩ bɛ́ nwʋn yuelɩ'n, bɛ kɔ man nzalafʋ'n-mɔ esika dɔʋn kpa.

ref: and teach them to do everything I have told you. I will be with you always, even until the end of the world.
text: Ninnge'n kʋalaa mɔ m'an han m'an hele ɛmɔ'n, ɛmɔ héhele bɛ́ maan bɛ yɔ́. Ɛsɛ ɛmɔ nwún yɩ́ kɛ mɩn nʋn ɛmɔ san alɩhɩan biala dede ɔ kɔ ju mân nɩn ayuelɩɛ.”

ref: Then Jesus said, “Don't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.” Report of the Guard
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi kɔ se bɛ́ kɛ: “Nán bɛ sulo. Ɛmɔ hɔ́ hán héle mɩ́n nianman'n-mɔ kɛ bɛ hɔ́ Galile mân nɩn anun ɛlɔ. Ɛbɛlɛ yɛ̂ bɛ 'nwun mɩ́n ɔ.” Nzalafʋ'n-mɔ ɛjɔlɛ'n

ref: Suddenly a strong earthquake struck, and the Lord's angel came down from heaven. He rolled away the stone and sat on it.
text: Kʋnmgba ala, asɩɛ'n kɔ kpusu sɛlɛ kpa. Yɛ́ Mɩn bɔfʋɛ'n kʋn fi anwunno ɔ kɔ ju. Ɔ kɔ kunndo ɛbʋɛ'n m'ɔ nyin sɛka nɩn anʋan'n ɔ kɔ tʋ yɩ́ ahanmɩan ɔ kɔ fʋ ɔ kɔ tanlan sʋ.

ref: The angel looked as bright as lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
text: Bɔfʋɛ nɩn anwʋn'n ta sʋa mɩaɩn mɩaɩn kɛ nyanmɩan'n m'ɔ kpɛ mɩaɩn mɩaɩn'n. Yɩ́ taladɩɛ'n tɩ fufue hyɩɛɩn hyɩɛɩn kpa.

ref: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
text: Esulo kɔ han nzalafʋ'n-mɔ kpa dede bɛ́ nwʋn kɔ wʋsʋ bɛ́, bɛ gugua kɛ menian mɔ b'a wu ɔ.

ref: The angel said to the women, “Don't be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus, who was nailed to a cross.
text: Bɔfʋɛ'n kɔ han kɔ hele mmalasua'n-mɔ kɛ: “Nán bɛ sulo. Afɩ mɩn sɩ kɛ ɛmɔ lɛ kpʋnndɛ Zozi, sʋanlan'n mɔ bɛ bʋbɔlɩ yɩ́ kʋlʋwa nɩn asʋ'n.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: Ɛmɔ hɔ́ ndɛ-ndɛ bɛ hán héle yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ ɔ fi ewue nun ɔ'a tinnge. Sian dɩɛ, ɔ lɛ kɔ a nwʋnndɛ ɛmɔ Galile mân nɩn anun. Ɛlɔ yɛ̂ ɛmɔ 'nwun yɩ́ ɔ. Ngalɩɛ'n mɔ mɩ́n nwan mɩn 'kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ nɩn anɩn.”

ref: While the women were on their way, some soldiers who had been guarding the tomb went into the city. They told the chief priests everything that had happened.
text: Mɔ anɩn mmalasua'n-mɔ aja wɔ atunnun'n, nzalafʋ'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ lɛ sisa sɛka'n, mmie-mɔ kɔ sa kɔ hɔ kulo. Bɛ kɔ hɔ kɔ bɔ ɛjɔlɛ'n m'ɔ walɩ nɩn anun amannɩɛ bɛ kɔ hele Nyanmɩan *tɛɛyɩfʋɛ mgbain'n-mɔ.

ref: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
text: Ɛsɛ bɛ́ nwan: “Sɛ bɛ bisa ɛmɔ a, ɛmɔ hán kɛ kɔngɔɛ mɔ ɛmɔ 'dafɩ'n, yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ a a a sa yɩ́ a hɔ.

ref: The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Mark
text: Nzalafʋ'n-mɔ lelɩ esika'n, bɛ hanlɩn yɩ́ kɛ Nyanmɩan tɛɛyɩfʋɛ mgbain'n-mɔ hanlɩn helelɩ bɛ́ nɩn ala. Ɛnɛ nʋn ɛnɛ, Zufʋ'n-mɔ tɛ kan ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n. Zozi 'ba yɩ yɩ́ nwʋn kele yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ (Maakɩ

ref: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
text: Zozi kɔ kpunnge bɛ́, ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele bɛ kɛ: “B'a fa anwunno ɛlɔ nʋn asɩ ɛwa tunmin'n kʋalaa b'a wula mɩ́n sa nun.

ref: Go to the people of all nations and make them my disciples. Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit,
text: Ɛhɩka ati, ɛmɔ hɔ́ mân nɩn anun menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa anwʋn, ɛmɔ mán bɛ káci mɩ́n menian. Ɛmɔ sɔ́nɩn bɛ́ wɔ Sɩɛ nʋn Baa, ɔ nʋn Wawɛ Nwannzan-nwannzan'n dunman nun.

ref: They saw him and worshiped him, but some of them doubted.
text: Mɔ bɛ nwunlin yɩ́'n, bɛ kʋtʋlɩ bɛ sʋlɩ yɩ́. Nan kʋsʋ bɛ́ mmie-mɔ a nne a nni man.

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: Kɛ Zozi anʋan 'tɔ ala, bɛ kɔ yaci bɛ́ dada'n-mɔ ɛbɛlɛ, bɛ kɔ si yɩ́ sʋ.

ref: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
text: Zozi fi ɛbɛlɛ, ɔ kɔ hɔ yɩ́ nyunnun kaan, ɔ kɔ nwun Zebede amma'n-mɔ: Zʋakɩ nʋn yɩ́ nianman Zʋan. Bɛ gua bɛ́ ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun, bɛ lɛ kpʋnmgba bɛ́ dada'n-mɔ anun.

ref: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Ɔ fi ɛbɛlɛ, Zozi dunman'n kɔ hɔ dede kɔ ju Galile mân'n lɩka kʋalaatin. Zozi 'ba man nwuluwafʋɛ dɔʋn anwʋn sa (Matie

ref: It began just as God had said in the book written by Isaiah the prophet, “I am sending my messenger to get the way ready for you.
text: Ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n bɔlɩ yɩ́ bʋ kɛ Nyanmɩan Kpɔmanfʋɛ Ezayi hɛlɛlɩ wɔ yɩ́ kalata nɩn anun'n. Ɔ'a hɛlɛ Zozi anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n kɛ Nyanmɩan nwan: Nɩan, mɩn 'sʋan mɩ́n bɔfʋɛ'n wɔ́ nyunnun mɩn 'man ɔ di mʋa ɔ sesie wɔ́ atɩn'n.

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Taladɩɛ'n mɔ Zʋan wula'n, sʋamo enyuan yɛ̂ b'a fa b'a nwʋn ɔ. Yɩ́ bɛlɛtɩ'n tɩ naan kpolo. Alɩɛ'n m'ɔ di'n, y'ɔ le tɛtɛbʋabʋa nʋn wɛ.

ref: John also told the people, “Someone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.
text: Ɛ 'nwun ɛjɔlɛ'n m'ɔ kan kele menian'n-mɔ'n. Yɩ́ nwan: “Sʋanlan kʋn fi mɩ́n nzin ɔ lɛ ba, ɔ le tunmin tala mɩ́n, mɩn nzɛ nvata man kɛ mɩn kʋtʋ mɩn nyannjɩ yɩ́ ja mgbabʋa'n nyanman'n bɔbɔ mgban ɔ.

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
text: Bɛ fi ɛbɛlɛ, Zozi nʋn bɛ́ kɔ hɔ kulo'n mɔ bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ Kapɛɛnawɔmʋn nɩn asʋ. Bɛ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ dede mɔ ɛnwʋnmɩan ele cɩan'n juli'n, ɔ nʋn bɛ́ kɔ hɔ Zufʋ'n-mɔ asɔnɩn sua nɩn anun. Ɔ kɔ hehele bagua'n Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ.

ref: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Tunmin'n m'ɔ fa kekele ninnge'n-mɔ'n sin menian'n-mɔ anwʋn. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ɔ ngele man yɩ́ ninnge'n-mɔ kɛ mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ dɩɛ'n, nan ɔ kele yɩ́ ninnge'n-mɔ tunmin sʋ.

ref: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
text: Mɛlɛ sɔ nɩn anun, Zozi fi Nazalɛtɩ, Galile mân nɩn anun, ɔ kɔ a kɔ tʋ Zʋan maan ɔ kɔ sɔnɩn yɩ́ asue Zuludɩn nɩn anun.

ref: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
text: Mɔ Zozi fi asue nɩn anun ɔ 'fite'n, ɔ 'nɩan an, anɩn anwunno nɩn a buke, Wawɛ Nwannzan-nwannzan'n tɩ kɛ abubule, ɔ fi nyanmɩansʋ ɔ kɔ ju kɔ tanlan yɩ́ sʋ.

ref: A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, “You have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Ɛlɛhʋn kʋn, kokobefʋɛ kʋn kɔ a kɔ tʋ Zozi ɔ kɔ tu kɔ tɔ yɩ́ ja nun. Ɔ kɔ bʋtʋ yɩ́. Yɩ́ nwan: “Mɩ́n mɩn, mɩn sɩ kɛ sɛ ɛ kulo a, ɛ kʋala kɛ ɛ man mɩ́n nwʋn sa, ɛ tu mɩ́n nwʋn efian'n.”

ref: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
text: M'ɔ juli ɛlɔ'n yɩ́ nwan: “Tɛmʋn nɩn a ju, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ Nyanmɩan Belemgbin elie mɛlɛ nɩn a kpunnge. Ɛmɔ lé Ɛjɔlɛkpa'n bɛ li nán ɛmɔ káci bɛ́ abalabɔ'n!”

ref: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Ɛbɛlɛ ala, Zozi kɔ fɛlɛ bɛ́. Bɛ kɔ yaci bɛ́ sɩ Zebede ɔ nʋn kpafʋ'n-mɔ ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun ɛbɛlɛ, bɛ kɔ si yɩ́ sʋ. Zozi 'ba fʋan wawɛ ɛtɛ fi sʋanlan kʋn anun (Luku

ref: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
text: Zozi kɔ hyʋɩn kɔ kpunnge yɩ́, ɔ kɔ sɔ yɩ́ sa nɩn anun ɔ kɔ jasʋ yɩ́. Ɛbɛlɛ ala, ebunnun'n fi yɩ́ nwʋn kɔ tu. Ɔ kɔ jasʋ, ɔ kɔ man bɛ́ like bɛ kɔ li.

ref: And when they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you!”
text: Bɛ kpʋnndɛlɩ yɩ́ dede mɔ bɛ nwunlin yɩ́'n, bɛ́ nwan: “Yɛ́ Mɩn, menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa lɛ kpʋnndɛ wɔ́.”

ref: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
text: Ɔ 'ba yɔ sɔ'n kʋsʋ, anɩn sʋanlan kʋn wɔ bɛ́ asɔnɩn sua nɩn anun ɛbɛlɛ. Sʋanlan sɔ'n, wawɛ ɛtɛ wɔ yɩ́ nun. Ɔ kɔ tɩan nun kɛ:

ref: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
text: “Zozi m'ɔ fi Nazalɛtɩ, ɛ 'se yɛ́ sɛ? Ɛ walɩ yɛ́ bʋsʋ ɛbɔ anaan? Mɩn sɩ wɔ́ bɔkɔɔ! Ɛ tɩ Nyanmɩan Sʋanlan Nwannzan-nwannzan!”

ref: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
text: Mɔ sʋanlan'n hanlɩn sɔ'n, Zozi kɔ tɩan wawɛ ɛtɛ nɩn asʋ sɛlɛ kpa. Yɩ́ nwan: “Muan wɔ́ nʋan'n nán fi sʋanlan ɛhɩ anun fite!”

ref: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
text: Kɛ Zozi 'kan sɔ ala, wawɛ ɛtɛ'n kɔ kpusu sʋanlan'n sɛlɛ sɛlɛ kpa, ɔ kɔ tɩan, ɔ fi yɩ́ nun kɔ fite.

ref: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
text: Sian'n, kɛ ɔ yɔlɩ sɔ'n, esulo kɔ han bɛ́ mɔ bɛ wɔ asɔnɩn sua nɩn anun'n kʋalaatin. Bɛ lɛ bisa bisa bɛ́ nwʋn kɛ: “Nan nzu like dɩɛ ahɩ? Tunmin sʋ like ehele fʋfɔlɛ ɔ! M'ɔ kɔ han'n, wawɛ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ di sʋ, ɛsɛ bɛ man yɩ́ anyɩnnzɔ.”

ref: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
text: Zozi kukuli yɩ́ nʋan sʋ a, yɩ́ nwan: “Ɛmɔ mán yɛ hɔ́ kulo'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ mantan mantan ɛwa'n-mɔ asʋ. Ɔ fata kɛ mɩn kan Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ ɛlɔhan kʋsʋ. Ɛhɩ ati yɛ̂ ɔ man n vi kulo nɩn anun m'an fite ɔ.”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
text: Mɔ Zozi nwunlin yɩ́ sɔ'n, kokobefʋɛ nɩn anwʋn kɔ yɔ yɩ́ koun. Ɛbɛlɛ ala, ɔ kɔ tɩnngɛ yɩ́ sa kɔ han kokobefʋɛ'n, yɩ́ nwan: “Mɩn kulo, man wɔ́ nwʋn tɩ́!”

ref: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
text: Mɔ Bɛ fi Nyanmɩan sua nɩn anun bɛ fitelɩ'n, ɛbɛlɛ ala, bɛ kɔ hɔ Simʋn nʋn Andele awulo. Mɔ bɛ 'kɔ'n, bɛ nʋn Zʋakɩ nʋn Zʋan yɛ̂ bɛ hɔlɩ ɔ.

ref: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Bɛ 'ju ɛbɛlɛ a, anɩn Simʋn asebala nɩn a fʋkɛ, ebunnun a hyɩ yɩ́, ɔ la. Ɛbɛlɛ ala, bɛ kɔ bɔ Zozi amannɩɛ.

ref: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
text: Bɛ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ dede eyua'n tɔlɩ, mɔ mân nɩn anun 'yɔ fɔʋn'n, menian'n-mɔ kɔ sʋa nwuluwafʋɛ ngacile, nʋn bɛ́ mɔ wawɛ ɛtɛ wɔ bɛ́ nun'n, bɛ kɔ bɛlɛ Zozi.

ref: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
text: Kulo nɩn asʋ menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa kɔ yia awulo nɩn anʋan nɩn anun.

ref: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
text: Zozi kɔ man nwuluwafʋɛ dɔʋn anwʋn kɔ sa, ɛsɛ ɔ kɔ fʋan wawɛ ɛtɛ dɔʋn. Ɔ ngɔ man man wawɛ ɛtɛ'n bie fɩ́ɩ́ atɩn, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ wawɛ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ sɩ yɩ́ sʋanlan ɛhɔlɛ kʋn'n. Zozi 'ba kpanza Galile mân nɩn anun (Luku

ref: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, mɔ alɩ́ɛ'n 'kɔ alɩbahɩan'n, anɩn mân nɩn anun tɛ du bɔbɔ, Zozi kɔ jasʋ. Ɔ fi awulo ɛbɛlɛ kɔ fite kɔ hɔ asɔnɩn ɛyɔlɛ ahanmɩan kulo'n tiba ɛlɔ.

ref: Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
text: Zozi le yɩ́ sɔ dede ɔ kɔ kpanza Galile mân'n lɩka kʋalaatin. Ɔ kɔ hɔ kɔ bɔ Ɛjɔlɛkpa'n kɔ hele menian'n-mɔ wɔ Zufʋ'n-mɔ asɔnɩn sua'n-mɔ anun. Ɛsɛ ɔ kɔ fʋan wawɛ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ. Zozi 'ba man kokobefʋɛ kʋn anwʋn sa (Matie

ref: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ nɩn ala, kokobe'n fi yɩ́ nwʋn kɔ tu, yɩ́ nwʋn kɔ tɩ.

ref: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
text: Mɔ bian nɩn anwʋn salɩ yuelɩ'n, Zozi kɔ jʋjɔ aminle sʋ kɔ hele yɩ́ asannan nán ɔ'a man ɔ'a hɔ.

ref: He said, “Don't tell anyone about this. Just go and show the priest that you are well. Then take a gift to the temple as Moses commanded, and everyone will know that you have been healed.”
text: Zozi nwan: “Mɔ a nwun yɩ́ ɛwa'n, n'ɛ kan nun ɛjɔlɛ fɩ́ɩ́ kele sʋanlan. Nan kɔ fa wɔ́ nwʋn kele Nyanmɩan tɛɛyɩfʋɛ'n nán yɩ wɔ́ nwʋn efian etue tɛɛ'n mɔ Moyizɩ mala'n kele'n maan bɛ nwún yɩ́ kɛ wɔ́ nwʋn a tɩ.”

ref: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
text: Nan mɔ bian'n hɔlɩ'n, ɔ'a ngʋala man ɛjɔlɛ nɩn asʋ fɩa. Ɔ kɔ bɔ yɩ́ kpayɛ kɔ hele mân'n kʋalaa. Sian'n, kɛ ɔ yɔlɩ sɔ'n, Zozi kɔ kulo'n-mɔ asʋ a, meninsʋnman'n dunman nun, ɔ ka ahanmɩan, lɩka mɔ sʋanlan nnʋn man'n, ɛbɛlɛ yɛ̂ menian'n-mɔ fi lɩka kʋalaa bɛ ba yɩ́ nwʋn ɔ.

ref: It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.”
text: Tɛɛyɩfʋɛ kpain Abiataa mɛlɛ sʋ, ɔ'a nwʋlʋ man Nyanmɩan sua nɩn anun, kpaʋn'n mɔ b'a fa b'a man Nyanmɩan'n, ɔ a nni man bie, ɔ'a nva bie ɔ'a man yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ b'a nni man ɔ? Nan kʋsʋ kpaʋn sɔ'n, sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ le man atɩn kɛ ɔ di ɔ, sannan Nyanmɩan tɛɛyɩfʋɛ'n-mɔ ala yɛ̂ bɛ kʋala kɛ bɛ di ɔ.”

ref: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
text: Menian dɔʋn kpa kɔ a kɔ yi sua'n nʋn anʋan nɩn anun dede bɛ nnwun man lɩka fɩ́ɩ́ mɔ bɛ sie bɛ́ nwʋn ɔ. Zozi kɔ han Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ'n kɔ hele bɛ́.

ref: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
text: Mɔ Zozi 'kan Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ'n, menian mmie-mɔ kɔ a kɔ ju ɛbɛlɛ. Bɛ́ nunhan nnan sʋ bubuluwafʋɛ kʋn, bɛ lɛ fa yɩ́ bɛ bɛlɛ Zozi.

ref: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.
text: Eyia kpili mɔ menian'n-mɔ a yia nɩn ati, bɛ ngʋala man wuluwafʋɛ'n fa fite Zozi anyunnun. Ɛhɩ a, bɛ kɔ fʋ sua'n mɔ Zozi wɔ nun nɩn asʋ, bɛ kɔ kpɛ nun tokolo. Bɛ kɔ fa bubuluwafʋɛ'n nʋn yɩ́ ɛbɛɛ'n bɛ kɔ wula tokolo sɔ nɩn anun bɛ kɔ jula yɩ́ Zozi anyunnun ɛbɛlɛ asɩ.

ref: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
text: “Nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ sʋanlan ɛhɩ kan ɛjɔlɛ ɛtɛ sʋa tia Nyanmɩan ɔ? Sɛ nán Nyanmɩan angʋnmɩn cein an, nwan yɛ̂ ɔ kʋala ɔ fa ɛtɛ ce ɔ?”

ref: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,
text: anɩn ɛmɔ a nwun yɩ́ kɛ Mân Baa'n le tunmin asɩɛ nɩn asʋ ɛwa ɔ fa ɛtɛ'n-mɔ ce.” Ɔ kacili yɩ́ nʋan bubuluwafʋɛ nɩn anwʋn ɛlɔ a, yɩ́ nwan:

ref: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: “M'an man wɔ́ atɩn, jasʋ, fa wɔ́ ɛbɛɛ'n kɔ awulo.”

ref: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Zozi kɔ sa kɔ hɔ Galile asubula nɩn anʋan ɛlɔ biekun. Menian dɔʋn kɔ kpʋ yɩ́, ɔ kɔ hehele bɛ́ ninnge.

ref: Jesus finished by saying, “People were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people.
text: Zozi kɔ tʋ sʋ biekun kɛ: “Bɛ yɔlɩ ɛnwʋnmɩan ele cɩan'n bɛ manlɩn sʋanlan, nan b'a nyɔ man sʋanlan b'a mman man ɛnwʋnmɩan ele cɩan'n.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, cɩan kʋn Zozi kɔ hɔ like elie Levi awulo ɛlɔ. Mɔ ɔ 'kɔ'n, ɔ nʋn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ nʋn menian dɔʋn mɔ bɛ dide ajule'n nʋn ɛtɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ yɛ̂ bɛ hɔlɩ ɔ. Afɩ bɛ́ mɔ bɛ si Zozi sʋ'n, bɛ sʋnlɩn.

ref: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text: Mɔ mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ wɔ Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ fâ dɩɛ nɩn anun'n nwunlin kɛ Zozi nʋn menian ɛhɩ-mɔ kʋalaa lɛ didi'n, bɛ kɔ bisa Zozi menian'n-mɔ kɛ: “Nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ Zozi nʋn bɛ́ mɔ bɛ dide ajule'n nʋn ɛtɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ lɛ didi'n?”

ref: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Cɩan kʋn, anɩn Zʋan Batisi menian'n-mɔ nʋn Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ bɛ lɛ di abuala. Menian mmie-mɔ walɩ tʋlɩ Zozi a, bɛ kɔ bisa yɩ́ kɛ: “Nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ Zʋan menian'n-mɔ nʋn Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ dɩɛ'n-mɔ lɛ di abuala, nan kʋsʋ wɔ́ dɩɛ'n-mɔ nni man bie ɔ?”

ref: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Mɛlɛ'n bie 'ba ju, bɛ 'ba yɩ atɔnvɔlɛ bian'n fi bɛ́ sa nun. Cɩan sɔ'n yɛ̂ bɛ́ kʋsʋ bɛ 'ba li abuala ɔ.

ref: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: Sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ nva man ndafuin fʋfɔlɛ ɔ nda man ɛtanlan daba nun. Sɛ ɔ yɔ yɩ́ sɔ a, ndafuin fʋfɔlɛ'n 'sua ɛtanlan daba'n. Sian'n, yɩ́ bɔɛ'n 'yɔ kpili tala daba dɩɛ'n.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
text: Sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ nva man divɩn fʋfɔlɛ ɔ ngua man naan kpolo kotoku daba nun. Sɛ ɔ yɔ yɩ́ sɔ a, kpolo'n 'sua nzan'n butu, anɩn yɩ́ munnyuan nɩn a sɛcɩ. Nan kpolo kotoku fʋfɔlɛ anun yɛ̂ bɛ gua divɩn fʋfɔlɛ ɔ.” Zozi nʋn ɛnwʋnmɩan ele cɩan'n (Matie

ref: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, cɩan kʋn, *ɛnwʋnmɩan ele cɩan nɩn anun, Zozi kɔ kpɛ nun *bele ebo mmie-mɔ asʋ. Cɩan sɔ'n, yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ tɩtɩ atɩn nɩn anʋan bele'n bie kɔ li.

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
text: Ɛhɩ a, Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ kɔ bisa Zozi kɛ: “Nɩan, nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ wɔ́ menian'n-mɔ yɔ like mɔ mala'n tua kɛ nán bɛ yɔ yɩ́ ɛnwʋnmɩan ele cɩan nɩn ɔ?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Haven't you read what David did when he and his followers were hungry and in need?
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi kɔ bua bɛ́ kɛ: “Mɔ ɛhɔɛ hunlin Davidi nʋn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ, mɔ anɩn bɛ le man like fɩ́ɩ́ bɛ nni man'n, like mɔ bɛ yɔlɩ'n, ɛmɔ kɩnngalɩ man yɩ́ le ɔ?

ref: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
text: Yɩ́ sɔ ati, Mân Baa'n tɩ ɛnwʋnmɩan ele cɩan'n muonun asʋ mɩnlɩan.”

ref: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi kɔ se yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ bɛ kpʋ́nndɛ ɛlɛɛ kʋn bɛ mán yɩ́ maan ɔ tɩ́ yɩ́ nwʋn kaan, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ menian'n-mɔ a mgbunmgbun yɩ́ sʋ sʋnman.

ref: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
text: Mɔ Zozi 'wʋlʋ asɔnɩn sua nɩn anun'n, anɩn Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ. Bɛ gua ɛbɛlɛ bɛ lɛ nɩan sɛ Zozi kɔ man wuluwafʋɛ nɩn anwʋn kɔ sa ɛnwʋnmɩan ele cɩan nɩn an. Afɩ bɛ kulo kɛ bɛ tʋn yɩ́ ɛsʋan.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
text: Mɔ Zozi nwunlin yɩ́ sɔ'n, ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele wuluwafʋɛ'n kɛ: “Njabian, jasʋ jinlan bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ́ nyunnun ɛwa maan bɛ nwún wɔ́.”

ref: Jesus was angry as he looked around at the people. Yet he felt sorry for them because they were so stubborn. Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his bad hand was healed.
text: Bɛ́ ti anun ɛtɛ'n nʋn bɛ nyɔlɩɛ sɔ nɩn anwʋn, ɛya kɔ hun Zozi ɛsɛ yɩ́ lʋa kɔ bɔ yɩ́ nwʋn. Ɛhɩ a, ɔ kɔ nɩan bɛ́ kʋalaa kɔ si kɔ yia. Ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele wuluwafʋɛ'n kɛ: “Tɩnngɛ wɔ́ sa nɩn anun.” Ɔ 'tɩnngɛ yɩ́ sa nɩn anun an, asaa kɔ yɔ kpa.

ref: Jesus led his disciples down to the shore of the lake. Large crowds followed him from Galilee, Judea,
text: M'ɔ yɔlɩ sɔ'n, Zozi nʋn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ bɛ hyʋɩnlɩn bɛ́ nzin bɛ hɔlɩ Galile asue nɩn anʋan ɛlɔ. Menian dɔʋn kɔ si yɩ́ sʋ.

ref: Simon was one of the twelve, and Jesus named him Peter.
text: Menian bulu nʋn nnyuan'n mɔ Zozi fɛlɛlɩ bɛ́ yɩ́ nwʋn ɔ manlɩn bɛ́ junman'n, bɛ́ ahɩ: Simʋn mɔ Zozi tʋnlɩn yɩ́ Pɩɛlɩ'n,

ref: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles. The others were Simon, known as the Eager One,
text: Andele, Filipʋ, Baatelemin, Matie, Toma, Alɩfe awa Zʋakɩ, Tade, Simʋn m'ɔ kpʋnndɛ yɩ́ mân'n fanwʋndi'n,

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Ɛhɩ, mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ fi Zoluzalɛmʋn bɛ walɩ a, bɛ́ nwan: “Bian ɛhɩ, *Bɛlɩzebulu m'ɔ tɩ mmɔnsanmʋn'n-mɔ kpain'n wɔ yɩ́ nun. Yɩ́ y'ɔ man yɩ́ anwʋnsɛlɛ m'ɔ fa tu wawɛ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ ɔ.”

ref: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
text: Anɩn menian dɔʋn kpa tɩtɩ asɩ, b'a ta b'a si b'a yia Zozi. Bɛ kɔ han bɛ kɔ hele yɩ́ kɛ: “Nɩan, wɔ́ nin nʋn wɔ́ nianman'n-mɔ jijin ahanmɩan ɛlɔ, bɛ lɛ kpʋnndɛ wɔ́.”

ref: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
text: Mɔ Zozi tɩlɩ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n, ɔ kɔ fɛlɛ bɛ́, ɔ kɔ bubu anyɩnndala-mɔ kɔ hehele bɛ́. Yɩ́ nwan: “*Satan 'yɔ sɛ nán ɔ'a hʋala Satan tu?

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
text: Sɛ belemgbin mân kʋn anun menian'n-mɔ bu bɛ́ nwʋn nun an, mân sɔ'n 'bɔ asɩ.

ref: And a family that fights won't last long either.
text: Ɛsɛ awulo kʋn kʋsʋ mɔ nun menian'n-mɔ nni man anʋannzɛ nan bɛ kpɔ bɛ́ nwʋn'n, awulo sɔ'n 'bɔ.

ref: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
text: Sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ ngʋala man kɛ ɔ wʋlʋ sʋanlan m'ɔ tɩ akpɔlɛ nɩn awulo yɩ́ nyɩnsʋ ɔ kɔ wua yɩ́, abɛlɛ m'ɔ cɩcɩlɩ man yɩ́'n. Sannan ɔ 'di mʋa ɔ cɩcɩ yɩ́ kʋalaaka nán ɔ'a hʋala ɔ'a wua yɩ́.

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
text: Mɩɩn kan yɩ́ ananhɔlɛ mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ bɛ 'fa menian'n-mɔ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ kʋalaa ɔ nʋn bɛ́ ɛjʋjɔlɛ ɛtɛ'n bɛ ce bɛ́.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Nan sʋanlan biala m'ɔ kɔ jʋjɔ kɔ tia Nyanmɩan Wawɛ'n, ɔ nnyan man face. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ anɩn ɔ'a yɔ ɛtɛ m'ɔ le man ayuelɩɛ.”

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
text: Zozi hanlɩn sɔ ɛjɔlɛ'n ɔ helelɩ bɛ́, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ bɛ́ nwan: “Wawɛ ɛtɛ wɔ yɩ́ nun.” Zozi anianman'n-mɔ nʋn yɩ́ nin (Matie

ref: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
text: Ɛbɛlɛ ala, yɩ́ nianman'n-mɔ nʋn bɛ́ nin kɔ ju ɛbɛlɛ. Bɛ kɔ ha ahanmɩan, bɛ kɔ yɩ sʋanlan kɛ ɔ hɔ́ fɛ́lɛ yɩ́ mán bɛ́.

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Mɔ Zozi tɩlɩ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n, yɩ́ nwan: “Mɩ́n nin'n nʋn mɩ́n nianman'n-mɔ y'ɔ le nwan-mɔ?”

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: M'ɔ hanlɩn sɔ'n, ɔ kɔ nɛnɩan bɛ́ mɔ bɛ tɩtɩ asɩ b'a ta b'a si b'a yia yɩ́'n, yɩ́ nwan: “Mɩ́n nin nʋn mɩ́n nianman'n-mɔ y'ɔ le bɛ́ mɔ bɛ gua ɛwa'n.

ref: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi fi ɛbɛlɛ yɩ́ nʋan y'ɔ le Galile asue nɩn anʋan ɛlɔ biekun. Ɔ 'kɔ a hehele menian'n-mɔ Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ'n. Menian dɔʋn kpa walɩ yialɩ yɩ́ nwʋn. Kɛmɔ menian'n-mɔ sʋnlɩn nɩn ati, ɔ kɔ fʋ kɔ tanlan ɛlɛɛ kʋn anun asue nɩn anyɩnsʋ. Meninsʋnman'n kɔ ha asue nɩn anʋan.

ref: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
text: Nan tɛmʋn mɔ ɔ 'gua bele'n, bie kɔ gua atɩn nɩn anʋan. Nnʋnman'n-mɔ kɔ a kɔ suso kɔ li.

ref: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
text: Zozi kɔ hehele bɛ́ ninnge dɔʋn kpa anyɩnndala nun. Mɔ ɔ 'kekele bɛ́ ninnge'n, yɩ́ nwan:

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: “Ɛmɔ tíe! Anɩn sʋanlan kʋn wɔ ɛbɛlɛ. Cɩan kʋn, ɔ hɔlɩ kɛ ɔ 'kɔ a gua yɩ́ *bele .

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Bie kʋsʋ kɔ gua nyɔbʋɛ nɩn asʋ. Ɛbɛlɛ ngɛtɩɛ nɩn a nzʋn man. Ɔ kɔ fifi ndɛ-ndɛ, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ ɔ gualɩ ngɛtɩɛ kpa kaan'n m'ɔ wɔ ɛbʋɛ nɩn ati sʋ nɩn asʋ.

ref: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots.
text: Nan mɔ eyua'n fili sɛlɛ'n, ɛbɛlɛ dɩɛ'n kɔ yala. Afɩ, yɩ́ ndɩan nɩn a ngɔ man asɩɛ bʋ.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
text: Bele'n bie kɔ gua eminlan mmowe'n-mɔ anun. Eminlan mmowe sɔ'n-mɔ nʋn bele'n kɔ fifi. Nan mɔ bele'n nyinlin'n, eminlan mmowe'n-mɔ kɔ fʋ kɔ gua sʋ. Bele'n ngɔ su man mma fɩ́ɩ́.

ref: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
text: Nan kʋsʋ, ɔ nzɔ man ndɩan bɛ́ nun. Bɛ tɩ kɛ cɩan kʋn menian. Sɛ yalɛ anaan ahʋlʋwa tʋ bɛ́ wɔ Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ'n dunman nun an, bɛ yaci bɛ dedi'n mɔ bɛ le yɩ́'n bɛ tʋ ndɛ-ndɛ.

ref: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
text: afɩ man Bɛ nɩ́an yɩ́ kpa bɔbɔ a, bɛ nnwun man yɩ́. Ɛsɛ man bɛ tíe yɩ́ kpa bɔbɔ a, bɛ ndɩ man yɩ́ bʋ. Sɛ ɔ tɩ man sɔ a, ahan b'a kaci bɛ́ abalabɔ'n b'a fa bɛ́ nwʋn b'a man Nyanmɩan maan ɔ a fa bɛ́ ɛtɛ nɩn a hye bɛ́.” Zozi 'ba tu anyɩnndala nɩn abʋ (Matie

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
text: Menian mmie-mɔ tɩ kɛ atɩn nɩn anʋan'n mɔ bele'n kɔ gua'n. Menian sɔ'n-mɔ, kɛ bɛ kɔ tɩ Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ nɩn ala, anɩn Satan a yɩ ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ bɛ kɔ tɩ'n fi bɛ́ ahʋnlɩn nɩn anun.

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
text: Sʋanlan m'ɔ lɛ gua bele'n y'ɔ le sʋanlan'n m'ɔ kan Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ'n.

ref: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
text: Mɔ Zozi tuli anyɩnndala sɔ nɩn abʋ yuelɩ'n, ɔ kɔ bisa bɛ́ biekun kɛ: “Asʋ sʋanlan sɔ kanlannɩɛ a, bokiti anaan sʋkpa abʋ yɛ̂ ɔ fa sie ɔ? Nán anwunno yɛ̂ bɛ fa bɛ sie ɔ?

ref: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
text: Ɛsɛ Zozi kɔ bu anyɩnndala kʋn biekun kɛ: “Nyanmɩan Belemgbin Mân'n, ɔ soman sʋanlan m'ɔ lɛ gua *bele yɩ́ ebo nɩn asʋ.

ref: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
text: Asɩɛ'n muonun y'ɔ man ɔ fifi ɔ. Alimʋa nɩn anun, ɔ tɩ ndile. Yɩ́ nzin, ɔ wɔlɔ, yɩ́ ayuelɩɛ'n, ɔ tin, ɔ bʋlʋ.

ref: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
text: Nan m'ɔ kɔ bʋlʋ'n, anɩn b'a fa dadɩɛ b'a wʋlʋ nun, afɩ anɩn yɩ́ ɛtɩɛ mɛlɛ nɩn a ju.” Muntadɩ nɩn anwʋn anyɩnndala'n (Matie

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: Nyanmɩan Belemgbin Mân'n soman muntadɩ baa'n mɔ bɛ dua'n. Ɔ tɩ kaan tala mmaka mma'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ dua'n kʋalaatin.

ref: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
text: Nan mɔ bɛ kɔ lua'n, ɔ yɔ kpili tala tola nɩn anun mmaka'n-mɔ kʋalaatin. Ɔ tʋtʋ yɩ́ sa anwunno, nnʋnman'n-mɔ nwʋn bɛ́ sâ yɩ́ nyâ nɩn abʋ fɔlɔlɔ nɩn anun.” Like ati mɔ Zozi bu anyɩnndala'n (Matie

ref: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
text: Ɔ ngan man ɛjɔlɛ fɩ́ɩ́ fɔ́ʋ́n ɔ ngele man bɛ́. Yɩ́ kʋalaa m'ɔ kan'n, ɔ bu yɩ́ nwʋn anyɩnndala. M'ɔ kɔ ha yɩ́ ngʋnmɩn nʋn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ'n, ɔ tu yɩ́ anyɩnndala'n-mɔ kʋalaa abʋ ɔ kele bɛ́. Zozi 'ba gua anwʋnman kpili kʋn asɩ (Matie

ref: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
text: Ɛhɩ, cɩan kʋnmgba sɔ'n nɔsʋba, Zozi kɔ han kɔ hele yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ: “Bɛ mán yɛ kpɛ́ asue'n yɛ hɔ́ yɩ́ nzin ɛlɔ.”

ref: Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand.
text: Zozi buli anyɩnndala m'ɔ tɩ sʋa dɔʋn ɔ hanlɩn Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ'n ɔ helelɩ bɛ́. Nunhan m'ɔ bu a, bɛ kʋala kɛ bɛ tɩ yɩ́ bʋ'n yɛ̂ ɔ bu ɔ.

ref: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ'n, yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ tuli ɛlɛɛ'n m'ɔ tɩ nun nɩn abʋ bɛ kɔ yaci meninsʋnman nɩn ɛbɛlɛ. Anɩn ɛlɛɛ mmie-mɔ kʋsʋ sisi asue nɩn anʋan ɛbɛlɛ.

ref: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
text: Bɛ hɔlɩ bɛ́ nyunnun kaan an, huuu, anwʋnman kpili kʋn nʋn asutue kɔ tu kɔ si bɛ́. Mgbaha'n kɔ bɔ nsue kɔ gua ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun dede ɔ ka m'ɔ nʋan ɔ.

ref: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
text: Zozi la ɛlɛɛ nɩn abʋ ati, yɩ́ sunmin sɔ yɩ́ ti, ɔ lɛ dafɩ. Yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ tinnge yɩ́. Bɛ́ nwan: “Kpain, yɛ lɛ wu! Ɔ njian man wɔ́ ɔ?”

ref: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
text: Zozi fi nafɛlɛ nun kɔ tinnge. Ɔ kɔ jʋjɔ aminle sʋ kɔ hele anwʋnman kpili'n. Ɛsɛ ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele asue'n kɛ: “Asue, gua diin!” Ɛbɛlɛ ala, anwʋnman kpili'n kɔ gua. Asue'n kɔ gua diin.

ref: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
text: Bɛ́ nwʋn ngɔmʋa kʋalaa fi bɛ́ nwʋn tuli, bɛ kɔ han yɩ́ bɛ́ afian ɛlɔ kɛ: “Sʋanlan benin y'ɔ le ɛhɩ mɔ anwʋnman bɔbɔ nʋn asutue man yɩ́ anyɩnnzɔ ɔ?”

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Afɩ yɩ́ nwan: “Sɛ mɩn kan yɩ́ taladɩɛ nɩn ala a, mɩ́n nwʋn 'sa.”

ref: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi nwan: “Mɩ́n wa balasua, wɔ́ dedi nɩn a le wɔ́ ngʋan. Kɔ anzʋnunjɔ nun, man wɔ́ nwʋn sá.”

ref: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
text: Ɔ nwunlin yalɛ dɔʋn wɔ ayile ɛyɔlɛfʋɛ sʋnman asa nun. Ɔ sɛcɩlɩ yɩ́ nwʋn esika kʋalaatin, kʋsʋ yɩ́ nwʋn a nza man. Yɩ́ ewue nɩn anyunnun ala yɛ̂ ɔ kɔ ɔ.

ref: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
text: Ɔ kɔ wʋlʋ sua nɩn anun ɔ kɔ bisa bɛ́ kɛ: “Nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ ɛmɔ lɛ bɔ awuye bɛ sun sʋa ɔ? Batʋnman talua'n wuli man, nan ɔ lɛ dafɩ.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi nʋn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ, bɛ kpɛlɩ Galile asue'n bɛ hɔlɩ juli Zelazɩ mân nɩn anun.

ref: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
text: Sʋanlan sɔ'n, wawɛ ɛtɛ wɔ yɩ́ nun, asielɩɛ nɩn asʋ yɛ̂ ɔ da ɔ. Sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ ngʋala man yɩ́ cɩ, nán b'a fa ngɔnzɔngɔnzɔ b'a bɔ yɩ́ kpaun.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Afɩ ɔ yɔ a, bɛ fa mmulalɛ anaan ngɔnzɔngɔnzɔ bɛ gua yɩ́ ja nʋn yɩ́ sa, kʋsʋ, ɔ bubu nun. Sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ le man anwʋnsɛlɛ m'ɔ kʋala sʋanlan sɔ'n cɩ ɔ.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
text: Cɩan biala, kɔngɔɛ nʋn eyua, ɛ 'nwun yɩ́ a, anɩn ɔ nanndɩ asielɩɛ'n nʋn mmʋka'n-mɔ asʋ, ɔ tɛtɩan, ɔ sɩsa nyɔbʋɛ'n-mɔ ɔ bʋta bʋta yɩ́ nwʋn.

ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
text: M'ɔ nwunlin Zozi mʋa ɛlɔ'n, ɔ kɔ tutu ɛnwanndie ɔ kɔ hɔ kɔ kʋtʋ yɩ́ nyunnun ɛbɛlɛ.

ref: He shouted, “Jesus, Son of God in heaven, what do you want with me? Promise me in God's name that you won't torture me!”
text: Ɔ kɔ tɩan nun sɛlɛ kpa. Yɩ nwan: “Zozi, Anwunno Nyanmɩan Kpili Awa, ɛ 'se mɩ́n sɛ? Mɩn 'sɛlɛ wɔ́, nɩan wɔ́ Nyanmɩan ɛlɔ, n'ɛ se mɩ́n sa fɩ́ɩ́.”

ref: He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
text: Ɔ kɔ bʋtʋ Zozi kɛ n'ɔ fʋan bɛ́ fi mân sɔ nɩn anun.

ref: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Lɩka mɔ bɛ jin nɩn afan nun, bʋka kʋn anwʋn ɛbɛlɛ, anɩn mgbolike fatule kʋn lɛ didi.

ref: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
text: Wawɛ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ kɔ bʋtʋ Zozi kɛ: “Man yɛ́ atɩn maan yɛ hɔ́ wʋ́lʋ mgbolike'n-mɔ anun.”

ref: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
text: Menian'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ nɩan mgbolike'n-mɔ asʋ'n kɔ nwanndi. Bɛ kɔ hɔ kɔ bɔ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n kpayɛ kulo, ɔ nʋn nanmue'n-mɔ asʋ. Bɛ́ mɔ bɛ tɩlɩ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n, bɛ walɩ nɩanlɩn kɛ ɔ kpɩnlɩn yɔlɩ yɩ́'n.

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Bɛ 'ba ju Zozi anwʋn ɛbɛlɛ a, sʋanlan'n mɔ anɩn wawɛ ɛtɛ'n-mɔ wɔ yɩ́ nun nɩn anyɩnsʋ a tɩ. Ɔ wula taladɩɛ ɔ tɩ ɛbɛlɛ. Ɛjɔlɛ'n yɔlɩ bɛ́ esulo.

ref: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
text: Mɔ bɛ tɩlɩ yɩ́ sɔ'n, bɛ bʋtʋlɩ Zozi kɛ ɔ fi bɛ́ mân nɩn anun ɔ jásʋ.

ref: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
text: Ɔ hanlɩn sɔ a, Zozi ngɔ tie man. Yɩ́ nwan: “Sa kɔ wɔ́ awulo, kɔ han yɩ́ kʋalaa mɔ yɛ́ Mɩn a yɔ a man wɔ́ nʋn kɛ ɔ'a kpɩn ɔ'a nwun wɔ́ nwʋn anwunnvoe'n kele wɔ́ nvilie'n-mɔ.”

ref: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
text: Ɛbɛlɛ ala, sʋanlan sɔ'n kɔ hɔ mân'n mɔ bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ Kulo Bulu nɩn anun. Ɔ kɔ hɔ kɔ bɔ mɔ Zozi yɔlɩ manlɩn yɩ́'n kpayɛ. Bɛ́ kʋalaa mɔ bɛ tɩlɩ yɩ́ ngan'n, ɔ sinlin bɛ́ nwʋn. Zʋayilusi awa balasua'n, ɔ nʋn balasua'n m'ɔ hanlɩn Zozi taladɩɛ'n (Matie

ref: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
text: Kɛ ɔ hanlɩn sɔ nɩn ala, menian'n-mɔ kɔ gʋlʋ yɩ́ nwʋn. Ɛhɩ a, Zozi kɔ man bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ kɔ fite, ɔ kɔ ha sɩɛ'n nʋn nian'n, nʋn yɩ́ menian nsan'n mɔ bɛ walɩ somanlɩn yɩ́'n. Ɔ nʋn bɛ́ kɔ wʋlʋ lɩka mɔ batʋnman'n la'n.

ref: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
text: Ɛhɩ, Zozi fi Galile asue nɩn anzin ɔ kɔ sa kɔ a. M'ɔ walɩ juli asue nɩn anʋan ɛbɛlɛ'n, menian dɔʋn kpa walɩ yɩ́ nwʋn.

ref: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
text: Kaan cɛ, Zufu'n-mɔ asɔnɩn sua nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ anun kʋn kɔ a kɔ ju ɛbɛlɛ. Kpain sɔ'n bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ Zʋayilusi. Ɔ nwunlin Zozi a, ɔ kɔ tu kɔ tɔ yɩ́ ja nun.

ref: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
text: Ɔ kɔ bʋtʋ yɩ́, yɩ́ sa la yɩ́ sa nun, yɩ́ nwan: “Mɩ́n wa balasua'n lɛ ka nun, yɩ́ ti bala fa wɔ́ sa fua yɩ́ nwʋn maan ɔ tɩ́ akpɔlɛ ɔ nyán ngʋan.”

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
text: Ɔ 'ba yɔ sɔ'n, anɩn balasua kʋn wɔ menian'n-mɔ afian ɛbɛlɛ. Moja tu balasua sɔ'n, yɩ́ afʋɛ bulu nʋn nnyuan ahɩ.

ref: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
text: Ɔ tɩlɩ kɛ menian'n-mɔ lɛ kan Zozi anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ. Ɔ kɔ a kɔ wʋlʋ menian nwala'n mɔ afian, Zozi anzin ɛbɛlɛ, ɔ kɔ han yɩ́ taladɩɛ'n.

ref: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
text: Ɔ hanlɩn yɩ́ a, yɩ́ moja'n m'ɔ tu yɩ́'n kɔ kpɛ. Ɛbɛlɛ ala, ɔ kɔ nwun yɩ́ kɛ yɩ́ nwʋn a sa.

ref: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
text: Zozi kʋsʋ kɔ nwun yɩ́ ɛbɛlɛ ala kɛ anwʋnsɛlɛ kʋn fi yɩ́ nun a fite. Ɔ kɔ kpɛ yɩ́ nyɩn menian'n-mɔ afian ɛlɔ ɔ kɔ bisa kɛ: “Nwan yɛ̂ ɔ'a han mɩ́n taladɩɛ'n?”

ref: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
text: Ɔ bisalɩ ɛhɩ a, yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ nwan: “Wɔ́ nyɩn tua kɛ menian'n-mɔ lɛ mian wɔ́, kʋsʋ anɩn ɛ lɛ bisa kɛ: Nwan yɛ̂ ɔ'a han mɩ́n ɔ?”

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Kɛmɔ balasua'n sɩ like m'ɔ nwunlɩn yɩ́ wɔ yɩ́ nwʋnnaan nɩn anun'n, esulo hanlɩn yɩ́ dede yɩ́ nwʋn wʋsʋlɩ yɩ́. Ɔ kɔ a kɔ tu kɔ tɔ Zozi aja nun, ɔ kɔ han ananhɔlɛ'n kʋalaa ɔ kɔ hele yɩ́.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: Anɩn Zozi anʋan tɔlɩ man bɔbɔ, menian fi kpain Zʋayilusi awulo ɛlɔ bɛ walɩ tʋlɩ yɩ́ a, bɛ́ nwan: “Wɔ́ wa balasua nɩn a wu, nan benin anwʋn ati yɛ̂ ɛ tɛ jɩjɩ mɩnlɩan nɩn ɔ?”

ref: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
text: Nan mɔ bɛ hanlɩn sɔ'n, Zozi ngɔ bu man yɩ́ ɛjɔlɛ. Yɩ́ nwan: “Zʋayilusi, gua wɔ́ ahʋnlɩn asɩ, nyan dedi ala.”

ref: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
text: Zozi ngɔ man sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ atɩn kɛ ɔ sí yɩ́ sʋ, sannan Pɩɛlɩ nʋn Zʋakɩ nʋn yɩ́ nianman Zʋan, bɛ́ ala.

ref: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
text: Mɔ bɛ juli Zʋayilusi awulo ɛlɔ'n, Zozi nwunlin kɛ ɛbɛlɛ asʋa yɔɔ. Menian'n-mɔ le esuan nʋn awuye ɛbɔ nʋn ɛtɛtɩan nʋn awɔ́lɛ ɛyɩɛ.

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Ɛbɛlɛ ala, talua'n kɔ jasʋ kɔ nanndɩ. Mɔ ɛjɔlɛ ɛhɩ 'ba'n, anɩn talua nɩn a li afʋɛ bulu nʋn nnyuan. Mɔ menian'n-mɔ nwunlin yɩ́ sɔ'n, ɔ sinlin bɛ́ nwʋn kpa.

ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi totualɩ bɛ́ kɛ nán bɛ kan ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n bɛ kele sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́. Ɛsɛ yɩ́ nwan: “Bɛ mán talua'n like lí.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Kɛ bɛ kpɛlɩ asue'n mɔ bɛ juli Zenezalɛtɩ mân nɩn anun'n, bɛ kɔ si asɩ, bɛ kɔ mɩnnda bɛ́ ɛlɛɛ'n.
