ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Mɔ Zozi nwunlin yɩ́ sɔ'n, yɩ́ nwan: “Bɛ bu Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ biala sʋanlan lɩka kʋalaa. Nan yɩ́ muonun yɩ́ kulo nɩn asʋ menian'n-mɔ nʋn yɩ́ nvilie'n-mɔ yɛ̂ bɛ mmu man yɩ́ sʋanlan ɔ.”

ref: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: Mɔ ɛnwʋnmɩan ele cɩan'n juli'n, Zozi hɔlɩ Zufʋ'n-mɔ asɔnɩn sua nɩn anun ɔ hanlɩn Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ'n ɔ helelɩ bɛ́. Anɩn menian dɔʋn wɔ sua nɩn anun. Ɛjɔlɛ mɔ Zozi hanlɩn mɔ bɛ tɩlɩ'n sinlin bɛ́ nwʋn. Bɛ kɔ bisa bisa bɛ́ nwʋn kɛ: “Nan ɔ nʋn ɛhɩ fi nin? Ninhan yɛ̂ ɔ nyanlɩn yɩ́ ngɛlɛ ɛhɩ ɔ? Ɔ yɔ sɛ mɔ ɔ yɔ asinbɛnwʋn ninnge sʋa ɔ?

ref: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
text: Nán Mali awa'n m'ɔ di tabʋa junman nɩn ɔ? Nán yɩ́ nianman-mɔ y'ɔ le Zʋakɩ nʋn Zozʋ, nʋn Zudɩ nʋn Simʋn ɔ? Nán yɩ́ nianman mmala'n-mɔ yɛ̂ bɛ wɔ yɛ́ afian ɛwa nɩn ɔ?” Zozi ɛsɩɛ mɔ bɛ sɩ yɩ́ nɩn ati, b'a nne man yɩ́ b'a nni man.

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Kulo biala mɔ ɛmɔ kɔ ju sʋ, mɔ bɛ ngulo man kɛ bɛ sike ɛmɔ anaan bɛ tie ɛmɔ ɛjɔlɛ'n, ɛmɔ wʋ́sʋ bɛ́ ja nɩn abʋ anyʋan'n bɛ gúa ɛbɛlɛ bɛ hɔ́. Ɔ tɩ adanzɩɛ elie kɛ bɛ ngʋala man ɛmɔ sʋnmʋɛ bɔ.”

ref: Herod had earlier married Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip. But John had told him, “It isn't right for you to take your brother's wife!” So, in order to please Herodias, Herod arrested John and put him in prison.
text: Nan mɔ Zʋan nwunlin yɩ́ sɔ'n, ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele Elɔdɩ kɛ: “Nannan, Nyanmɩan mala'n mman man wɔ́ atɩn kɛ ɛ fá wɔ́ nianman nɩn ayɩ'n já.”

ref: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it
text: Zʋan ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n ngɔ yɔ Elodiadɩ fɛ fɩ́ɩ́. Ɔ kɔ fa Zʋan anwʋn ɛkpɔ. Ɔ kulo kɛ ɔ man bɛ kun Zʋan, kʋsʋ Elɔdɩ dunman nun, ɔ nnwun man kɛ ɔ kpɩn yɔ ɔ.

ref: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
text: Afɩ Elɔdɩ fɛlɩ Zʋan kpa. Ɔ sɩ kɛ Zʋan tɩ sɛsɛfʋɛ ɛsɛ ɔ tɩ anwʋntɩɛ sʋanlan. Ɔ nɩan Zʋan asʋ. Mɛlɛ mɔ Zʋan hanlɩn ɛjɔlɛ'n m'ɔ tɩlɩ'n, ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n jɩjɩlɩ yɩ́ kpa. Kʋsʋ afɩ, ɔ le fɛ elie ɔ tie ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ Zʋan kan'n.

ref: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Yɩ́ nin anʋan ngɔ tɔ man bɔbɔ, ndɛ-ndɛ talua'n kɔ hɔ kɔ tʋ yɩ́ sɩ, yɩ́ nwan: “Baba, mɩn kulo kɛ ɛ fa Zʋan Batisi ati'n tʋ apawa nun man mɩ́n kɩkaala.”

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: Mɔ Elɔdɩ tɩlɩ yɩ́ sɔ'n, yɩ́ lʋa kɔ bɔ yɩ́ nwʋn kpa. Nan kʋsʋ ndâ'n m'ɔ hanlɩn wɔ yɩ́ mgbain'n-mɔ anyunnun ɛbɛlɛ nɩn ati, ɔ ngɔ hʋala man yɩ́ nʋan kaci.

ref: At once he ordered a guard to cut off John's head there in prison.
text: Ɛbɛlɛ ala, ɔ kɔ sʋan yɩ́ nzalafʋ'n-mɔ nunhan kʋn kɛ ɔ hɔ́ fiadɩ sua nɩn anun ɛlɔ, ɔ hɔ́ kpɛ́ Zʋan Batisi ati'n ɔ fá bɛ́lɛ yɩ́.

ref: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
text: Nzalafʋ'n kɔ hɔ fiadɩ sua nɩn anun. Ɔ kɔ hɔ kɔ kpɛ Zʋan ati'n ɔ kɔ tʋ yɩ́ apawa nun ɔ kɔ bɛlɛ talua'n. Talua'n kɔ le, ɔ kɔ fa kɔ hɔ kɔ man yɩ́ nin.

ref: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
text: Mɔ Zʋan menian'n-mɔ tɩlɩ yɩ́ sɔ'n, bɛ walɩ falɩ yɩ́ fuin'n bɛ hɔlɩ sielɩ yɩ́. Zozi 'ba man menian akpɩɩ nnun alɩɛ (Matie

ref: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
text: Menian'n-mɔ mɔ bɛ ba Zozi anwʋn'n sʋnlɩn sʋnman. Mmie-mɔ lɛ ba, anɩn mmie-mɔ lɛ kɔ. Ɔ man Zozi nʋn yɩ́ mmɔfʋɛ'n-mɔ nnyan man alaje bɔbɔ mɔ bɛ fa bɛ di like ɔ. Ɛhɩ ati, Zozi kɔ han kɔ hele yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ: “Ɛmɔ mán yɛ tɩ́ yɛ́ nwʋn yɛ hɔ́ ahanmɩan, nán ɛmɔ lé bɛ́ ɛnwʋnmɩan kaan.”

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ yuelɩ'n, bɛ kɔ hɔ kɔ fʋ ɛlɛɛ kʋn anun, bɛ kɔ hɔ ahanmɩan, lɩka mɔ sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ nnʋn man'n.

ref: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
text: Mɔ alɩ́ɛ'n yɔlɩ bie'n, yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ kpunnge yɩ́ nwʋn ɛbɛlɛ, bɛ́ nwan: “Yɛ́ Mɩn, alɩ́ɛ'n lɛ yɔ, ɛsɛ lɩka mɔ yɛ wɔ'n kʋsʋ tɩ yɩ́ nwʋn.

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Mɔ bɛ hanlɩn ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n, Zozi nwan: “Ɛmɔ muonun mán bɛ́ like lí.” Yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ nwan: “Nzu? Man yɛ hɔ́ tó kpaʋn bɔbɔ esika jɛtɛ ɛya nnyuan an, ɔ nza man like fɩ́ɩ́ yɔ.”

ref: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: Mɔ bɛ hanlɩn sɔ'n, Zozi kɔ bisa bɛ́ kɛ: “Kpaʋn nyɛ yɛ̂ ɛmɔ le ɔ? Ɛmɔ hɔ́ nɩ́an.” Bɛ hɔlɩ bɛ walɩ a, bɛ́ nwan: “Yɛ le kpaʋn nnun nʋn ejue nnyuan.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Mɔ bɛ hanlɩn sɔ'n, Zozi kɔ han kɔ hele bɛ́ kɛ bɛ mán meninsʋnman'n kʋalaatin tɛ́tanlan asɩ kpule kpule ndile nɩn asʋ.

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Esulo hanlɩn bɛ́, ɔ sanlɩn kɛ bɛ́ kʋalaatin bɛ́ nwunlin yɩ́. Ɛbɛlɛ ala, Zozi kɔ han kɔ hele bɛ́ kɛ: “Ɛmɔ gúa bɛ́ ahʋnlɩn asɩ! Nán bɛ sulo nan mɩ́n ɔ!”

ref: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
text: Mɔ bɛ tanlanlɩn asɩ yuelɩ'n, Zozi kɔ le kpaʋn nnun'n, ɔ nʋn ejue nnyuan'n. Ɔ kɔ tʋ yɩ́ nyɩn anwunno, ɔ kɔ la Nyanmɩan asɩ. Ɔ kɔ bubu kpaʋn'n-mɔ anun ɔ kɔ man yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ bɛ hyé bɛ mán meninsʋnman'n. Ɔ kɔ fa ejue nnyuan'n ɔ kɔ man bɛ́ kɛ bɛ hyé bɛ mán bɛ́ kʋalaatin.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
text: Bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ kɔ li dede bɛ́ kʋ kɔ yi, bie kɔ ha.

ref: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
text: M'ɔ halɩ'n, bɛ sɩsalɩ dede a, ɔ tɩ ece mma bulu nʋn nnyuan. Ejue'n kʋsʋ bie halɩ.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Ɛhɩ, meninsʋnman'n yuelɩ like di ɛbɛlɛ ala, Zozi kɔ han kɔ hele yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ: “Ɛmɔ fʋ́ ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun bɛ kpɛ́ asue'n bɛ lí mʋa Bɛtɩsayida ɛlɔ. Mɩn 'gua menian'n-mɔ atɩn kʋalaaka nán m'an a.”

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Kɛ Zozi gualɩ menian'n-mɔ atɩn yuelɩ'n, ɔ kɔ hɔ asɔnɩn ɛyɔlɛ bʋka kʋn asʋ.

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: Mɔ alɩ́ɛ'n sanlɩn'n, anɩn ɛlɛɛ nɩn a ju asue nɩn afian. Zozi angʋnmɩn cein jin asɩ ɛbɛlɛ.

ref: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
text: Ɔ 'nɩan an, anɩn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ ngʋala man ɛlɛɛ'n kan kpa. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ bɛ nʋn asutue nɩn a yia nun. Ɛhɩ a, alɩbahɩan dɔɛ nsan mɔ ɔ 'kɔ dɔɛ nsian'n, Zozi kɔ nanndɩ asue nɩn anyɩnsʋ ɔ kɔ kpʋ bɛ́. M'ɔ walɩ dede m'ɔ ka kaan tʋ bɛ́'n, ɔ yɔlɩ kɛ ɔ lɛ san bɛ́ nwʋn.

ref: When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming.
text: Nan mɔ bɛ nwunlin kɛ ɔ nanndɩ asue nɩn anyɩnsʋ'n, bɛ bu kɛ ɛnwʋnmɩn ɔ. Bɛ kɔ tɛtɩan.

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: Kɛ ɔ hanlɩn sɔ mɔ ɔ 'fʋ bɛ ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun ala, asutue'n kɔ gua asɩ. Zozi nyɔlɩɛ sɔ'n kɔ sin bɛ́ nwʋn kpa.

ref: Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread. Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Matthew
text: Afɩ asinbɛnwʋn like'n mɔ Zozi falɩ kpaʋn'n yɔlɩ'n, b'a ndɩ man yɩ́ bʋ. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ b'a nva man bɛ́ ajʋnlɩn b'a nza man sʋ. Zozi 'ba man Zenezalɛtɩ nwuluwafʋɛ'n-mɔ anwʋn sa. (Matie

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Mɔ bɛ fi ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun bɛ juli'n, ɛbɛlɛ ala, menian'n-mɔ kɔ nwun kɛ Zozi ɔ.

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: Bɛ kɔ nwanndi bɛ kɔ hɔ lɩka kʋalaatin bɛ kɔ hɔ kɔ bɔ menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa amannɩɛ. Lɩka biala mɔ bɛ kɔ tɩ kɛ Zozi wɔ'n, bɛ sʋa nwuluwafʋɛ'n-mɔ ɛbɛɛ sʋ bɛ bɛlɛ yɩ́.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Lɩka biala m'ɔ kɔ hɔ'n, nanmue nun o, kulo baa asʋ o, kulo kpili asʋ o, bɛ fa nwuluwafʋɛ'n-mɔ bɛ sesie anʋan'n-mɔ anyunnun gua'n-mɔ asʋ. Bɛ sɛlɛ Zozi kɛ ɔ mán bɛ́ atɩn maan bɛ hán yɩ́ taladɩɛ nɩn anʋan nɩn ala. Sakpa, bɛ́ kʋalaa mɔ bɛ kɔ han yɩ́'n, bɛ́ nwʋn sa.

ref: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
text: Ɛhɩ, Falisifʋɛ mmie-mɔ nʋn mala nɩn asʋ mgbain mmie-mɔ fi Zoluzalɛmʋn bɛ walɩ yialɩ Zozi anwʋn ɛbɛlɛ.

ref: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
text: Ɛsɛ Zozi kɔ han biekun kɔ tʋ sʋ. Yɩ́ nwan: “Ɛmɔ a fa bɛ́ mala dɩɛ'n b'a nunnun Nyanmɩan dɩɛ nɩn abʋsʋ.

ref: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
text: Afɩ, Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ nʋn Zufʋ'n-mɔ kʋalaa, bɛ di bɛ́ nan-mɔ mala sɔ nɩn asʋ kpa. Sɛ bɛ 'ba li like a, bɛ tʋ bɛ́ ahʋnlɩn asɩ bɛ nwunnzin bɛ́ sa nɩn anun kpa bɔkɔɔ.

ref: None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
text: Ɛsɛ like mɔ bɛ kɔ to yɩ́ gua nun mɔ bɛ 'ba li'n, sɛ bɛ nwunnzinlin man bɛ́ sa nɩn anun bɛ tuli man bɛ́ nwʋn efian an, bɛ nni man. Bɛ le daba mala dɔʋn mɔ bɛ di sʋ sɛlɛ kpa kɛ kangɔ nʋn bua ɔ nʋn daansɛ'n-mɔ anun enwunnzian.

ref: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
text: Ɛhɩ a, Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ nʋn mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ kɔ bisa Zozi kɛ: “Mala mɔ yɛ́ nan-mɔ a hyɩhyɛ a tʋ ɛbɛlɛ'n, nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ wɔ́ menian'n-mɔ nni man sʋ mɔ bɛ le bɛ́ sa nɩn anun efian'n bɛ lɛ didi'n?”

ref: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
text: Mɔ Zozi tɩlɩ bɛ́ kosuan sɔ'n, yɩ́ nwan: “Apoo mma, ɛmɔ anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ Ezayi hanlɩn yɩ́ daba'n tɩ ananhɔlɛ. Ezayi hɛlɛlɩ yɩ́ kɛ: Menian ɛhɩ-mɔ le bɛ́ nʋan tuin mgban yɛ̂ bɛ yɩ mɩ́n ayɛ ɔ, nan bɛ́ ahʋnlɩn'n nnʋn man mɩ́n sʋ.

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.”
text: Ɛsʋɛ'n mɔ bɛ sʋ mɩ́n'n tɩ mgban. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ kulo sʋanlan mala yɛ̂ bɛ kekele ɔ, nan nán Nyanmɩan dɩɛ nɩn ɔ.”

ref: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
text: Zozi nwan: “Ɛmɔ nni man Nyanmɩan mala dɩɛ nɩn asʋ nan kulo menian'n-mɔ dɩɛ nɩn asʋ yɛ̂ ɛmɔ di ɔ.”

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
text: Nan ɛmɔ dɩɛ, ɛmɔ nwan sɛ sʋanlan kʋn kan kele yɩ́ sɩ nʋn yɩ́ nin kɛ: ‘Like mɔ n ne yɩ́ mɔ n gɔ fa n gɔ bʋka ɛmɔ'n, ɔ tɩ kɔɔban’ ,

ref: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
text: Ɛmɔ yɔ sɔ bɛ nunnun Nyanmɩan mala nɩn abʋsʋ ɛmɔ di bɛ́ dɩɛ'n-mɔ asʋ. Ɛsɛ bɔbɔ ɛmɔ yɔ ninnge dɔʋn mmiekun-mɔ m'ɔ nvata man ɔ.” Like m'ɔ gua sʋanlan anwʋn efian'n (Matie

ref: Then Jesus said: What comes from your heart is what makes you unclean.
text: Ɛsɛ yɩ́ nwan: “Nan ɛjɔlɛ ɛtɛ m'ɔ fi sʋanlan ahʋnlɩn nun kɔ fite'n yɛ̂ ɔ gua yɩ́ nwʋn efian ɔ.

ref: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, “Pay attention and try to understand what I mean.
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ yuelɩ'n, ɔ fɛlɛlɩ meninsʋnman'n yɩ́ nwʋn biekun an, yɩ́ nwan: “Bɛ́ kʋalaa bɛ tíe mɔ mɩɩn kan'n bɛ tɩ́ yɩ́ bʋ.

ref: After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant.
text: M'ɔ hanlɩn sɔ yuelɩ m'ɔ fi bagua nɩn anun ɔ fitelɩ ɔ hɔlɩ awulo'n, yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ bisa yɩ́ anyɩnndala sɔ'n m'ɔ buli nɩn abʋ.

ref: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi kɔ bua bɛ kɛ: “Yɩ́ ti, ɛmɔ kʋsʋ nzɩ man ngɛlɛ ɔ? Ɛmɔ ndɩ man yɩ́ bʋ kɛ like biala mɔ sʋanlan kɔ li'n, ɔ ngʋala man yɩ́ nwʋn efian gua ɔ?

ref: It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.” By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ like mɔ sʋanlan kɔ li'n, ɔ ngɔ man yɩ́ ahʋnlɩn nɩn anun. Nan ɔ kɔ yɩ́ kunnun yɛ̂ ɔ je gua.”

ref: unfaithfulness in marriage, greed, meanness, deceit, indecency, envy, insults, pride, and foolishness.
text: ajaa ɛtʋan, anyɩnbʋlʋ, ati anun ɛtɛ ɛyɔlɛ, nnaka, basa-basa ɛyɔlɛ, nyunnunngunlan, dunmannzɛcɩɛ, atubɛnwʋn, kʋasɩanzɛmʋn.

ref: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
text: Ninnge ɛtɛ ɛtɛ ɛhɩ-mɔ kʋalaa m'ɔ fi sʋanlan nun fite'n, bɛ́ yɛ̂ bɛ gua yɩ́ nwʋn efian ɔ.” Ɛyɛfʋɛ balasua kʋn 'ba le Zozi di (Matie

ref: The woman replied, “Lord, even puppies eat the crumbs that children drop from the table.”
text: Zozi hanlɩn sɔ a, balasua'n nwan: “Ɔ tɩ ananhɔlɛ, yɛ́ Mɩn. Nan kʋsʋ mmatʋnman'n-mɔ alɩɛ nɩn abʋ mgbʋkpʋlʋka'n m'ɔ kɔ yɩyɩ kɔ gua tɔbɩlɩ nɩn abʋ'n yɛ̂ njʋa'n-mɔ di ɔ.”

ref: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Zozi anʋan y'ɔ le Silifeninsi mân nun. Lɩka m'ɔ juli'n mantan kulo kʋn. Kulo sɔ'n, bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ Tii. Ɔ ngulo man kɛ bɛ tɩ yɩ́ ngan. Yɩ́ ti, ɔ kɔ hɔ kɔ wʋlʋ awulo kʋn anun. Nan kʋsʋ ɔ'a ngʋala man fɩa.

ref: A woman whose daughter had an evil spirit in her heard where Jesus was. And at once she came and knelt down at his feet.
text: Afɩ anɩn balasua kʋn wɔ ɛbɛlɛ, wawɛ ɛtɛ wɔ yɩ́ wa talua nɩn anun. Ɔ kɔ tɩ Zozi angan. Ɔ kɔ a kɔ tʋ Zozi ɔ kɔ tu kɔ tɔ yɩ́ ja nun.

ref: The woman was Greek and had been born in the part of Syria known as Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to force the demon out of her daughter.
text: Balasua sɔ'n tɩ man Zufʋ. Bɛ wʋlɩ yɩ́ Silifeninsi mân nun. Ɔ tɩ *gɛlɛkɩ. Ɔ kɔ sɛlɛ Zozi kɛ ɔ tú wawɛ ɛtɛ'n m'ɔ wɔ yɩ́ wa talua nɩn anun'n.

ref: But Jesus said, “The children must first be fed! It isn't right to take away their food and feed it to dogs.”
text: Mɔ bala'n hanlɩn sɔ'n, Zozi kɔ bua yɩ́ kɛ: “Nwʋnndɛ maan mmatʋnman'n-mɔ didi kʋalaaka. Afɩ, ɔ nvata man kɛ bɛ ju mmatʋnman'n-mɔ alɩɛ'n bɛ gua bɛ man njʋa'n-mɔ di ɔ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “That's true! You may go now. The demon has left your daughter.”
text: Mɔ Zozi tɩlɩ yɩ́ mmua sɔ'n, yɩ́ nwan: “Wɔ́ nʋan ɛjɔlɛ nɩn ati, sa kɔ, nan wawɛ ɛtɛ'n fi wɔ́ wa nɩn anun ɔ'a tu.”

ref: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
text: Sakpa kʋsʋ, balasua'n fi ɛbɛlɛ ɔ 'kɔ a ju a, yɩ́ wa'n la mgba nɩn asʋ, wawɛ ɛtɛ'n fi yɩ́ nun a tu. Zozi 'ba man amunlefʋɛ anzʋtiliwa kʋn anwʋn sa

ref: Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Zozi fi kulo'n mɔ bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ Tii nɩn afan ɔ kɔ sɩn Sidʋn kulo nɩn afan, ɔ kɔ sa kɔ a Galile asue nɩn anʋan. Mɔ ɔ 'sa ba'n, ɔ kpɛlɩ nun mân'n mɔ bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ Kulo Bulu nɩn anun.

ref: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk. They begged Jesus just to touch him.
text: Ɛbɛlɛ, bɛ kɔ fa amunlefʋɛ anzʋtiliwa kʋn bɛ kɔ bɛlɛ yɩ́ kɛ ɔ fá yɩ́ sa fúa yɩ́ nwʋn maan ɔ tɩ́ akpɔlɛ.

ref: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi kɔ fa wuluwafʋɛ'n bagua nɩn anun ɛbɛlɛ, ɔ nʋn yɩ́ kɔ hɔ ahanmɩan. Bɛ juli ɛlɔ a, ɔ kɔ fa yɩ́ sa mɛsɩka'n kɔ wula amunlefʋɛ nɩn anzʋ'n-mɔ anun kɔ yɩ. Ɛsɛ ɔ kɔ fa yɩ́ nʋan ngɛsɛlɛ'n bie kɔ han amunlefʋɛ'n tofeleman'n.

ref: Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, “Effatha!” which means “Open up!”
text: M'ɔ yɔlɩ ɛhɩ kʋalaa yuelɩ'n, ɔ kɔ tʋ yɩ́ ti anwunno ɔ kɔ gua ɛnwʋnmɩan asɩ. Yɩ́ nwan: “Ɛfata!” Yɩ́ bʋ y'ɔ le kɛ tike!

ref: At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
text: Ɛbɛlɛ ala, yɩ́ nzʋ'n-mɔ kɔ tike tike, yɩ́ tofeleman'n kɔ nyannjɩ, ɔ kɔ jʋjɔ yuein-yuein.

ref: Jesus told the people not to say anything about what he had done. But the more he told them, the more they talked about it.
text: Mɔ Zozi yɔlɩ asinbɛnwʋn like sɔ'n yuelɩ'n, ɔ kɔ totua bɛ́ kɛ nán bɛ kan ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n bɛ kele sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́. Ɔ 'totua bɛ́ a, anɩn bɛ lɛ kan bɛ kɔ.

ref: They were completely amazed and said, “Everything he does is good! He even heals people who cannot hear or talk.”
text: Ɛjɔlɛ'n sinlin menian'n-mɔ anwʋn kpa. Bɛ́ nwan: “Like biala m'ɔ kɔ yɔ'n, ɔ yɔ yɩ́ kanlanman kpa! Ɔ man bɛ́ mɔ bɛ́ nzʋ a ti'n tɩ ɛjɔlɛ! Bɛ́ mɔ b'a tɔ amunle'n, ɔ man bɛ jʋjɔ!”

ref: Jesus warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod.”
text: Zozi kɔ tu bɛ́ fʋɔ kɛ: “Ɛmɔ nɩ́an bɛ́ nwʋn kpa wɔ Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ nʋn Elɔdɩ fali ayile nɩn anwʋn.”

ref: Then Jesus explained clearly what he meant. Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that.
text: Zozi kɔ han yɩ́ ewue sɔ nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ yuein kɔ hele yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ. Ɛhɩ, Pɩɛlɩ kɔ fɛlɛ Zozi ahanmɩan ɔ kɔ tu yɩ́ nyunnun.

ref: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
text: “Menian ɛhɩ-mɔ anwʋn yɔ mɩ́n koun. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ bɛ nʋn mɩ́n wɔ ɛwa yɩ́ cɩan nsan ahɩ, bɛ nnyan man like fɩ́ɩ́ bɛ nni man.

ref: Some of them live a long way from here. If I send them away hungry, they might faint on their way home.”
text: Sɛ mɩn man bɛ́ atɩn kɛ bɛ fá bɛ́ kunnun bɛ sá bɛ hɔ́ a, bɛ 'ba gɔlɔ atunnun. Afɩ bɛ́ nun mmie-mɔ fi mʋa.”

ref: After Jesus told the crowd to sit down, he took the seven loaves and gave thanks. He then broke the loaves and handed them to his disciples, who passed them out to the crowd.
text: Ɛhɩ a, ɔ kɔ man meninsʋnman'n kɔ tɛtanlan asɩ. Ɔ kɔ fa kpaʋn nsʋ'n, ɔ kɔ la Nyanmɩan asɩ. Ɔ kɔ bubu nun kɔ man yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ bɛ hyé bɛ mán meninsʋnman'n. Zozi menian'n-mɔ kɔ hye kɔ man bɛ́.

ref: They also had a few little fish, and after Jesus had blessed these, he told the disciples to pass them around.
text: Ɛsɛ anɩn bɛ le ejue nganngan mmiekun-mɔ. Zozi kɔ yeyila sʋ ɔ kɔ han kɔ hele yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ bɛ hyé bɛ bʋ́ka sʋ.

ref: he got into the boat with the disciples and crossed to the territory near Dalmanutha. A Sign from Heaven (Matthew
text: Ɛbɛlɛ ala, ɔ nʋn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ, bɛ kɔ fʋ ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun bɛ kɔ hɔ mân'n mɔ bɛ fɛlɛ yɩ́ Dalɩmannunta nɩn anun. Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ 'ba bisa Zozi kɛ ɔ yɔ́ asinbɛnwʋn like kʋn maan bɛ nɩ́an. (Matie

ref: The Pharisees came out and started an argument with Jesus. They wanted to test him by asking for a sign from heaven.
text: Ɛhɩ, *Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ kɔ a kɔ ju ɛbɛlɛ. Bɛ nʋn Zozi kɔ su akpʋlʋwa. Kɛmɔ bɛ kulo kɛ bɛ sɔ yɩ́ bɛ nɩan'n, bɛ kpʋnndɛ kɛ ɔ yɔ́ asinbɛnwʋn like kʋn m'ɔ kele kɛ yɩ́ tunmin'n fi Nyanmɩan ɛlɔ sakpa.

ref: The disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
text: Mɔ bɛ 'kpɛ asue'n, Zozi menian'n-mɔ alʋa kɔ fi kpaʋn mɔ bɛ 'fa bɛ sa bɛ́ nwʋn'n. Kpaʋn baa kʋnmgba yɛ̂ anɩn bɛ le yɩ́ bɛ nwʋn ɛbɛlɛ ɛlɛɛ nɩn anun ɔ.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking and asked, “Why are you talking about not having any bread? Don't you understand? Are your minds still closed?
text: Zozi kɔ tɩ, ɔ kɔ bisa bɛ́ kɛ: “Nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ ɛmɔ lɛ kan kɛ: ‘Anɩn kpaʋn'n mɔ y'a nva man nɩn ati ɔ’? Yɩ́ ti, ɛmɔ nzɩ man fasie bɛ ndɩ man ɛjɔlɛ bʋ ɔ? Ɛmɔ ajʋnlɩn nza man ɛmɔ ɔ?

ref: Jesus took him by the hand and led him out of the village, where he spit into the man's eyes. He placed his hands on the blind man and asked him if he could see anything.
text: Zozi kɔ sɔ anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ nɩn asa ɔ nʋn yɩ́ kɔ hɔ kulo'n tiba. Ɔ kɔ fa yɩ́ nʋan ngɛsɛlɛ kɔ ta wuluwafʋɛ nɩn anyɩn nɩn asʋ. Ɔ kɔ fa yɩ́ sa kɔ fua wuluwafʋɛ nɩn anyɩn'n ɔ kɔ bisa yɩ́ kɛ: “Asʋ ɛ nwun like ɔ?”

ref: The man looked up and said, “I see people, but they look like trees walking around.”
text: Anyɩnsinlinwafʋɛ'n kɔ tike yɩ́ nyɩn'n, yɩ́ nwan: “Mɩn nwun menian'n-mɔ, nan kʋsʋ bɛ tɩtɩ kɛ mmaka mɔ bɛ lɛ nanndɩ ɔ.”

ref: Once again Jesus placed his hands on the man's eyes, and this time the man stared. His eyes were healed, and he saw everything clearly.
text: Zozi kɔ fa yɩ́ sa kɔ fua wuluwafʋɛ nɩn anyɩn'n biekun. Mɔ Zozi yɩlɩ yɩ́ sa'n, wuluwafʋɛ'n kɔ nɩan yɩ́ nyunnun fɔ́ʋ́n. Yɩ́ nwʋn kɔ sa ɔ kɔ nwun asɩ kpa bɔkɔɔ.

ref: Jesus said to him, “You may return home now, but don't go into the village.” Who Is Jesus? (Matthew
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi kɔ han kɔ hele yɩ́ kɛ: “Kɔ awulo, kʋsʋ n'ɛ wʋlʋ kulo nɩn anun.” Pɩɛlɩ 'ba han kɛ Zozi yɛ̂ ɔ tɩ Kilisi nɩn ɔ (Matie

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?” “You are the Messiah!” Peter replied.
text: Ɛhɩ a, Zozi kɔ bisa bɛ́ kɛ: “Nan ɛmɔ kʋsʋ lɩlɩ, ɛmɔ bu kɛ nwan y'ɔ le mɩ́n?” Pɩɛlɩ nwan: “*Kilisi'n y'ɔ le wɔ́, Izalayɛ Ngʋandefʋɛ'n.”

ref: Jesus warned the disciples not to tell anyone about him. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Matthew
text: Mɔ Pɩɛlɩ hanlɩn sɔ'n, Zozi kɔ totua bɛ́ kpa kɛ nán bɛ kan ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n bɛ kele sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́. Zozi 'ba han yɩ́ ewue'n nʋn yɩ́ etinnge nɩn anwʋn ɛjɔlɛ (Matie

ref: Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “The nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make the Son of Man suffer terribly. He will be rejected and killed, but three days later he will rise to life.”
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Zozi kɔ han kɔ hele yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ: “Ɔ di kɛ mân Baa'n nwun amannɩnhunlun kpa. Izalayɛ mân nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn Nyanmɩan tɛɛyɩfʋɛ mgbain'n-mɔ nʋn mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ 'ba yɩ yɩ́ tʋ. Bɛ 'ba hun yɩ́. Bɛ kun yɩ́ a, yɩ́ cɩan nsan nɩn asʋ, ɔ 'tinnge.”

ref: But when Jesus turned and saw the disciples, he corrected Peter. He said to him, “Satan, get away from me! You are thinking like everyone else and not like God.”
text: Zozi kɔ bɩlɩ yɩ́ nyɩn ɔ kɔ nɩan yɩ́ menian bʋ nga'n-mɔ. Ɔ kɔ tu Pɩɛlɩ anyunnun. Yɩ́ nwan: “Satan, fi mɩ́n nwʋn ɛwa kɔ mʋa. Wɔ́ ajʋnlɩn'n tɩ man Nyanmɩan ajʋnlɩn, nan kulo menian ajʋnlɩn yɛ̂ ɛ le yɩ́ ɔ.”

ref: Jesus then told the crowd and the disciples to come closer, and he said: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
text: M'ɔ yɔlɩ sɔ'n, Zozi fɛlɛlɩ meninsʋnman'n nʋn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ a, yɩ́ nwan: “Sʋanlan m'ɔ kulo kɛ ɔ si mɩ́n sʋ'n, ɔ fá yɩ́ nwʋn yɩ́ tɛɛ, ɔ fá yɩ́ kʋlʋwa'n ɔ tʋ́ yɩ́ kɔmɩn sʋ, nán ɔ'a si mɩ́n sʋ.

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me and for the good news, you will save it.
text: Sʋanlan biala m'ɔ kulo kɛ ɔ kpʋnndɛ yɩ́ ngʋan'n, ɔ 'minlin yɩ́ ngʋan sɔ'n. Nan m'ɔ kɔ minlin yɩ́ ngʋan'n wɔ Ɛjɔlɛkpa'n nʋn mɩ́n dunman nun'n, sʋanlan sɔ'n, ɔ 'nyan ngʋan.

ref: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself?
text: Sɛ sʋanlan kʋn nyan eyuadɩ nɩn anun ninnge'n kʋalaa nán ɔ minlin yɩ́ ngʋan nɩn an, anɩn nzukɛ nvasʋɛ dɩɛ yɛ̂ ɔ 'nyan yɩ́ sʋ ɔ?

ref: What could you give to get back your soul?
text: Nzu like dɩɛ bɔbɔ yɛ̂ kulo sʋanlan kʋala kɛ ɔ fa kaci yɩ́ ngʋan nɩn ɔ?

ref: Don't be ashamed of me and my message among these unfaithful and sinful people! If you are, the Son of Man will be ashamed of you when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.
text: Ajulisʋ ɛtɛfʋɛ menian ɛhɩ-mɔ mɔ bɛ nzulo man Nyanmɩan'n, bɛ́ afian, wɔ́ mɔ ɛ kɔ sa mɩ́n nʋn mɩ́n ɛjɔlɛ nɩn anwʋn nyian'n, *Mân Baa'n kʋsʋ, mɛlɛ m'ɔ nʋn yɩ́ nyanmɩansʋ anwʋntɩɛ mmɔfʋɛ'n-mɔ 'ba sa bɛ ba Nyanmɩan anunminnyanmʋn nɩn anun'n, wɔ́ nwʋn 'ba yɔ yɩ́ nyian.”

ref: I can assure you that Elijah has already come. And people treated him just as they wanted to, as the Scriptures say they would. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
text: Afɩ, mɩɩn kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ kɛ Eli a a dada angʋnmɩn. Nan menian'n-mɔ helelɩ yɩ́ yalɛ kɛ bɛ́ kunnun kulo. Kɛ Ɛhɛlɛ Ɛjɔlɛ'n hanlɩn yɩ́ nwʋn ɛjɔlɛ nɩn anɩn.” Zozi 'ba man sʋanlan kʋn m'ɔ kpɛ nɩn anwʋn sa (Matie

ref: After Jesus and the disciples had gone back home and were alone, they asked him, “Why couldn't we force out that demon?”
text: Mɔ Zozi hɔlɩ awulo m'ɔ halɩ yɩ́ nʋn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ angʋnmɩn'n, yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ bisa yɩ́ kɛ: “Nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ y'a ngʋala man wawɛ sɔ'n tu'n?”

ref: Then Jesus asked a child to stand near him. He put his arm around the child and said,
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ yuelɩ'n, ɔ kɔ fa batʋnman kaan kʋn kɔ a bɛ́ afian ɛbɛlɛ. Ɔ kɔ fa yɩ́ kɔ bɔ yɩ́ nwʋn. Yɩ́ nwan:

ref: Then Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
text: Zozi menian nsan'n 'nɩan an, bɛ kɔ nwun *Eli nʋn *Moyizɩ kɛ bɛ nʋn Zozi lɛ jʋjɔ.

ref: The shadow of a cloud passed over and covered them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my Son, and I love him. Listen to what he says!”
text: Bɛ jijin ɛbɛlɛ ala, bɔlɛ́ kʋn fi nyanmɩansʋ kɔ a kɔ hala Eli nʋn Moyizɩ nʋn Zozi asʋ. Bɔlɛ́ nɩn anun, anɩɛ kʋn kɔ te. Anɩɛ sɔ'n nwan: “Sʋanlan ɛhɩ tɩ mɩ́n ehulo Baa, ɛmɔ tíe yɩ́!”

ref: So they kept it to themselves. But they wondered what he meant by the words “raised from death.”
text: Bɛ falɩ m'ɔ hanlɩn helelɩ bɛ́ nɩn asʋ, nan kʋsʋ bɛ lɛ bisa bisa bɛ́ nwʋn bɛ́ afian ɛlɔ kɛ: “Ɔ fi ewue nun ɔ 'tinnge'n, yɩ́ bʋ y'ɔ le benin?”

ref: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say that Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
text: M'ɔ yɔlɩ sɔ'n, bɛ kɔ bisa Zozi kɛ: “Nzukɛ ati yɛ̂ mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ nwan Eli yɛ̂ ɔ 'di mʋa ba'n?”

ref: Jesus answered: Elijah certainly will come to get everything ready. But don't the Scriptures also say that the Son of Man must suffer terribly and be rejected?
text: Zozi kɔ bua bɛ́ kɛ: “Eli 'di mʋa ba ɔ sesie ninnge'n-mɔ biekun. Nan ɔ yɔ sɛ mɔ b'a hɛlɛ yɩ́ kɛ, Mân Baa'n 'ba nwun amannɩnhunlun kpa, ɛsɛ bɛ 'kpɔ yɩ́'n?

ref: When Jesus and his three disciples came back down, they saw a large crowd around the other disciples. The teachers of the Law of Moses were arguing with them.
text: Zozi nʋn Pɩɛlɩ nʋn Zʋan nʋn Zʋakɩ fi bʋka nɩn asʋ bɛ kɔ ju. Mɔ bɛ walɩ dede mɔ bɛ ka kaan bɛ ju Zozi menian ɛhɩnlɩn-mɔ anwʋn ɛbɛlɛ'n, bɛ kɔ nwun meninsʋnman bɛ́ nwʋn ɛbɛlɛ. Mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ wɔ bɛ́ nun. Bɛ kɔ fa akpʋlʋwa esue bɛ kɔ tʋa Zozi menian'n-mɔ.

ref: They brought the boy, and as soon as the demon saw Jesus, it made the boy shake all over. He fell down and began rolling on the ground and foaming at the mouth.
text: Bɛ kɔ fa batʋnman'n bɛ kɔ bɛlɛ Zozi. Kɛmɔ batʋnman'n nwunlin yɩ́ nɩn ala, wawɛ ɛtɛ'n m'ɔ wɔ yɩ́ nun'n kɔ wʋsʋ yɩ́ sɛlɛ kpa. Batʋnman'n kɔ tɔ asɩ ɔ kɔ kunndo, yɩ́ nʋan ngɛsɛlɛ fʋkʋʋ.

ref: The crowd was really surprised to see Jesus, and everyone hurried over to greet him.
text: Zozi kɔ li meninsʋnman'n nvu nun. Kɛ bɛ nwunlin yɩ́ nɩn ala, bɛ kɔ tutu ɛnwanndie bɛ kɔ hɔ yɩ́ atɩn ɛkpa.

ref: Jesus said, “You people don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy to me.”
text: Zozi kɔ han kɔ hele bɛ́ kɛ: “Ɛmɔ menian ɛhɩ-mɔ, ɛmɔ le man dedi. Mɩn nʋn ɛmɔ yɛ tánlan dede ɔ hɔ́ jú mɛlɛ benin? N zʋ́ ɛmɔ dede ɔ jú tɛmʋn benin? Bɛ fá batʋnman'n bɛ bɛ́lɛ mɩ́n.”

ref: Jesus asked the boy's father, “How long has he been like this?” The man answered, “Ever since he was a child.
text: Zozi kɔ bisa batʋnman nɩn asɩ'n kɛ: “Ɔ mantanlɩn yɩ́ ɛyɔlɛ'n yɩ́ cɩan nyɛ ahɩ?” Yɩ́ sɩ nwan: “Ɔ bɔlɩ yɩ́ bʋ yɩ́ mmatʋnman nun.

ref: At once the boy's father shouted, “I do have faith! Please help me to have even more.”
text: Ɛbɛlɛ ala, batʋnman nɩn asɩ'n kɔ tɩan nun, yɩ́ nwan: “Mɩn de mɩn di, kʋsʋ bʋka mɩ́n nán mɩ́n dedi'n tɩ kaan sʋa!”

ref: When Jesus saw that a crowd was gathering fast, he spoke sternly to the evil spirit that had kept the boy from speaking or hearing. He said, “I order you to come out of the boy! Don't ever bother him again.”
text: Meninsʋnman'n kɔ nwanndi kɔ hɔ Zozi nʋn batʋnman nɩn anwʋn ɛlɔ. Mɔ Zozi nwunlin yɩ́ sɔ'n, ɔ kɔ jʋjɔ aminle sʋ ɔ kɔ hele wawɛ ɛtɛ'n, yɩ́ nwan: “Wawɛ ɛtɛ, wɔ́ mɔ a munndun batʋnman ɛhɩ anʋan'n ɛsɛ a ti yɩ́ nzʋ'n, mɩɩn kan mɩn kele wɔ́ kɛ ɛ fi yɩ́ nun tú, ɛsɛ n'ɛ wʋlʋ yɩ́ nun kʋ́n lé!”

ref: The spirit screamed and made the boy shake all over. Then it went out of him. The boy looked dead, and almost everyone said he was.
text: Wawɛ ɛtɛ'n kɔ wʋsʋ batʋnman'n sɛlɛ kpa. Ɔ kɔ tɛtɩan ɔ fi yɩ́ nun kɔ tu. Batʋnman'n tɩ kɛ ɔ'a wu. Ɔ man bɛ́ dɔʋn mɔ anɩn bɛ wɔ ɛbɛlɛ'n nwan: “Batʋnman nɩn a wu! Batʋnman nɩn a wu!”

ref: But Jesus took hold of his hand and helped him stand up.
text: Mɔ bɛ 'kɩkan ɛhɩ'n, Zozi kɔ sɔ batʋnman nɩn asa ɔ kɔ jasʋ yɩ́ kɔ jinlan.
